title: cos: route-aware init repair inspect for Claude routes
description:
Background:
This is Unit 3 of the approved Claude orchestrator skill plan. After config schema and complete package templates exist, scaffold internals can implement the actual route-aware init, repair, and setup inspection behavior.

Goal:
Make ApplyInit, ApplyRepair, and Inspect understand agent-symlink, orchestrator-skill, preserve, and none routes while preserving existing default symlink behavior.

Exact payload file list:
- internal/scaffold/targets.go
- internal/scaffold/apply.go
- internal/scaffold/repair.go
- internal/scaffold/inspect.go
- internal/scaffold/targets_test.go
- internal/scaffold/apply_test.go
- internal/scaffold/repair_test.go
- internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go
- internal/scaffold/fixture_workflow_test.go
- docs/result-contract.md only if setup/init/repair JSON route fields need public contract text
- backpressure/attestations/<gate-hash>.json generated by Burpvalve

Binding route behavior:
- agent-symlink creates or repairs CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md and remains the default.
- orchestrator-skill creates a generated regular CLAUDE.md bootstrap plus the full .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/ package.
- none creates or repairs no Claude route files.
- repair preserve repairs whichever recognized route already exists; if none exists, it uses agent-symlink.
- ORCHESTRATOR.md remains an independent sidecar target controlled by orchestrator target/defaults only.

Binding AGENTS.md dependency:
- agent-symlink and orchestrator-skill require AGENTS.md to exist or be created in the same operation.
- --no-agents with an active Claude route fails before mutation when AGENTS.md is missing.
- --no-agents with an active Claude route may proceed if AGENTS.md already exists, while reporting that AGENTS.md was used but not repaired.
- claude_route=none does not require AGENTS.md.

Binding conflict/adoption behavior:
- Generated orchestrator CLAUDE.md is an intentional route artifact, not abandoned Claude notes.
- Unmarked regular CLAUDE.md is a conflict by default for both active routes.
- Add route internals for an explicit repair-only adoption mode: adoption imports unmarked regular CLAUDE.md into AGENTS.md using the existing marker style, then applies the selected route.
- Adoption is invalid for generated orchestrator bootstrap files.
- Generated skill drift is repaired or reported according to the existing scaffold generated-file policy chosen by implementation; behavior must be tested.

Public alias boundary:
- Expose public target aliases for claude-orchestrator, claude-skill, orchestrator-skill, and .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator only in this bead once behavior is implemented.
- Route conflicts in target normalization fail before mutation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default init still creates CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md only.
- Orchestrator route init creates AGENTS.md, generated regular CLAUDE.md, and the full skill package.
- Orchestrator route init does not create ORCHESTRATOR.md unless the sidecar target/default is active.
- Repair can preserve symlink route, convert symlink to orchestrator bootstrap when explicit, and convert generated bootstrap back to symlink when explicit.
- Setup/inspect reports expected route, detected route, missing route pieces, drift, and conflicts in JSON and human output.
- No generated route points to a missing AGENTS.md.

Test commands:
- go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Claude|Route|Orchestrator|Inspect|Apply|Repair|Adopt|Agents|Skill|Conflict|Fixture'
- go test ./...

Non-goals:
- No TUI route prompt, broad robot/setup JSON redesign, or full route UX in this bead; ruling 3262 permits only the minimal repair adoption selector, matching robot field, and route-flag plumbing required to keep this intermediate commit green.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.

Ruling 3262 scope merge:
- RusticDog approved merging the minimal Unit 4 adoption/route CLI surface into this bead because Unit 3 alone made `go test ./...` red and would leave repair stricter without a public adoption escape hatch.
- This bead therefore also owns the smallest required `cmd/burpvalve` surface: repair `--adopt-claude-md`, the matching repair robot field, and route-flag plumbing strictly required to pass explicit adoption/route values into scaffold internals.
- Keep all non-minimal Unit 4 work out of this commit: no TUI route prompt, no broader setup JSON/confirmation-flow redesign, no extra help polish beyond the new minimal flags, and no unrelated config/help changes.
- Record this ruling in verifier packets and closure evidence.

Expanded payload from ruling 3262:
- cmd/burpvalve/main.go
- cmd/burpvalve/init_test.go
- .beads/issues.jsonl tracker update for this ruling, if this bead update is staged with the implementation
close_reason: Implemented route-aware Claude scaffold and ruling 3262 minimal adoption/route CLI merge in commit 8ecdb73 (cos: add route-aware Claude scaffold), pushed to origin/main. Attestation: backpressure/attestations/d43872194e6ac084ed4cb4e52d540d72d500b0995c7d73fa661e396da1473a90.json. Verifier evidence: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3292 and ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3291; shared judgment trio agreed pass. Tests: focused internal/scaffold route/adoption suite, focused cmd init/repair/setup/config/help/robots suite, staged-slice stash --keep-index verification, go test ./..., and live Burpvalve hook passed.

title: PRS-09A Implement backpressure CLI core loaders, detection, hashes, and matrix
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-09 core responsibilities and Feature Detection / Matrix Rule. Implement backpressure-commit core library without final interactive prompt flow. Files/patterns: cmd/backpressure-commit/, internal/attestations/, internal/gitindex/, internal/lintconfig/. Behavior: load backpressure/manifest.yaml condition order and lint_commands, compute stable staged payload hash excluding backpressure/attestations/** and log/backpressure/failed/**, hash condition files and manifest, detect explicit --feature and staged-diff feature fallback, block ambiguous/multiple atomic units, and build |F| x |C| matrix cells.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: tests cover manifest loading, condition order, payload hash exclusions, condition file hashing, explicit feature flag, ambiguous feature blocking, multiple feature blocking, and matrix size; CLI has --mode=pre-commit and --mode=ci stubs that route through core; go test ./... passes; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.
close_reason: Implemented backpressure core manifest loading, condition ordering/hashes, staged payload hashing with generated artifact exclusions, feature detection/blocking, matrix construction, and pre-commit/ci CLI core routing. Verified by Nash: APPROVED no gaps. go test ./... and make build passed.

title: cos: end-to-end Claude route acceptance
description:
Background:
This is Unit 6, the final acceptance unit of the approved Claude orchestrator skill plan. Prior units added schema, templates, route-aware scaffold internals, public setup surfaces, and docs alignment. This bead proves the routes work end-to-end from user-facing commands.

Goal:
Add and run end-to-end route acceptance coverage for fresh repos and repair/drift scenarios, including generated Claude skill package validation.

Exact payload file list:
- cmd/burpvalve/init_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/setup_test.go if present, otherwise the current setup/init test file that owns setup JSON scenarios
- internal/scaffold/fixture_workflow_test.go
- docs/result-contract.md or user-facing docs only if final acceptance reveals missing public contract wording
- backpressure/attestations/<gate-hash>.json generated by Burpvalve

Required acceptance scenarios:
- Fresh default init creates AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md, with no .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator package.
- Fresh orchestrator route init creates AGENTS.md, generated regular CLAUDE.md, and the full .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator package.
- setup --json reports the correct route state for both fresh routes.
- repair after drift restores the symlink route.
- repair after drift restores the orchestrator-skill route.
- setup/repair reports conflicts for unmarked regular CLAUDE.md unless explicit adoption is selected.
- --no-agents plus active route fails when AGENTS.md is absent and succeeds with a reported non-repaired dependency when AGENTS.md already exists.
- ORCHESTRATOR.md sidecar remains independent: it appears only when explicitly targeted or configured.
- Generated Claude skill package has upload-safe frontmatter, all linked resources, no binaries, scripts with --help, dry-run default, and explicit mutation/execute flags.
- jsm validate passes for a generated temporary .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator package when jsm is available. If jsm is unavailable, tests still check frontmatter/no-binaries/resource-link rules directly and the run report states jsm was unavailable.

Acceptance criteria:
- The route matrix passes through public commands, not only internal helpers.
- Generated package validation is covered.
- Full go test ./... passes.
- Any final docs/examples are consistent with the implemented route behavior.

Test commands:
- go test ./internal/config ./internal/scaffold ./internal/charmui ./cmd/burpvalve
- go test ./...
- tmp=$(mktemp -d); go run ./cmd/burpvalve init --force --json --target "$tmp/agent" --git-init --claude-route agent-symlink
- tmp=$(mktemp -d); go run ./cmd/burpvalve init --force --json --target "$tmp/orchestrator" --git-init --claude-route orchestrator-skill
- go run ./cmd/burpvalve setup --json --target <tmp-agent>
- go run ./cmd/burpvalve setup --json --target <tmp-orchestrator>
- jsm validate <tmp-orchestrator>/.claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator || true  # record unavailable jsm honestly

Non-goals:
- No new route semantics beyond proving what prior units implemented.
- No public launch, release, or global Claude skill installation.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.
close_reason: Completed in commit b920bff (cos: add route acceptance coverage), pushed to origin/main. Attestation: backpressure/attestations/07aed8119d0938e60d88f268352a6757c96379a2899ff7e94cf581134c747bf3.json. Verifier refs: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3447 for judgment cells; ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3459 for mechanical/shared cells; WhiteGorge Agent Mail 3448 provided additional judgment corroboration. Gate accepted staged payload hash sha256:07aed8119d0938e60d88f268352a6757c96379a2899ff7e94cf581134c747bf3. Tests covered: focused Claude route e2e, generated orchestrator skill package acceptance including jsm validate, and go test ./internal/config ./internal/scaffold ./internal/charmui ./cmd/burpvalve.

title: osl: handle v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 old releases
description:
Background:
v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 release assets become public when the repo flips. Their packaged skills say proprietary, so the recommended plan deletes those GitHub releases/assets before flip unless owner vetoes.

Scope:
- If owner chose delete: delete v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 GitHub releases and downloadable assets before flip. Keep tags unless separately vetoed.
- If owner chose keep: record acceptance that proprietary-labeled artifacts remain public.
- Add one launch release-note line that earlier versions were private pre-releases when deletion path is used.
- Verify release list/asset state after action.

Acceptance criteria:
- Old release decision is implemented exactly.
- If delete: v0.1.0/v0.1.1 GitHub releases/assets are gone; tags may remain.
- If keep: risk acceptance is visible in release/audit notes.
- Launch release notes mention earlier private pre-releases as approved.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Deleted GitHub releases v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 with gh release delete <tag> --yes, preserving git tags by not using --cleanup-tag. Deleted assets for each release: burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz, burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256, burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz, burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256, burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz, burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256, burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz, burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256, checksums.txt. Verification: gh release list --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --limit 20 now shows v0.1.3 and v0.1.2 only; gh release view v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 both return release not found exit=1; gh release view v0.1.2 still succeeds with nine assets; all 18 captured old asset download URLs return HTTP 404; git tag -l 'v0.1.*' shows v0.1.0, v0.1.1, v0.1.2, v0.1.3; gh api repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/git/ref/tags/v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 both return refs/tags entries. Launch release note for later v0.2.0 notes: earlier versions were private pre-releases.

title: rel: coordinate v0.1.2 release plan and final acceptance
description:
Background:
This umbrella coordinates the v0.1.2 release plan from `plans/release-v0.1.2.md` revision 2 and decision D6. It starts only after the separate land-working-set plan has completed, all feature commits are landed, and `git status --short` is clean. Do not wire that cross-plan dependency here; RusticDog will wire plan-level dependencies after both bead sets exist.

Goal:
Ship v0.1.2 as the release that catches docs, changelog, badges, packages, release assets, and issue hygiene up to the landed post-v0.1.1 surface: verifier policy/provenance, verifier prompts, explain, attestations list/show/latest/browse, beads preflight, config/config init, completion verify, lint enforcement levels, and the recovery result contract.

Why this matters:
Without this release pass, users and agents can see v0.1.1 docs and assets while the repo contains a much larger working set. That mismatch creates false installs, false smoke tests, and issue closure ambiguity. This umbrella closes only after all child beads are complete and the release has been verified from a pinned v0.1.2 install path.

Acceptance criteria:
- All child `rel:` beads are closed or deliberately superseded with a comment explaining why.
- `br dep cycles` returns empty before and after `br sync --flush-only`.
- `gh release list --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve` shows v0.1.2 as latest.
- The v0.1.2 GitHub release exposes the full nine-asset set including `checksums.txt`.
- Pinned post-release install reports v0.1.2 and `setup --json` runs.
- `docs/CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/release-install.md`, README badges, release package, and issue comments match the release state.
- Working tree is clean after release except for explicitly planned follow-up work owned by other plans.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
close_reason: Completed v0.1.2 release plan. All child rel beads closed; v0.1.2 is latest at https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/releases/tag/v0.1.2 with exactly nine assets including checksums.txt; pinned post-release install smoke passed; docs/README/changelog/package/issue hygiene agree on v0.1.2; #13/#14/#17 remain open; br dep cycles clean. Current worktree contains explicitly planned active follow-up work from other chains, not release residue.

title: oxp: wire option-B install rule through init setup repair TUI and robots
description:
Background:
After the config schema and template target exist, the user-facing install surfaces must enforce the exact option-B rule. The main risk is accidental silent installation because Beads and NTM are currently standard scaffold targets. Revision 3 forbids silent installation.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Option B installation rule (exact): the orchestrator target is NOT added to `standardScaffoldTargets`. It is created only when one of: (a) explicit positional target (`burpvalve init orchestrator`); (b) config `defaults.init.orchestrator` set to a non-off value; (c) the init TUI checkbox, which is OFFERED (visible, unchecked by default) whenever the Beads or NTM checkbox is selected in the same run. Never installed silently."

Scope:
- Wire CLI init/repair/setup/inspect/apply paths to recognize the orchestrator target.
- Explicit target `burpvalve init orchestrator` creates `ORCHESTRATOR.md`.
- Config `defaults.init.orchestrator=orchestrator-md` selects the target.
- Config `defaults.init.orchestrator=off` leaves it absent.
- Config `defaults.init.orchestrator=claude-md` fails as unsupported until deferred option A ships.
- Init TUI offers a visible unchecked orchestrator checkbox only when Beads or NTM checkbox is selected in the same run; selecting it creates `ORCHESTRATOR.md`.
- Repair touches `ORCHESTRATOR.md` only when explicitly targeted or `defaults.repair.orchestrator=true`.
- Setup/readiness reports distinguish missing orchestrator file only when required by target/config.
- Help text and robot docs explain the exact install rule.

Acceptance criteria:
- Plain default init does not create `ORCHESTRATOR.md` even though Beads/NTM may be standard targets.
- TUI checkbox is visible but unchecked when Beads or NTM is selected; it is not silently selected.
- Robots and `--force --json` paths support explicit target and config behavior deterministically.
- Repair preserves local project-owned content unless target/config explicitly requires repair.
- Help/robot docs leave no ambiguity about standard vs explicit installation.

Tests:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Init|Repair|Setup|TUI|Robots|Help|Orchestrator|Config'`
- `go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Apply|Repair|Inspect|Orchestrator|Config|Target'`
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented in commit f3c4a46 with attestation backpressure/attestations/66154fa9880b4acce1a5af4fa4484e2e2235d8daa904f30efd61e6ef89966237.json; option-B ORCHESTRATOR.md install surfaces wired through init/setup/repair/TUI/robots, verifier-caught hidden TUI selection defect fixed, 12 pass / 1 not_applicable matrix recorded.

title: GH-4 fixed: generated guidance authorizes verifier subagents
description:
GitHub issue #4 was verified fixed in the current tree.

Issue: https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/issues/4

- internal/scaffold/templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl contains the same language.
- templates/docs/ntm-bridge.md and internal/scaffold/templates/docs/ntm-bridge.md contain the same language.

When Burpvalve requires verifier cells, the agent is expected to spawn read-only verifier subagents for the current staged feature/condition cells when its runtime permits repo-level authorization. Do not fabricate subagent confirmation.

This bead records closure of the already-fixed GitHub issue; it is not new implementation work.

title: osl: rewrite README and documentation with codebase-report, readme-writing, and de-slopify skills
description:
Background:
Before the visibility flip, the README and public-facing documentation need a source-grounded rewrite and style pass. This bead exists because the public launch now depends on a scrubbed current tree, MIT metadata, stable install guidance, and D-LEX vocabulary all agreeing in the first public impression.

Owner order:
Use three installed Codex skills in sequence:
1. codebase-report: generate an accurate, current picture of the codebase as source material, with file/line references where useful.
2. readme-writing: restructure README.md to that skill's standards while preserving the lexicon glossary, the README pitch, D-LEX vocabulary, license/badge/install accuracy, and maintainer contribution policy.
3. de-slopify: perform the final manual prose pass over README.md and all public-facing docs to remove filler, hedging, formulaic AI writing patterns, and em dash overuse without damaging technical accuracy.

Scope:
- Rewrite README.md for public launch using current codebase facts, not stale plan language.
- Sweep public-facing docs that a new user or anonymous verifier will read after the visibility flip.
- Preserve the lexicon glossary and pitch, including the D-LEX vocabulary distinctions: work unit, feature as stable CLI/schema term, bead as Beads tracker issue, valve, burp, seal, and attestation.
- Preserve MIT license metadata, status/license badges, release/install accuracy, SECURITY.md references, contribution policy, and supported platform claims.
- Preserve the public install contract from the install-docs bead: pinned release tag first, piped install second, no go install recommendation, linux/darwin amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep all work behind the D-OSL hard no-launch gate. This bead does not authorize visibility flip, repo rename, release publication, old-release deletion, or GitHub metadata changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- README.md is rewritten and internally consistent with the current codebase, release packaging, license metadata, install surface, and D-LEX vocabulary.
- Public-facing docs are swept with de-slopify and no known filler/hedging/AI-slop patterns remain in edited public prose.
- No development time or timeline language is introduced; docs describe scope, sequencing, and blockers instead.
- Lexicon glossary and README pitch remain present and accurate.
- License, badge, install, supported-platform, SECURITY.md, and contribution-policy surfaces remain accurate after the rewrite.
- `rg -n "go install" README.md docs skill` has no Burpvalve public install recommendation.
- `jsm validate skill/burpvalve` passes if the skill docs/frontmatter are touched.
- Gated commit(s) are used per unit; do not combine unrelated feature work with the docs rewrite.
close_reason: Completed in commit 81b8322 (docs: rewrite public README and docs), pushed to origin/main. Attestation backpressure/attestations/b027afbbd3866dc39a2b3b07f9f3aba0fce119dd0113b26888196445004c66b8.json. Verifier evidence: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3534 judgment/high pass; ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3533 mechanical/shared pass/not_applicable; refreshed after c09dfee with matching hash and go test ./... pass.

title: Final compile, run, and commit-gate verification
description: Terminal verification bead for the user objective, to run after all PRS implementation beads are closed and before the final GOAL.md acceptance audit. Compile the project-readiness-kit Go code, run the setup/readiness tooling against this repo itself, and verify that the pre-commit/backpressure gate behaves correctly during a real commit attempt. This is not a product feature bead; it is an end-to-end release gate for the setup skill and CLI. Files/patterns: go.mod, Makefile, cmd/setup-command-project, cmd/backpressure-commit, tools/backpressure-commit, .githooks/pre-commit, backpressure/, backpressure/attestations/, log/backpressure/failed/, <scrubbed-local-path>, .beads/issues.jsonl. The test must prove the compiled binaries are used, not mocked, and must leave the repo clean or with only intentional tracked verification artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: all PRS implementation beads are closed before this bead is claimed; br dep cycles --json reports no cycles; br doctor is healthy or any remaining warning is explicitly documented as non-blocking; go test ./... passes; release/local-cache builds compile setup-command-project and backpressure-commit with -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w" and each binary is <=12 MiB with warnings investigated above 8 MiB; the compiled setup-command-project binary is run against <scrubbed-local-repo-path> in check mode and reports ready/expected state or precise blockers; the compiled setup-command-project binary is run in init/repair mode against a disposable fixture clone or temp repo and proves idempotency; the installed/prepared .githooks/pre-commit path calls the compiled backpressure-commit binary; a real commit-gate smoke test is performed in this repo or a disposable clone by staging a harmless verification change, attempting git commit, and confirming the hook blocks when required attestation is missing and passes when the correct tracked attestation is staged; any temporary commit is reverted/reset in the disposable clone or intentionally retained only if it is the final release verification commit; jsm validate skills/setup-command-project passes from <scrubbed-local-path> or records a precise environmental blocker; final output includes exact commands run and results; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.

title: lint: defer concurrent attestation scan until evidence volume warrants it
description:
Background:
Revision 2 explicitly defers concurrent attestation directory scanning until real repos accumulate enough evidence records to need it. It is part of D9, but not part of the issue-17 first increment.

Deferred scope:
- Later, make `attestations list/latest` scan evidence directories concurrently once a repo demonstrates hundreds of records and sequential scan time is measurable.
- Preserve deterministic output ordering and existing result contract.
- Do not implement now.

Acceptance criteria for future work:
- A real performance problem is documented before implementation.
- Tests prove deterministic ordering and unchanged JSON schema.
- The implementation is bounded and does not add goroutines outside attestation scan code.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.

title: PRS-11 Enforce exact attestation prompt flow and subagent confirmations
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-11, Subagent Attestation, Matrix Rule, and Failure Message Requirement. Implement the fixed interactive question sequence in backpressure-commit pre-commit mode. Files/patterns: cmd/backpressure-commit/, internal/attestations/prompt*.go or equivalent. Sequence: entry banner, atomicity confirmation, per-condition subagent confirmation, verdict, message/evidence collection, summary, artifact write, staging check. The CLI must distinguish subagent_confirmed=false from not_applicable, refuse pass without confirmed dedicated subagent, require messages for non-pass verdicts and missing subagent checks, and fail closed on malformed/contradictory/stale attestations.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: golden-output or prompt-driver tests verify entry banner text, fixed question order, one prompt per feature/condition cell, pass requires dedicated subagent confirmation, not_applicable requires message, fail/unknown require blocker/evidence/next action, subagent_confirmed=false writes unknown blocked cell, summary is printed before artifact writing, and stale/malformed attestations fail; go test ./... passes; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.
close_reason: Implemented attestation prompt flow; verifier Confucius approved: no gaps

title: vorch: 01 config schema for defaults.verifier
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 1 starts with a strict config schema change because `internal/config` uses `json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields`. A `.burpvalve.json` containing `defaults.verifier` fails today, so verifier preferences must become first-class config before prompt or submit flows can consume them.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/config/config.go`: add `Defaults.Verifier`, a `VerifierDefaults` struct, merge/normalize/source tracking, and validation helpers.
- `internal/config/config_test.go`: load, merge, project-over-global override, source tracking, and validation matrix for verifier defaults.

Binding config shape:
```json
{
  "defaults": {
    "verifier": {
      "authorized": true,
      "authorized_at": "2026-07-02",
      "authorization_scope": "read-only verifier subagents for backpressure checks",
      "spawn_method": "native",
      "default_model": "sonnet",
      "condition_models": { "security-boundaries": "high-reasoning" },
      "read_only_tools": true,
      "max_parallel_verifiers": 4,
      "transcript_dir": "log/backpressure/verifiers",
      "transcripts": "summary"
    }
  }
}
```

Binding validation rules:
- `spawn_method` is one of `native`, `ntm`, `hermes`, or `manual`.
- `max_parallel_verifiers` is 1 through 32 and is advisory runtime guidance only; Burpvalve does not enforce or observe spawn counts.
- `transcript_dir` is cleaned, repo-relative, non-empty, no traversal, no absolute path, no NUL bytes.
- `transcripts` is one of `summary`, `full`, or `committed`; summary is the default submit behavior.
- Model names and `condition_models` values are free-form local aliases; Burpvalve never validates provider/model lists.
- `condition_models` keys are validated against manifest condition IDs at use time with warnings, not at config load.
- Project config overrides global config through existing precedence; `authorized: false` revokes a global authorization.
- `noteDefaultsSources` records all verifier keys, including individual condition model keys where practical.

Acceptance criteria:
- Config files with the exact `defaults.verifier` shape load, normalize, merge, and round-trip through `Write`.
- Malformed enum/range/path values fail with named errors mentioning the bad key.
- Global verifier defaults appear when only global config exists; project values override them; project `authorized: false` overrides global true.
- Unknown keys still fail because strict parsing remains intact.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/config -run 'Verifier|Defaults|Merge|Source|Validate|Unknown'`
- `go test ./internal/config`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.

Live dogfooding finding added 2026-07-02 - A17 schema compatibility:
A17 is binding if this bead touches shared schema/type surfaces while adding `defaults.verifier`: the bound response/attestation condition schema must reserve optional support for multi-verifier evidence. Per condition, the new bound schema needs:
- `supplemental_verifiers`: optional array. Each entry carries full verifier provenance (kind, agent/name where available, runtime, model, separate_context, transcript_ref when available), verdict, message, evidence, and next_action when the supplemental verdict is fail/unknown.
- `adjudication`: optional object for disagreement/ruling cases. It records who ruled, ruling summary, final ruling/verdict if applicable, and a mail/message reference such as Agent Mail message id or thread id.

Compatibility rule:
Do not add new `defaults.verifier` config keys for A17 unless implementation proves they are needed. These schema fields are optional in new bound response/attestation files and absent means no supplemental verifiers and no adjudication. Legacy unbound `--responses` files remain on the existing compatibility path described by vorch-06/vorch-10: do not require these fields for legacy files, and do not break old fixtures except for documented notices.

Additional acceptance criteria from A17:
- Schema/type tests cover a condition with no supplemental verifier fields, one with `supplemental_verifiers`, and one with `adjudication`.
- Round-trip/write behavior preserves optional A17 fields when present and omits or treats them as empty when absent.
- Any schema documentation or robot help touched by this bead names the legacy compatibility rule explicitly.
close_reason: Implemented defaults.verifier config schema in commit 91597b3

title: rel: final v0.1.2 release acceptance gate
description:
Background:
The release needs a final gate after asset verification, post-release smoke, issue hygiene, and docs checks. This bead is the final checklist before the release plan umbrella can close.

Scope:
- Re-run a compact release acceptance check from the plan.
- Confirm no tracked files under `dist/` and no accidental generated archives/binaries are staged.
- Confirm README, docs, changelog, package, GitHub release, and issue hygiene agree on v0.1.2.
- Confirm launch-plan boundaries remain intact: no license/visibility changes and no #13/#14/#17 closure.
- Run `br dep cycles`, then `br sync --flush-only`; do not git commit as part of this assignment unless a later human explicitly requests it.

Exact final verification commands:
```bash
gh release list --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --limit 5
gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets
rg -n 'v0\.1\.1|v0\.1\.2' README.md docs/release-install.md docs/CHANGELOG.md
rg -n 'license: proprietary|distribution: forbidden' skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md
git ls-files dist
git status --short
br dep cycles
br sync --flush-only
```

Acceptance criteria:
- v0.1.2 is latest in `gh release list`.
- Exactly the expected nine release asset names are present; extra archive or checksum assets are a blocker unless explicitly approved.
- Pinned post-release smoke evidence exists and passed.
- `docs/release-install.md` has no v0.1.1-pinned examples.
- README release badge says v0.1.2 and CI badge is present.
- `git ls-files dist` returns no tracked release archives.
- `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` still has proprietary/forbidden frontmatter.
- #13/#14/#17 remain open.
- `br dep cycles` returns empty and `.beads/issues.jsonl` has been flushed.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
close_reason: Completed final v0.1.2 release acceptance gate. Evidence: <scrubbed-temp-path> Verified v0.1.2 is latest; release has exactly nine expected assets; docs/install/changelog/README agree on v0.1.2; README CI badge present; skill metadata remains proprietary/forbidden; git ls-files dist is empty; #13/#14/#17 remain open; br dep cycles reports none; br sync --flush-only reported no dirty issues. Current git status contains unrelated active work from other chains but no release archive/dist residue.

title: oxp: add hook-context-aware next_steps for evidence failures
description:
Background:
When the pre-commit hook blocks, orchestrators and coders need next steps that recognize hook context: whether the hook ran from source, a repo-local fallback, or PATH, and what staged/evidence action is required. Finding A6 identified this as an evidence-surface improvement.

Approved evidence-surface rule:
This belongs to the orchestrator evidence-surface increment. Some evidence-surface items may already land as standalone fixes before this plan; absorb already-landed fixes by documenting them and do not duplicate.

Absorb rule:
Before implementing, inspect current blocked-report/next_steps behavior. If hook-context-aware next steps already landed, update docs/tests to cover the landed behavior and close this bead as absorbed rather than adding a parallel implementation.

Scope:
- Improve blocked `commit`/hook `next_steps` so they name whether the failure occurred in hook context and what action the user/agent should take next.
- Include guidance for staged attestation creation/rerun discipline when the hook blocks for missing verifier evidence.
- Preserve truthful evidence language: do not imply lint or verifier checks passed unless they ran.
- Keep messages deterministic enough for tests.
- Avoid adding a workflow engine; this is reporting/diagnostic behavior only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hook-triggered failures show hook-aware next steps.
- Direct `burpvalve commit` failures continue to show appropriate non-hook next steps.
- Missing evidence, stale attestation, lint not enforced, and command unavailable cases are distinguishable where current result contracts support it.
- JSON and human output remain consistent.
- Already-landed behavior is absorbed rather than duplicated if present.

Tests:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Hook|NextSteps|Commit|Blocked|Evidence|Lint|Unavailable'`
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'NextSteps|Blocked|Hook|Evidence'` if helper code lives there.
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented and pushed in commit e3464a4 (oxp: add hook-aware commit recovery steps); attestation backpressure/attestations/f538d04254cb7974eed5c3a5472ad922bcde37f1ab9f1a009d9a4dfe3009eedc.json validates the hook-context next_steps payload.

title: lint: Unit E bounded Go and Node Astro detection
description:
Background:
The first increment must be useful without pretending to support every language. Detection is bounded to Go and Node/Astro roots, with a precise multi-root heuristic.

Scope:
- Add non-mutating detection for Go roots: a directory containing `go.mod`.
- Propose Go commands when the root exists and `go` is on PATH: `go test ./...`, `go vet ./...`, and a gofmt check. These are toolchain-standard proposals, not detected scripts.
- Add non-mutating detection for Node/Astro roots: a directory containing `package.json`.
- For Node/Astro, propose only existing exact-name scripts: `lint`, `test`, `typecheck`, `check`, `build`, `format:check`.
- Detect package manager from lockfiles: `package-lock.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `yarn.lock`, `bun.lockb`.
- Exclude vendored/generated directories: `node_modules`, `vendor`, `dist`, `docs/demos/generated`.
- Multi-root heuristic: more than one distinct directory after exclusions containing `go.mod` or `package.json`, or one of each. Nothing else triggers the polyglot question in first increment.
- Existing CI workflow commands may be future work; do not parse CI in first increment.

Tests and commands:
- Add fixtures for single Go root, single Node root, Go+Node polyglot, multi-package Node, excluded generated/vendor dirs, missing Go on PATH, and package manager lockfile selection.
- Run `go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/backpressure -run 'Test.*LintDetect|Test.*GoRoot|Test.*NodeRoot|Test.*PackageManager|Test.*MultipleRoots' -count=1` or actual test names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- Detection produces facts without mutating files.
- Candidate commands come from bounded first-increment rules.
- The wizard asks about scoped subdirectory commands when detection appears polyglot or multi-root.
- Single-language roots do not trigger unnecessary subdirectory questions by default.
- Tests cover single-language repo and polyglot repo detection.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Completed in commit 0fd4454: bounded Go and Node/Astro lint detection with attestation 4d6481532944b05ffdd58a7f75ba303507ee11523d825d7e0a3072f4c10f47cc

title: oxp: add canonical orchestrator prompt bank content including verifier-bootstrap
description:
Background:
The prompt bank must contain the repeated orchestrator moves proven in this round, not speculative prose. Review 2 identified that a fresh verifier needed a bootstrap/contact prompt before any packets could flow; Revision 3 adds it explicitly.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Bank addition: `verifier-bootstrap` (read AGENTS.md + backpressure/README.md, register via macro_start_session, contact-request the orchestrator, poll inbox, packet-priority rule, verdict schema pointer) — review 2 confirmed this was the missing arrival prompt."

Required initial prompt names:
- `commit-choreography`: explains the commit flow as paste-safe numbered steps: finish work; verify; close bead; sync beads; stage payload and bead state; run Burpvalve; commit. Do not use shell-hazardous arrow chains in the prompt body or in this bead text.
- `verifier-brief`: rules of engagement for a verifier agent, including read-only evidence, verdicts pass/not_applicable/fail/unknown, and no fabricated confirmations.
- `verifier-packet-relay`: peer-pane relay instructions for forwarding cell packets and preserving evidence.
- `marching-orders`: coder start-of-session brief.
- `orchestrator-tick`: observe/assign/verify/unblock/log loop.
- `bead-conversion`: plan-to-beads standard.
- `plan-review-packet`: review-dispatch packet template.
- `cross-review-polish`: adversarial/cross-review improvement template.
- `verifier-standby-brief`: standby role setup for verifier pools.
- `packet-not-received-status`: status prompt when packets are delayed/missing.
- `bead-conversion-assignment`: assignment prompt for converting approved plans into beads.
- `verifier-bootstrap`: read `AGENTS.md` and `backpressure/README.md`, register via Agent Mail `macro_start_session`, contact/request the orchestrator, poll inbox, honor packet-priority over other work, and point to verdict schema.

Prompt safety:
- Prompts embed D4 paste-safety rules by construction.
- Avoid bare shell workflow arrows that could create zero-byte files through redirection.
- Rendering must be tested with hostile variable values: spaces, quotes, backticks, `$status`-style reserved words, and shell-looking values.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every required prompt exists with stable name, description, variable metadata, and rendered body.
- `verifier-bootstrap` would have been sufficient for a fresh WhiteGorge-like verifier to register, request contact, poll inbox, and prioritize verifier packets.
- Prompt content does not duplicate large skill workflows where a short reference is enough, but remains useful when only the binary is available.
- Hostile variable rendering tests prove no shell-hazardous interpolation or accidental command text breakage.

Tests:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'PromptBank|VerifierBootstrap|PasteSafety|Hostile|Variables'`
- `rg -n -- ' -> |=>|\$status|verifier-bootstrap|packet-priority' <prompt source files>` as a manual review aid, with final tests carrying the assertion.
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Completed in commit 126a0fc (oxp: add canonical prompt bank content), pushed to origin/main; gate accepted attestation sha256:ff344b4cbec33ea1d77fab3e768f4eae322123c1d8e5f042ad18882ef5f5815d.

description:

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- All current reviewed plans are tracked and indexed.
- Decision document is tracked and clearly marks D1-D12 as binding for this round.
- No Phase 2 code, `.beads`, binary, or release work is included.
- The attestation for this admin/docs commit is generated under the tracked hook.

Test commands:

title: osl: audit tracked files runtime state and binary artifacts
description:
Background:
D2 and the v0.1.2 plan should remove committed binaries and archives. Public launch must verify no regression before flip.

Scope:
- Run `git ls-files` sweeps for `bin/`, `dist/`, `*.tar.gz`, `*.sha256`, generated binaries, and runtime state.
- Review `git status --short --ignored` before flip.
- Confirm no `.ntm/`, `.doctor/`, local logs, runtime database files, or generated artifacts are tracked unintentionally.

Acceptance criteria:
- `git ls-files` shows no committed release archives or repo-local binaries except intentionally tracked source assets.
- `git status --short --ignored` reviewed and recorded.
- Runtime state is untracked/ignored and not staged.
- Any regression is fixed before launch release is cut.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
notes: Parked 2026-07-03 by WhiteGorge due owner priority redirect to v0.1.3 release. Audit evidence gathered but not yet mailed/closed: no tracked release archives/binaries/runtime DBs found; D-OSL-3 blockers found for legacy project-readiness-kit naming and local machine paths. Resume by sending the tracked-files audit report and mapping fixes into OSL beads.

Evidence reported in Agent Mail 3556. Residual legacy/local/test/template publish-blocking residue moved to project-readiness-kit-osl-preflip-gate-557 checklist for flip-layer enforcement.

title: bug: ci --commit blocks on multi-cluster commits even with explicit --feature
description:
Background:
The OXP final acceptance gate found a product defect in `burpvalve ci --commit`: committed multi-cluster payloads still fail feature detection even when the caller supplies an explicit `--feature` assertion. This is verifier-caught product defect #12 and blocks `project-readiness-kit-oxp-final-acceptance-z6e`.

References:
- RusticDog ruling message 2997: fix, do not waive the final-acceptance hold.
- WhiteGorge message 2997: co-signed the blocker; `f3c4a46` class fails while `1ead769` single-cluster control passes.
- BrightOwl message 2996: reproduced and co-signed the same blocker; current output says `staged changes map to multiple diff clusters` despite explicit `--feature`.

Observed behavior:
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit HEAD --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8` blocks with `staged changes map to multiple diff clusters (.beads, skill); split commit or pass --feature explicitly`, even though the committed attestation records `feature.id=project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8` and `bead_ids=[project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8]`.
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu` blocks on multiple clusters (`.beads`, `cmd`, `internal`) despite the explicit feature assertion.
- Control case: `go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit 1ead769 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-hash-staged-helper-a48` passes for a single-cluster commit.

Expected behavior:
When auditing committed attestations, an explicit `--feature` must scope the audit plan to that feature for committed payloads and then assert that the committed attestation feature id matches exactly. It must not run unscoped multi-cluster detection first and fail before the assertion. A mismatched `--feature` must still fail with the existing feature assertion mismatch.

Implementation notes:
- Likely scope is `internal/backpressure/ci.go` plus focused tests in `cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go` and/or `internal/backpressure/ci_test.go`.
- Keep `--feature` assertion-only semantics for commit audit: the explicit feature can build the expected binding for the committed payload, but validation must reject if the attestation feature id differs.
- Do not weaken normal staged CI/pre-commit atomicity behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add a regression test where a committed payload spans multiple diff clusters and `ci --commit <sha> --feature <matching-feature>` passes.
- Add or preserve a test where the same committed payload with a wrong `--feature` fails with `feature assertion mismatch`.
- Preserve the single-cluster committed audit behavior and robot-mode behavior.
- Verify with focused tests and `go test ./...`.
- Run full Burpvalve gate with real verifier evidence, commit, push, close this bead with the commit ref, and return to OXP final acceptance.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 92a8e17 (bug: fix ci commit feature assertion), pushed to origin/main. Attestation: backpressure/attestations/e2332d370b939efb9f151dba4ec8b9b83277ac904c587208482cf09e95878135.json. Evidence: focused tests for matching multi-cluster ci --commit --feature and wrong-feature rejection passed; go test ./... passed; live ci --commit checks for f3c4a46, HEAD/dc8c2ee, and 1ead769 passed; post-commit ci --commit 92a8e17 passed.

title: rel: verify issue hygiene and release references
description:
Background:
The landing plan closes issues #8, #9, #10, #11, #12, and #15 as their implementation commits land. The v0.1.2 release is a verification and hygiene pass, not first closure. #6 and #16 depend on their verification-cell outcomes. #13, #14, and #17 explicitly remain open after v0.1.2.

Scope:
- Verify closed issues #8/#9/#10/#11/#12/#15 reference their implementation commit(s) and add a v0.1.2 release reference where useful.
- Check #6 and #16 against the landed verification-cell results. Comment only if the release adds useful context; do not force closure if verification says they remain open.
- Comment on #13 and #14 pointing to `plans/verifier-orchestration.md` and `plans/bead-closure-mode.md` respectively; leave both open.
- Leave #17 open for `plans/issue-17-guided-lint-setup.md`; do not imply v0.1.2 completed guided lint setup.

Suggested issue commands:
```bash
gh issue view 6 --comments
gh issue view 8 --comments
gh issue view 9 --comments
gh issue view 10 --comments
gh issue view 11 --comments
gh issue view 12 --comments
gh issue view 13 --comments
gh issue view 14 --comments
gh issue view 15 --comments
gh issue view 16 --comments
gh issue view 17 --comments
```
Example release-reference comment shape:
```text
Released in v0.1.2 after verification. Implementation landed in <commit>; release assets verified in v0.1.2.
```
Boundary comment shape for #13/#14:
```text
v0.1.2 includes the landed foundation only. The full follow-on work remains tracked in <plan path>; keeping this issue open for that plan.
```

Acceptance criteria:
- #8/#9/#10/#11/#12/#15 each have clear implementation/release traceability or already had it before this bead.
- #13/#14/#17 remain open.
- #13 and #14 have boundary comments if not already present.
- #6/#16 disposition matches evidence, not release optimism.
- No issue is closed merely because v0.1.2 shipped.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
close_reason: Completed v0.1.2 issue hygiene. Closed fixed/investigated issues #6, #8, #9, #10, #11, #12, #15, #16 with release traceability comments. Added boundary comments to #13 and #14 and left them open. Confirmed #17 remains open for guided lint setup. Release URL: https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/releases/tag/v0.1.2.

title: osl: document pinned public install paths and reject go install
description:

Scope:

- Public install docs use pinned tag raw URL, not `main`.

This plan executes LAST. - Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
