title: land: 19 arrow-chain sanitization
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 19 performs the D4/#16 arrow-chain sanitization. Agent-facing prose and printed CLI output must not encode workflows as bare `->` chains because agents copy them as ambiguous shell syntax.

Exact payload file list:
- `README.md`
- `install.sh`
- `skill/burpvalve/INSTALL.md`
- `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md`
- `skill/burpvalve/references/deterministic-backpressure.md`
- `docs/matt-pocock-skill-patterns.md`
- `docs/setup-pre-commit-patterns.md`
- `plans/bead-closure-mode.md`
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: only the printed `create command shim %s -> %s` string or equivalent output hunk identified by the main.go staging map.

Implementation notes:
- Replace bare workflow arrows with numbered steps or explicit prose.
- Allowed cases: symlink notation such as `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md`, and `->` inside fenced code blocks that are not presented as copy-paste shell workflows.
- This unit completes #16 only after the separate verification cell passes.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- User/agent-facing prose and CLI output no longer contain bare workflow arrow chains.
- Allowed symlink/code-block uses are intentionally preserved.
- The `create command shim` output string no longer teaches an unsafe arrow workflow.
- Issue #16 is ready for closure only after the verification bead confirms behavior.

Test commands:
- `rg -n -- '-> ' README.md install.sh skill/burpvalve/INSTALL.md skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md skill/burpvalve/references/deterministic-backpressure.md docs/matt-pocock-skill-patterns.md docs/setup-pre-commit-patterns.md plans/bead-closure-mode.md cmd/burpvalve/main.go`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Shim|Install|Help'`
- `bash -n install.sh`
- `go test ./...`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-19-verify`, listed tests, `git stash pop`.
close_reason: Completed in commit 94c3465: sanitized bare arrow-chain workflows and committed attestation 79f6f66e8855890220f5766e2bbe53ce6ee57109e330d5008350bace9e3b1af6 with A14b security-boundaries ruling.

title: cos: generated agent guidance and route docs
description:
Background:
This is Unit 5 of the approved Claude orchestrator skill plan. The generated Claude skill package was completed in Unit 2. This bead aligns ordinary-agent and coordinator-adjacent docs with the new route so Codex agents, Claude orchestrators, and ORCHESTRATOR.md users see the same operating model.

Goal:
Update generated AGENTS.md, optional ORCHESTRATOR.md, prompt-bank references, and the existing Codex Burpvalve skill docs so they describe both Claude routes and current repair behavior accurately.

Exact payload file list:
- internal/scaffold/templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl
- internal/scaffold/templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl
- templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl if still present as a public/release template mirror
- internal/backpressure/prompt_bank.go if prompt names/text must change
- cmd/burpvalve/prompts_test.go
- skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md
- skill/burpvalve/SELF-TEST.md if affected by route docs
- docs/ntm-bridge.md only for a small clarification if needed
- backpressure/attestations/<gate-hash>.json generated by Burpvalve

Binding AGENTS.md additions:
- Add a concise Taking Orders And Handoffs section: accept owner/orchestrator assignments, claim beads, announce shared-file scope, use reservations, do not widen unclear requirements.
- Add a Burpvalve Gate Choreography section: one atomic payload per commit, stage only owned files, run verifier/gate, keep responses honest and hash-bound, stage generated attestations, no bypass flags.
- Add a Verifier Work section: verifiers are read-only, verdicts need evidence, authorization is never condition evidence, disagreements/unknowns block.
- Add route pointer text: Claude orchestrator route users should read .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/SKILL.md; ORCHESTRATOR.md is supplemental coordinator guidance if present.
- Do not turn AGENTS.md into a full orchestrator manual.

Binding docs/skill alignment:
- ORCHESTRATOR.md may point to the Claude orchestrator skill when both are present, but remains a sidecar and not the canonical coding-agent contract.
- skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md must no longer state or imply every regular CLAUDE.md is invalid or always imported/replaced by repair. It must describe the new explicit route model and adoption behavior accurately.
- Prompt-bank references used in generated docs must name existing prompts only.
- NTM bridge boundary remains intact: Burpvalve owns contracts/prompts/evidence/gates; Agent Mail and NTM remain coordination substrates.

Acceptance criteria:
- Scaffolded AGENTS.md is useful to ordinary agents in a coordinated Burpvalve repo.
- Generated docs teach coordination without duplicating the entire Claude skill package.
- Codex skill docs describe agent-symlink, orchestrator-skill, no route, and explicit adoption accurately.
- Prompt tests cover any changed prompt-bank text.

Test commands:
- go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Agents|Orchestrator|Template|Claude|Route|Coordination|Verifier|Gate'
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Prompts|Help|Config|Claude|Route'
- jsm validate skill/burpvalve || true  # record if jsm is unavailable rather than fabricating success
- go test ./...

Non-goals:
- Do not alter the generated Claude orchestrator skill package templates in this bead unless a doc reference would otherwise be broken; package content belongs to Unit 2.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.
close_reason: Completed in commit 6a54256 (cos: add agent guidance docs), pushed to origin/main. Attestation: backpressure/attestations/0c309d38de18ece0bed98d828de5eb60c57ab26f0c7df69ef1622b05b90c0129.json. Verification: temp-worktree staged-slice git diff --check, focused scaffold/cmd tests, jsm validate skill/burpvalve, and go test ./... passed; BrightOwl Agent Mail 3385 and ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3381 provided hash-bound verifier evidence with agreement on shared cells.

title: bcm: 04 delivery close resume fixture matrix
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 3 is not complete without proving resumability at every partial or expected middle state. This bead makes the review-required fixture matrix first-class so the implementation cannot claim safe resume with only a happy path.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/beads_test.go` or new `cmd/burpvalve/beads_close_test.go`: unit/CLI fixtures for partial failures, expected middle states, and resume.
- `cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go`: end-to-end fixture if a real git/br/gate integration test is more appropriate for any case.
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: only small testability hooks if needed, not new product behavior beyond bead 03.

Required fixture matrix:
1. Close succeeded, sync failed.
2. Sync succeeded, gate blocked.
3. Attestation written but unstaged after a successful gate run. This is the normal Burpvalve attestation-staging bounce observed in land-01; it is an expected nonfatal middle state, not a failure.
4. Attestation staged but payload changed afterward.
5. `git commit` failed after gate pass.
6. Bead already closed on entry.
7. `br` missing.
8. Unrelated dirty files present.

Expected behavior for every fixture:
- The command stops at the classified state, not a generic fatal panic.
- Result JSON has `partial_success: true` whenever a mutating step already succeeded.
- The journal is updated with the failed/skipped/done/waiting step statuses.
- `next_steps` is structured as objects, not bare strings. Each step has `id`, `message`, exact `command`, and `fatal`.
- Output includes the exact resume invocation: `burpvalve beads close <id> --resume` plus required flags if needed.
- A resume run recomputes reality first and either continues safely or explains the remaining blocker.
- For attestation-written-but-unstaged, `fatal` is false and recovery stages exactly the named attestation path, then reruns the gate. The fixture must prove this state is not reported as a failed verification.
- For the staged-attestation-then-payload-changed fixture, the stale attestation is rejected and recovery points to regenerating evidence for the current staged payload.
- For unrelated dirty files, the command must not stage them and must name them as unrelated/dangerous context.

Acceptance criteria:
- All eight fixtures are implemented and fail before the state-machine behavior exists.
- Fixture assertions check journal content, result JSON, command stdout/stderr capture references, fatal/nonfatal classification, and resume command text.
- Existing Beads preflight tests still pass.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'BeadsClose.*(CloseSucceededSyncFailed|SyncSucceededGateBlocked|AttestationWrittenUnstaged|AttestationStagedPayloadChanged|GitCommitFailed|AlreadyClosed|MissingBR|UnrelatedDirty)'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'BeadsClose|Resume|PartialSuccess|Journal|Fatal|Nonfatal|AttestationWritten'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed Beads preflight and verifier-orchestration foundations it uses. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m bcm-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses when the payload is delivery work, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. Never fabricate verification evidence, and never gather evidence before `.beads/issues.jsonl` is staged for a closure flow.
close_reason: Complete bcm-04 delivery close resume fixture matrix

title: bcm: 01 policy doc and AGENTS closure clause
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 1 makes the delivery policy durable before mutating closure code exists. Issue #14 is about drift between tracker state and git state; the first step is to document a numbered, evidence-safe closure workflow that agents and generated repo contracts can follow. D4 is binding: do not express workflows as bare arrow chains in docs, generated text, or CLI output.

Exact payload file list:
- `docs/beads-delivery-workflow.md`: new policy document for delivery/admin closure, batching exception, drift check usage, and the numbered safe order.
- `README.md`: link the existing `beads preflight` section to the policy document.
- `internal/scaffold/agents_template.go`: add a short generated AGENTS clause for closing delivery beads through the gate.
- `internal/scaffold/template_contract_test.go`: assertions for the generated AGENTS clause.
- `templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl` and `internal/scaffold/templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl` if those template copies remain active in the target revision.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go` only if robot/help text gains policy links or D4 assertions in this increment.

Binding safe order to document as numbered steps, never as an arrow chain:
1. Agent stages the delivery payload: implementation, docs, tests, or config. `beads close` does not scan for dirty files and does not stage payload files.
2. `beads close` runs preflight checks by reusing landed report code.
3. `br close <id> --reason "<reason>"` runs. The reason references the bead and feature, not a commit SHA.
4. `br sync --flush-only` runs.
5. `beads close` stages exactly `.beads/issues.jsonl`; the staged payload is now final.
6. Verifier evidence is collected against the final staged payload, either through `verifier begin` plus `verifier submit` or through a caller-supplied `--responses` file bound to the current staged hash.
7. `burpvalve commit --bead <id>` runs the gate and may write the attestation as an expected intermediate state.
8. The attestation is staged and the gate revalidates.
9. `git commit` runs only with explicit confirmation. Interactive default is No. Robots require `confirm: true` plus a message. Without confirmation, the command stops after step 8 and prints the exact commit command in structured `next_steps`.

Resolved open questions to embed:
- The journal directory `log/backpressure/closures/` is created lazily on first `beads close`, not scaffolded by init for every repo.
- The close-before-commit order is kept: beads close and sync before the gate so `.beads/issues.jsonl` is inside the attested payload.

Acceptance criteria:
- Policy doc explains delivery versus admin, batching, the nine-step safe order, confirmation behavior, drift check usage, and lazy journal creation.
- README links to the policy from `beads preflight` docs.
- Generated AGENTS text tells agents to close delivery beads through the gate without claiming one-commit-per-bead for admin housekeeping.
- No changed prose or generated text introduces bare workflow arrow chains.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'AGENTS|Template|Contract|Beads|Closure'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Help|Beads|Robots'`

title: rel: run post-release pinned private install smoke
description:
Background:
After the release exists, run the documented private install path pinned to v0.1.2. This is intentionally separate from pre-release smoke: pre-release proves the local archive, post-release proves the GitHub release package and checksum path.

Scope:
- Fetch `install.sh` through the authenticated private-repo `gh api` flow because the repo remains private under D5.
- Install with `--version v0.1.2`, not `latest`, into temp skills/bin directories.
- Verify installed binary reports v0.1.2.
- Run `setup --json` from the installed binary.
- Optionally repeat with `--version latest` only after the pinned v0.1.2 smoke passes, to check latest propagation.

Exact pinned smoke commands:
```bash
tmp="$(mktemp -d)"
tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"
tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"
gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"
chmod +x "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"
bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes
"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version
"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json
```
Expected version output contains `v0.1.2`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pinned install downloads the v0.1.2 release package from GitHub and verifies checksums.
- Installed binary reports v0.1.2.
- `setup --json` exits successfully.
- Any optional `latest` smoke is recorded separately and does not replace the pinned smoke.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
close_reason: Completed post-release pinned private install smoke for v0.1.2. Fetched install.sh via authenticated gh api ref=main; installed with --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 into temp skills/bin dirs; shim resolved inside temp skills; installed binary reported v0.1.2; setup --json exited successfully with summary.ready true. title: vorch: 05 verifier submit ingestion and transcripts
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 3 continues with `verifier submit`, the deterministic ingestion path for real per-cell verifier results. It updates exactly one condition in the begin-created response file without letting stale packets, authorization-only text, or concurrent writes corrupt evidence.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts.go` or `internal/backpressure/verifier_responses.go`: submit validation, merge, atomic write, transcript storage, lock helpers.
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts_test.go` and/or `internal/backpressure/verifier_responses_test.go`: submit merge/rejection/locking/transcript tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: `verifier submit` command, flags, stdin JSON handling, result-contract output.
- `cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go`: submit CLI tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: submit robot help assertions if separate.

Binding behavior:
- Command shape: `go run ./cmd/burpvalve verifier submit --condition dry --json < verdict.json`.
- Verdict JSON must echo packet/file bindings. Mismatch against the response file or current staged payload rejects with a result-contract error naming the stale binding.
- Requires verifier provenance: kind, model, runtime, and separate_context according to the landed verifier model.
- Requires non-empty `evidence` for every verdict, including `pass`.
- Requires non-empty `message` for `not_applicable`, `fail`, and `unknown`.
- Rejects unknown condition IDs.
- Rejects evidence that consists only of recorded authorization language or D7 boilerplate.
- Merge semantics replace exactly the named condition entry in place, preserve condition order, preserve begin atomicity, and allow resubmitting an already-passing cell only with a printed warning.
- Writes through temp-file plus rename under an exclusive file lock so parallel submits cannot corrupt the file.
- Transcript handling: accepts `--transcript <path|->`. By default stores a compact summary under configured `transcript_dir`; `transcripts: full` stores raw transcript; `transcripts: committed` means the project intends to track the transcript dir. File names are `<staged-hash-short>-<condition-id>.md`; `transcript_ref` is repo-relative. Missing transcript at commit time is a warning, not a block.

Acceptance criteria:
- Submit updates one condition and leaves all other conditions and atomicity untouched.
- Submit rejection matrix covers binding mismatch, missing evidence on pass, missing evidence on non-pass, missing message on non-pass, unknown condition ID, invalid/missing provenance, authorization-only evidence, stale current staged payload, and duplicate/concurrent write safety.
- Lock test proves concurrent submits cannot leave partial JSON or lose a condition update.
- Transcript summary-by-default is implemented and configurable by `transcripts`.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'VerifierSubmit|Submit|Binding|Evidence|Authorization|Transcript|Lock|Concurrent'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'VerifierSubmit|Submit|Rejection|Transcript|Robots|Help'`
- `go test -race ./internal/backpressure -run 'Lock|Concurrent|Submit'` if race detector is practical in the environment.
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.

Live dogfooding finding added 2026-07-02 - A17 submit merge/adjudication support:
A17 is binding for `verifier submit`: submit must accept and merge multi-verifier evidence into the bound response file instead of forcing the orchestrator to keep corroborating verifier evidence and rulings only in Agent Mail.

Binding behavior additions:
- Submit accepts a condition result containing optional `supplemental_verifiers` entries and/or optional `adjudication` for the same condition.
- Submit can add supplemental verifier entries to an already-populated condition without erasing the primary condition verdict or other condition cells. Merge semantics must be deterministic, preserve condition order, and run under the same lock/temp-file safety as primary submit.
- Supplemental verifier entries require the same evidence and provenance bar as primary verifier results: full verifier provenance, verdict, non-empty evidence for every verdict including pass, non-empty message for non-pass, and next_action for fail/unknown.
- Adjudication is optional but, when present, must record ruling authority, ruling summary, final ruling/verdict if applicable, and audit reference (for example Agent Mail message id/thread id). Submit must preserve this field into the response file so commit-time attestation can carry it forward.
- Duplicate supplemental submissions from the same verifier/condition/binding should warn and either replace deterministically or be rejected with an actionable result-contract error; the implementation must document the chosen behavior in help/tests.
- Backward compatibility follows vorch-06/vorch-10: legacy unbound `--responses` files are not retroactively required to include supplemental verifier or adjudication fields. Strict requirements apply to new bound submit flows while legacy validation remains unchanged except for the existing notice path.

Additional acceptance criteria from A17:
- Submit tests cover adding a supplemental verifier to a condition that already has a primary verdict, preserving all other conditions and existing evidence.
- Submit tests cover adjudication merge/persistence and commit-readable output shape.
- Rejection tests cover supplemental entries missing provenance, evidence, required message, or stale binding.
- Concurrent submit tests include at least one primary result and one supplemental/adjudication update to prove locking does not lose either update.
close_reason: Implemented in 66e127d; audit passed for VORCH-05 verifier submit ingestion.

title: land: 08 formatter-safe attestation JSON and stale guard
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 8 makes attestation JSON formatter-safe and adds a stale-guard around staged attestation paths. This closes issue #10 and partially addresses issue #16 by preventing formatter churn from invalidating evidence unexpectedly.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts.go`: only `json.MarshalIndent`/trailing-newline and `stagedAttestationPaths` hunks.
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts_test.go`: formatter-safe JSON and stale-attestation-path tests.
- `docs/attestation-schema.md`: formatter-safety sections only.

Implementation notes:
- Do not include verifier-policy model work from unit 7 or bead metadata work from unit 16.
- The stale guard should make changed payload/evidence relationships explicit rather than silently accepting invalidated attestations.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- Generated attestation JSON is indented and ends with a newline.
- Formatter-safe output remains stable under normal JSON formatting.
- Staged attestation paths are detected and stale payload/evidence situations are surfaced.
- Documentation explains the formatter-safety guarantee and its limits.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Artifact|Attestation|Marshal|Stale|Format'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Attestation|Stale|Commit'`
- `go test ./...`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-08-verify`, listed tests, `git stash pop`.
close_reason: Completed in commit 2cdc1ce (land: guard stale attestation evidence).

description:

Push main only after accepted gated commits; push tag v0.1.3 only after the release-prep commit is on origin/main.\n- Do not commit dist archives or local binaries; GitHub release assets are canonical.\n- Keep license/visibility/launch metadata unchanged; open-source visibility flip remains out of scope.\n- Coordinate GATE-WINDOW before staging any commit; begin by proving git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Use version v0.1.3 everywhere in this chain.\n

title: E2E bead delivery and attestation scenarios
description:
Background:
Users and agents need to find, inspect, and trust attestation artifacts after commits. Bead metadata is valuable only if it is bound to the exact staged payload and can be found again without reading prose.

Goal:
Add representative E2E scenarios for bead delivery metadata, attestation artifacts, attestation query APIs, and stale-evidence detection.

Scope:
- A single-bead delivery workflow in a temp repo: staged change, responses template, passing attestation, attestation query, final gate against the exact staged payload.
- A coupled-bead workflow that records multiple bead IDs and a required rationale, then proves both IDs are queryable.
- Drift detection after verification when the implementation payload changes; include bead-state drift when it is deterministic enough at E2E level, otherwise keep that in focused fake-br tests and name the coverage.
- Attestation list/show/latest over passing attestations and blocked reports.
- Generated JSON formatting and trailing-newline regression coverage for passing and blocked evidence artifacts.
- A non-Beads control path proving ordinary commit and attestation query behavior still works without br or .beads.

Out of scope:
- Full attestation browser TUI behavior; that belongs to the TUI bead.
- Automatic br mutation, bead closing, admin-only inference, or any write to real .beads state.
- Repeating every fake-br edge case at E2E level when a focused unit test gives stricter coverage.
design:
Plan audit revision 5:
- This bead should prove user-level delivery workflows, not become a slow duplicate of lower-level Beads tests.
- Use hermetic temp repos, isolated HOME/XDG/PATH, and a temporary Burpvalve binary. Never depend on the developer machine global burpvalve, br, or shell config.
- For the single-bead path, assert the generated artifact includes bead_ids, feature metadata, verifier provenance, payload hash binding, and stable pretty JSON with exactly one trailing newline.
- For the coupled-bead path, assert multiple bead_ids require and preserve coupled-work rationale so unrelated work is not normalized.
- Query assertions should cover list, show, and latest JSON contracts; human output assertions should be short and stable.
- At least one stale-evidence scenario must alter the staged implementation payload after verification and prove Burpvalve blocks or tells the user to rerun verification instead of accepting stale evidence.
- Bead-state drift and preflight edge cases should use fake br or isolated fixtures where that is more deterministic than a full E2E flow; the test names should make that division explicit.
- Non-Beads behavior is required: ordinary burpvalve commit without --bead and attestation queries must still work in a repo with no .beads directory.
- Every scenario should clean or isolate generated logs and assert final git status or an exact expected dirty state before cleanup.
- If an attestation artifact itself is staged, the test must assert either rerun-required behavior or a clear reason why the artifact is excluded from payload drift.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- E2E tests assert exact exit codes and stable JSON fields for single-bead delivery, coupled-bead delivery, stale payload drift, attestation queries, blocked reports, and the non-Beads control path.
- Generated artifact JSON includes bead_ids, coupled-work rationale when multiple beads are supplied, feature metadata, verifier provenance, and payload binding where applicable.
- Attestation list/show/latest expose bead_ids and blocked status through JSON without requiring prose parsing.
- Drift scenarios warn or block with rerun-verification guidance instead of silently accepting stale evidence.
- Formatter checks prove valid pretty JSON with exactly one trailing newline for passing attestations and blocked reports.
- Focused Beads preflight tests, either existing or added, prove dry-run/no-mutation behavior for delivery-ready, coupled, admin-only, missing br, invalid bead ID, and stale bead-state cases; these do not all need full E2E duplication.
- Attestation queries remain usable in projects that do not use Beads.
- Ordinary burpvalve commit without --bead is covered as a non-Beads control path.
- Final git status is asserted after every E2E scenario, or the expected dirty files are explicitly asserted and cleaned.
- A verifier subagent reviews this bead after implementation before it is closed.
close_reason: Implemented E2E bead delivery and attestation workflow coverage. Evidence: TestE2EBeadDeliveryAttestationQueryAndDrift covers single-bead delivery, coupled bead metadata/rationale, attestation list/show/latest JSON and human output, stale payload drift blocking with rerun guidance, blocked report queryability, formatter-safe passing/blocked JSON, and non-Beads commit/query control path. Added stale staged-attestation detection, explicit bead metadata handling, legacy source_bead query fallback guard, and Beads column in human attestation list. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; br dep cycles --json passed; read-only verifier Confucius reported PASS.

title: Add attestation list/show/latest JSON APIs
description:
Background:
Approved idea #13 should be TUI-first for humans, but deterministic CLI/JSON APIs are still required for agents, tests, and the TUI substrate. This API should not be blocked on bead-delivery metadata; it can expose optional bead fields when present, and the delivery bead can later populate them.

Goal:
Add attestation query APIs for listing and showing passing attestations and blocked reports without mutating files.

Scope:
- Commands: burpvalve attestations list, burpvalve attestations show <hash-or-path>, burpvalve attestations latest, with --json supported for all.
- Human list output should be a compact table suitable for terminal scanning; JSON output is the stable robot contract.
- Read from backpressure/attestations and log/backpressure/failed without mutating files.
- Normalize records into a summary with schema_version, artifact_type, status, feature ids, optional bead ids, payload hash, condition verdicts, verifier metadata, generated_by, created time, path, stale/missing condition hash warnings when detectable, and parse warnings.
- Support filters useful to both humans and the future TUI: --status pass|blocked|all, --limit, --feature, and --bead when bead ids are present.
- Support stable sorting by created time then path, with a deterministic fallback when timestamps are absent.
- Treat malformed artifacts as reportable records in list where possible, and as clear JSON errors for show.
- Preserve enough raw-path identity that a human can jump from a list row to show/details without guessing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests create fixture attestation and blocked report files and assert list/show/latest human and JSON behavior.
- show accepts both full path and unambiguous hash-like id/prefix.
- Missing artifacts, ambiguous hash prefixes, and malformed artifacts produce clear JSON errors and non-zero exit for show.
- list remains usable when one artifact is malformed and reports parse warnings.
- The API exposes verifier provenance fields and an optional bead_ids array without requiring bead-delivery work to have shipped first.
- Robot help documents the stable query schema and tells agents to use it instead of scraping human output.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented read-only attestation query APIs: internal/attestations query scanner for backpressure/attestations and log/backpressure/failed; burpvalve attestations list/show/latest with compact human output and stable JSON; path/hash/prefix lookup; missing/ambiguous/malformed JSON errors; malformed artifacts remain visible in list with parse_warnings; status/feature/bead/limit filters; created-time/path sorting; verifier provenance and optional bead_ids in records; robot help tells agents to use --json instead of scraping human tables. Verification: go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'TestAttestations' -count=1 -v passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier subagent Mencius rechecked and reported PASS.

title: Build hermetic E2E harness for real Burpvalve hook flows
description:
Background:
Approved idea #4 needs an execution plan. Burpvalve has many unit tests, but several observed bugs only appear in real hook/commit flows. This bead creates the reusable harness foundation before feature-specific scenarios are added.

Goal:
Build a hermetic end-to-end regression harness that uses the real Burpvalve binary built from the current source against temporary Git repositories and real Git hook execution.

Scope:
- Provide Go test helpers or scripts that build a temporary test binary from the current source into t.TempDir; do not rely on a checked-in repo-local bin/burpvalve.
- Create temp repos, isolate HOME/XDG_CONFIG_HOME/XDG_DATA_HOME/PATH, configure git user, run burpvalve init/repair/setup/commit/lint, and inspect filesystem/git status.
- Exercise real git commit/pre-commit hook paths where the scenario is about commit behavior; direct command execution is acceptable only for non-hook setup probes.
- No network, no real user config, no mutation of the source repo except temp directories created by the test framework.
- Capture stdout/stderr, exit codes, generated artifacts, git status, hook output, relevant environment values, command transcripts, and the exact binary path used.
- Provide helper assertions for exact JSON fields, stable human-output substrings, generated file existence, unexpected root files, final git cleanliness, and artifact hash binding.
- Make failures readable in CI by printing a compact transcript and preserving temp paths only when the test framework requests it.
- Keep runtime acceptable for normal go test ./...; if a scenario is too slow or shell-specific, gate it behind a clear make test-e2e target and document the split.
- Make the harness easy for later beads to reuse without copying setup code.

Acceptance criteria:
- A basic E2E test proves init -> setup ready -> missing attestation blocks -> valid attestation stages/passes in a temp repo.
- At least one test commits through the real pre-commit hook rather than only invoking burpvalve commit directly.
- Harness fails if unexpected untracked root files appear after a successful hook commit.
- Harness can run with fake HOME/XDG/PATH without reading or writing real user shell/config files.
- Logs are detailed enough to debug failures in CI without rerunning locally.
- go test ./... passes, or any slow suite is documented and wired as make test-e2e.
close_reason: Implemented hermetic E2E harness foundation in cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go. The harness builds a real temporary Burpvalve binary from current source into t.TempDir, runs against temporary Git repos with isolated HOME/XDG/PATH, captures command transcripts with env/exit/stdout/stderr, provides reusable assertions for JSON fields, output substrings, generated artifact existence, artifact hash binding, final git cleanliness, and unexpected untracked files. Baseline scenario covers init -> setup ready -> missing attestation blocks through a real pre-commit hook -> valid responses write/stage passing attestation -> final git commit passes through the real hook. Verification: go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run TestE2EHarnessInitSetupAndRealPreCommitHook -count=1 -v passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier subagent Pasteur reported PASS.

title: osl: final private pre-flip launch gate
description:
Background:
This is the final pre-flip gate. It ensures owner decisions, license/package work, audit, old release handling, installer docs, security surface, and private release all completed before visibility changes.

Scope:
- Confirm all four owner decisions are closed/recorded.
- Confirm full audit checklist pass/fail is recorded and owner signed off.
- Confirm old release handling matches owner decision.
- Confirm public install docs and installer changes are complete.
- Confirm `go install` appears nowhere in public docs.
- Confirm license files, package-skill changes, dependency-license review, metadata, README, SECURITY.md, release package, and repo About metadata are ready.
- Confirm no Windows support claims and no unsupported community contribution claims.
- Run `br dep cycles` before close.

Acceptance criteria:
- All dependencies complete.
- `br dep cycles` returns empty.
- Visibility flip is explicitly authorized by owner after this gate.
- No known blocker remains.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.


Security reporting deferral acceptance (D-OSL security channel ruling, 2026-07-03):
- Before the visibility flip, verify that GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting for `clicksopendoors/burpvalve` is active and reachable.
- Treat the current private-repo HTTP 404 from the private-vulnerability-reporting API as a pre-launch deferral only, not as launch acceptance.
- Do not close this preflip gate until the working private reporting channel has direct evidence recorded, or the owner records a concrete replacement channel.
- Do not publish personal security email fallback text unless the owner explicitly supplies and approves that channel.
D-OSL-7/D-OSL-8 squash-relaunch acceptance:
- The public repository is initialized from a scrubbed current tree as a fresh initial commit, not by pushing this private repository's existing history.
- Before creating the public `clicksopendoors/burpvalve` repository, rename the current private repository at flip-prep time (for example, to `private-source-repo`) to free the canonical public name.
- The private-repo rename, public repo creation, visibility flip, and launch remain behind the hard no-launch gate and require explicit owner authorization.
- Verify the current-tree scrub bead is complete before this gate closes; the full-history audit BLOCK is resolved by D-OSL-7 strategy, but current-tree scrub remains mandatory.

Preflip residue checklist from tracked-files audit ruling (WhiteGorge 3556, 2026-07-03):
- Before public initial commit creation, resolve legacy `project-readiness-kit` naming in tracked public tree paths or explicitly regenerate/exclude the private evidence surface: `.beads/config.yaml`, `.beads/issues.jsonl`, `backpressure/attestations/*.json`, `README.md`, `docs/flip-day-runbook.md`, `docs/oxp-round-retrospective-2026-07.md`, `docs/burpvalve-issue-drafts-2026-07.md`, `docs/upstream-br-feedback-2026-07.md`, `docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md`, `docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md`, `plans/README.md`, `plans/land-working-set.md`, `cmd/burpvalve/prompts_test.go`, `internal/backpressure/ownership_accounting_test.go`, `internal/backpressure/prompt_bank_test.go`, and `internal/backpressure/prompt_export_test.go`.
- Perform the disruptive Beads ID prefix rename as the LAST pre-flip scrub step after all other private-tree launch work is complete; verify `.beads/issues.jsonl`, `.beads/config.yaml`, docs, tests, and attestations are consistent with the owner-approved replacement prefix.
- Before public initial commit creation, resolve local-path residue in tracked public tree paths or explicitly regenerate/exclude private evidence: `backpressure/attestations/*.json`, `internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go`, `internal/ntm/ntm_test.go`, `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`, `cmd/burpvalve/config_test.go`, `internal/config/config_test.go`, `internal/backpressure/prompt_bank_test.go`, `internal/backpressure/ownership_test.go`, `skill/burpvalve/SELF-TEST.md`, `scripts/package-skill_test.sh`, `plans/issue-17-guided-lint-setup.md`, and `docs/flip-day-runbook.md`.
- Before public initial commit creation, resolve fixture email residue in tracked public tree paths or explicitly regenerate/exclude private evidence: `internal/backpressure/core_test.go`, `cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go`, `cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go`, `internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts_test.go`, and `backpressure/attestations/*.json`.
- Before public initial commit creation, resolve private/proprietary/distribution residue in tracked public tree paths or explicitly regenerate/exclude private evidence: `templates/claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/SKILL.md.tmpl`, `internal/scaffold/templates/claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/SKILL.md.tmpl`, `internal/scaffold/template_contract_test.go`, `internal/scaffold/fixture_workflow_test.go`, `plans/claude-orchestrator-skill.md`, `plans/release-v0.1.2.md`, `plans/open-source-launch.md`, and `backpressure/attestations/*.json`.
- Before closing this preflip gate, rerun the tracked-runtime audit commands from `project-readiness-kit-osl-audit-tracked-files-9gq` and confirm no tracked `bin/`, `dist/`, release archive/checksum, runtime DB/lock, `.ntm/`, `.doctor/`, local log, or generated scratch artifact will enter the public initial tree.
close_reason: Preflip gate satisfied: all dependencies closed (scrub campaign 4 units fa7215a/eeeeac4/d952dce/979dae0, orchestrator-template beads, GitHub-surfaces audit, old-release deletion of v0.1.0+v0.1.1 with tags preserved), re-audit 9/9 pass zero blockers (preflip-audit-report.json), zero legacy prefix in tracked files, tracker prefix renamed to burpvalve (6df0acf), 19 issue-reference edits complete, PVR API 404 recorded as the documented pre-flip deferral with post-flip enablement as a required runbook step (D-OSL-17), br dep cycles empty. Visibility flip authorized by owner (D-OSL-20).

title: osl: add license files to repo and release archives
description:
Background:
The chosen license must exist at repo root and inside every release archive/skill package. Today `scripts/package-skill.sh` copies only the skill directory, binary, and VERSION, so package metadata could claim a license the artifact does not carry.

Dependency-license review feed-forward from Agent Mail 3116:
- Owner selected MIT for Burpvalve's project license in D-OSL-2.
- The Go dependency graph is MIT-compatible; no GPL/LGPL/AGPL/MPL/EPL/copyleft dependency license was found.
- A third-party notices bundle is still required despite MIT compatibility, because dependency licenses impose attribution/notice preservation obligations.
- Minimum notice scope: the shipped `./cmd/burpvalve` binary dependency graph. Conservative scope may include full `go list -m all`, but the binary graph minimum must be covered.
- Include module name, version, license family, and required license/notice text in a `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`, `NOTICE`, or `LICENSES` equivalent.
- `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` is mixed MIT/Apache and includes an upstream `NOTICE`; that NOTICE content must be preserved in the release notice bundle.
- Apache-2.0 dependencies in the binary/full graph include `github.com/spf13/cobra`; the full graph also includes Apache dev/test/transitive modules such as `github.com/inconshreveable/mousetrap` and `github.com/kylelemons/godebug`. Package at least the Apache license text for shipped binary dependencies and preserve upstream NOTICE where present.
- BSD-family dependencies require copyright/list-of-conditions/disclaimer preservation in binary distribution documentation/materials. Binary graph examples include `github.com/atotto/clipboard`, `github.com/spf13/pflag`, `golang.org/x/sync`, and `golang.org/x/sys`.
- MIT dependencies also require preservation of copyright/permission notices in substantial copies.

Scope:
- Add root `LICENSE` using the owner-selected MIT license.
- Add a third-party notices bundle because the dependency review requires it.
- Include `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` upstream NOTICE content.
- Include Apache/BSD/MIT attribution/license text needed for public binary/source release, with binary graph as the minimum scope.
- Update `scripts/package-skill.sh` so every release archive includes `LICENSE` and the notices bundle if present.
- Ensure the skill package carries the same license text and notices needed for redistributed dependencies.
- Add tests or package validation proving extracted archives contain the license file(s) and notices bundle.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root `LICENSE` exists.
- Third-party notice bundle exists and covers the shipped binary dependency graph at minimum.
- The notice bundle includes `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` NOTICE content.
- Release archive extraction shows `LICENSE` present.
- Release archive extraction shows the notice bundle present.
- `scripts/package-skill.sh` handles license and notice files deterministically.
- No release artifact claims a license it does not contain.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Completed by commit e6b5173 (pushed): added MIT LICENSE, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md covering the shipped binary dependency graph including yaml.v3 NOTICE and Apache/BSD attribution, and wired package validation/copying so release archives include the required license/notice files; attestation backpressure/attestations/ac57016f3bf0df00b00a01a6f137bc15d227a68ad7fe342cada6899b922d4976.json.

title: rel: untrack committed dist release artifacts
description:
Background:
The v0.1.1 release committed `dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz` and `dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256` even though `.gitignore` ignores `dist/`. Revision 2 resolves that v0.1.2 stops committing release archives to source. This extends D2's no-committed-binaries rule: `bin/burpvalve` is also being removed by the land-working-set plan, and this bead handles the remaining tracked `dist/` artifacts for the release plan.

Scope:
- Run exactly: `git rm --cached dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`
- Confirm `.gitignore` already keeps future `dist/` files out of source control.
- Confirm `git ls-files dist` returns no tracked release archives after the removal is staged/committed.
- This is a chore commit at the start of the release sequence, before packaging.

Reasoning:
GitHub release assets are the canonical distribution artifacts. Keeping built archives in source makes future diffs noisy, increases repo weight, and lets agents accidentally validate an old local tarball instead of the tag-built release asset.

Acceptance criteria:
- `git ls-files dist` shows no tracked release archive files.
- The chore commit contains only the `dist/` untracking change unless an immediately necessary `.gitignore` correction is discovered.
- Local packaging later uses `DIST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)` so the repo's ignored `dist/` directory is not part of the release validation path.
- No source archive/binary is re-added in later release commits.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
close_reason: Completed in commit a21e4ef (rel: untrack dist release artifacts); committed payload removes tracked dist artifacts and includes Burpvalve attestation f422bcd7.

title: Verify global install, PATH, and shell completion wiring
description:
Background:
Approved idea #14: installing completions or PATH shims should prove that the result actually works. Current completion/install flows write files but need stronger verification, clearer next steps, and a clean split between human guide flow, raw shell script output, and read-only verification.

Goal:
Make install, PATH, and shell-completion setup explainable and verifiable instead of merely writing files.

Scope:
- burpvalve completion with no shell argument should detect the current shell when possible and enter a human-readable guide/install flow; it should not dump raw shell script unless the user explicitly asks for script output.
- Prefer an explicit script mode such as burpvalve completion script zsh or burpvalve completion zsh --print for automation, and document it as raw shell output.
- Add a non-mutating verification path such as burpvalve completion verify and/or burpvalve install verify, whichever best matches the existing command model.
- After completion install, verify the completion script path exists, the rc update was written when requested, the configured bin dir exists, and burpvalve resolves from PATH when that can be observed from the current process.
- If the command is installed but the current shell has not reloaded PATH/completions yet, print the exact source/restart command instead of calling it failed.
- Installer should verify final command resolution when possible and print exact PATH/source guidance when not.
- Support bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell semantics without pretending to source a shell when that is not possible.
- Use shared config-default behavior for bin dir, shell, completion behavior, skills dir, color, and confirmation defaults; explicit flags remain highest priority.
- Keep global installation state in user-level locations: PATH shim/bin dir, shell rc/completion files, and global config. Project setup should write only project-scoped scaffold/config/hook files unless the user explicitly chooses otherwise.
- If shell detection is ambiguous, explain what was inspected and require clarification rather than guessing silently.
- Robot JSON should expose enough facts for an agent to decide whether to retry, source a file, ask the user, or stop.
- Any mutating install/completion flow must show the planned writes and ask for final confirmation defaulting to No unless --force or robots confirm=true is used.
design:
Plan audit revision 4:
- Treat this as the first-install and global command discovery bead, not just completion generation. The user question is: can I run burpvalve from my normal shell, and what should I do next if I cannot?
- Keep three paths distinct: human guide/install flow, explicit raw completion script output for shell managers, and read-only verify output. No-arg completion should detect the shell when possible and start a guide; raw script output requires explicit script/print mode.
- Global installation state belongs in user-level locations: PATH shim/bin dir, shell rc/completion files, skill install dir, and global config. Project setup should write only project-scoped scaffold/config/hook files unless the user explicitly chooses otherwise.
- Treat repo-local binaries as a developer fallback, not a successful user install. Output should say when a repo-local binary exists, when a global command exists, and when those differ.
- Treat install.sh as part of the product. Fresh install from a release archive or skill-shaped checkout must preview user-location writes, require confirmation by default, and explain PATH/source guidance after success.
- Cancellation must happen before user-owned writes. Temporary download/extract directories are acceptable; skills dir, bin dir, rc files, and project files must not be partially created on cancel.
- Verification should distinguish installed-and-working, installed-but-not-sourced, repo-local-only, global-command-missing, wrong-version/wrong-origin where detectable, and truly missing. Those are different user actions.
- Shell support must be honest: bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell get shell-specific paths and source/restart guidance; ambiguous detection asks for a shell instead of guessing.
- Config consumption should use shared config defaults and keep explicit flags highest priority. Robot JSON should expose facts and next_steps, not a single vague success boolean.
- The guide should answer what to run next without requiring the user to understand completion internals.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover zsh/bash/fish/PowerShell plan verification, shell detection, ambiguous detection, missing bin dir, command not on PATH, command found on PATH, repo-local binary only, both global and repo-local present, rc update present, no-rc update mode, configured defaults, explicit flag override, and no-arg guide behavior.
- Tests cover install.sh from a local release/archive fixture: default cancel leaves no partial user-location writes, --yes applies the planned writes, existing skill/shim replacement is previewed, and the installed shim points at the installed skill binary.
- Human output names detected shell, detection evidence, installed completion path, command path/origin, config source if used, whether a shell reload/source is still needed, and exact next command to run.
- install.sh and completion install output name every planned user-location write before mutating: skill destination, existing replacement/backup behavior, shim/bin path, rc/completion path when applicable, and whether the command should be reloaded or sourced.
- JSON output exposes verified booleans, detected shell, detection evidence, config source, command_path, command_origin, completion_path, rc_path, version when available, repo_local_path when relevant, and next_steps.
- Raw completion script output is available only through documented explicit script mode and remains compatible with shell completion managers.
- Mutating install/completion flows preview writes and require confirmation by default. Cancel leaves no partial writes.
- README, skill INSTALL.md, and command help explain global-vs-project installation clearly.
- go test ./... and make build pass.
- A verifier subagent reviews the completed bead before it is closed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified global install, PATH, and shell completion wiring. Evidence: install.sh previews planned skill/shim writes, defaults to confirmation, --yes applies a skill-shaped archive fixture with SKILL.md, completion install prints planned writes before forced mutation, completion verify distinguishes PATH, repo-local, and PATH+repo-local states, docs explain global-vs-project boundary. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier Jason reported PASS.

title: bug: prompts list red after gate-operator prompt addition
description: Root cause task for GATE ESCAPE A25. Commit 7b3f761 added the gate-operator-brief prompt entry but did not update the cmd/burpvalve prompt list expectations, leaving TestPromptsListJSONAndHumanOutput red in a clean worktree. Bisect from RusticDog: e79595d passes, 7b3f761 fails. Scope: fix the prompt entry/list-test mismatch, add any targeted coverage needed so the prompts list stays aligned with PromptBank entries, and document the escape mechanism in this bead close reason/comment: whether go test ./... executed after the final edit during the doctrine gate, and whether verifier test-evidence cells relied on stale or incomplete output. Acceptance: go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run TestPromptsListJSONAndHumanOutput -count=1 passes; go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/backpressure -run 'Prompt|Prompts' -count=1 passes; go test ./... passes or any unrelated failure is explicitly identified; gated commit with real verifier responses and attestation; push immediately after commit.
close_reason: Fixed in gated commit c09dfee (bug: fix prompt list expectation), pushed to origin/main. Payload: cmd/burpvalve/prompts_test.go plus attestation backpressure/attestations/007dc6ee30335d93bbfe01b1dea5241463ef0a038b975269c61d7ee56bb5288d.json. Test evidence after final edit: go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run TestPromptsListJSONAndHumanOutput -count=1 passed; go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/backpressure -run 'Prompt|Prompts' -count=1 passed; go test ./... passed. Verification: native read-only subagents Avicenna 019f2a31-a906-7551-8328-82bef732aaef and Parfit 019f2a31-debc-7ed1-9bad-b33ef6b1583b both matched staged hash sha256:007dc6ee30335d93bbfe01b1dea5241463ef0a038b975269c61d7ee56bb5288d and passed assigned cells. Escape mechanism recorded in bead comment 4: 7b3f761 did not run go test ./... as an executable gate; prior verifier evidence was focused on internal/backpressure/internal/scaffold and did not exercise cmd/burpvalve prompt-list tests.

comments.author: EmeraldBrook
comments.text: Escape mechanism reconstruction for A25: the 7b3f761 doctrine gate did not run go test ./... as an executable gate. The committed attestation has plan.lint_commands null and the hook reported burpvalve lint not_enforced / no executable lint commands declared. My pre-gate manual checks after the final doctrine edit were focused on internal/backpressure prompt-bank tests, internal/scaffold template tests, prompt rendering commands, mirror cmp checks, and git diff --check; they did not include go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run TestPromptsListJSONAndHumanOutput or go test ./... after the gate-operator-brief entry was added. The verifier cells also passed on focused stale/incomplete evidence: ScarletMarsh 3512 cited internal/backpressure + internal/scaffold prompt/template tests, and BrightOwl 3515 cited patch-isolated focused tests and prompt rendering, but neither exercised the cmd/burpvalve prompts list contract. The fix therefore updates cmd/burpvalve/prompts_test.go to include gate-operator-brief in the full ordered list and verifies the exact red test plus broader prompt/full-suite tests before gate.

title: land: 12 setup init readiness and repair
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 12 lands setup/init readiness and repair behavior. This completes the implementation side of issue #6 and issue #9, subject to the explicit post-unit verification cell.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/scaffold/inspect.go`
- `internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go`
- `internal/scaffold/apply.go`
- `internal/scaffold/apply_test.go`
- `internal/scaffold/repair.go`
- `internal/scaffold/repair_test.go`
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: setup/init/repair command hunks and config-consumption hunks identified by the main.go staging map.
- `cmd/burpvalve/init_test.go`: readiness/git-repo assertions.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: setup/init/repair help assertions only.

Implementation notes:
- Non-git and git-dir readiness must be explicit and recoverable.
- Preserve non-destructive scaffold behavior; repair should put missing Burpvalve files back safely.
- Do not add the later issue #17 guided lint wizard or D9 goroutine work.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- `setup`, `init`, and `repair` produce contract-shaped results and actionable next steps.
- Non-git repository behavior is clear and tested.
- Git repository `init --force --json --no-beads --no-ntm` path is tested.
- Issue #9 can be delivered here; issue #6 still requires the explicit verification bead before later units proceed.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Inspect|Apply|Repair|Setup|Init'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Init|Setup|Repair|Help|Git'`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve setup --json`
- `go test ./...`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-12-verify`, listed tests, `git stash pop`.
close_reason: Completed in commit 3c2671f (land: add setup init readiness); attestation backpressure/attestations/3ef29285d66c4ecbc05e9920f02e30cc02c049516f52c610dae7515b5c517375.json records 13 cells, 12 pass / 1 not_applicable.

title: rel: update release docs and badges for v0.1.2
description:
Background:
The release docs and badges must point at v0.1.2 before the tag is cut. Revision 2 explicitly keeps the Go badge minor-only (`1.25`), adds a dynamic CI badge for `ci.yml`, updates pinned release install examples, and preserves proprietary/private metadata until the public launch plan.

Scope:
- In `docs/release-install.md`, update every pinned `--version v0.1.1` example to `--version v0.1.2`.
- In README, bump the static release badge from v0.1.1 to v0.1.2.
- Confirm the Go badge remains minor-only `1.25` and matches `go.mod` (`go 1.25.0`). Do not change it to a patch badge unless `go.mod` changes.
- Add the dynamic CI badge: `https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=main`.
- Note for validation: while the repo is private, badge rendering is verified as an authenticated viewer. Anonymous badge behavior belongs to the later public launch plan.

Suggested verification commands:
- `rg -n 'v0\.1\.1|v0\.1\.2' README.md docs/release-install.md docs/CHANGELOG.md`
- `rg -n 'go-1\.25|go 1\.25' README.md go.mod`
- `rg -n 'actions/workflows/ci\.yml/badge\.svg\?branch=main' README.md`

Acceptance criteria:
- `docs/release-install.md` has no v0.1.1-pinned install example.
- README release badge says v0.1.2.
- README includes the CI badge URL for `ci.yml` on branch `main`.
- Go badge remains `1.25` and matches `go.mod`.
- Skill frontmatter still says `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden`.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
