title: cos: config schema for Claude route
description:
Background:
This is Unit 1 of the approved Claude orchestrator skill plan. Burpvalve currently has defaults.init.orchestrator for the optional ORCHESTRATOR.md sidecar and legacy defaults.init.claude/defaults.repair.claude booleans for the CLAUDE.md symlink route. The owner-approved design adds a separate Claude route enum instead of overloading defaults.init.orchestrator or the rejected claude-md placeholder.

Goal:
Add durable config schema, validation, merge/source tracking, and robot-help documentation for explicit Claude route selection.

Exact payload file list:
- internal/config/config.go
- internal/config/config_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/config_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go
- docs/result-contract.md only if config/setup JSON contract wording must be updated
- backpressure/attestations/<gate-hash>.json generated by Burpvalve

Binding config contract:
- Add InitDefaults.ClaudeRoute string mapped to defaults.init.claude_route.
- Add RepairDefaults.ClaudeRoute string mapped to defaults.repair.claude_route.
- defaults.init.claude_route allowed values: agent-symlink, orchestrator-skill, none.
- defaults.repair.claude_route allowed values: preserve, agent-symlink, orchestrator-skill, none.
- Keep defaults.init.orchestrator scoped only to ORCHESTRATOR.md: off or orchestrator-md. The old claude-md placeholder must either be removed from accepted values or rejected with a migration hint to defaults.init.claude_route=orchestrator-skill.
- Keep defaults.repair.orchestrator as the existing boolean for ORCHESTRATOR.md repair only.

Legacy boolean compatibility rules:
- If defaults.init.claude=false and defaults.init.claude_route is unset, effective init route is none.
- If defaults.repair.claude=false and defaults.repair.claude_route is unset, effective repair route is none.
- If a legacy Claude boolean is true and route is unset, use the route default: agent-symlink for init and preserve for repair. Boolean true never implies orchestrator mode.
- If a legacy Claude boolean is false and route is explicitly agent-symlink, orchestrator-skill, or preserve, validation must fail naming both keys.
- If a legacy Claude boolean is true and route is explicitly none, validation must fail naming both keys.
- Command-line flags are not implemented in this bead, but config tests should leave room for command-line --no-claude to win in Unit 4.

Acceptance criteria:
- Config load/merge/source tracking includes defaults.init.claude_route and defaults.repair.claude_route.
- Invalid enum values fail with actionable errors.
- Inconsistent legacy boolean plus route combinations fail validation with key names.
- Existing configs that only set defaults.init.orchestrator=off/orchestrator-md and defaults.repair.orchestrator continue to work.
- Robot/config help explains that ORCHESTRATOR.md remains separate from the Claude route.

Test commands:
- go test ./internal/config -run 'Claude|Route|Orchestrator|Defaults|Merge|Validate|Source'
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Config|Help|Robots|Claude|Route|Orchestrator'
- go test ./...

Non-goals:
- No scaffold behavior, templates, CLI flags, TUI, or generated skill package files in this bead.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.
close_reason: Completed in commit e605cd8 (cos: add Claude route config schema), pushed to origin/main. Burpvalve attestation backpressure/attestations/db96ceeddcbe93be4d9acbd2a3469a519eb618ccf06ae52644a9279aa49b7b9b.json. Verification: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3187 for judgment cells; ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3186 for mechanical cells; isolated staged-slice go test ./internal/config -run 'Claude|Route|Orchestrator|Defaults|Merge|Validate|Source', go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Config|Help|Robots|Claude|Route|Orchestrator', and go test ./... all passed.

title: bcm: 02 preflight reuse classification and admin guard
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 2 extends the landed read-only preflight code into the classification foundation for mutating close flows. The decisive signal is the staged payload, not bead metadata. This closes the review-found bypass where `--admin-only` could be claimed while non-Beads files were staged.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: extend `beadsPreflightOptions`, `beadsPreflightReport`, `beadsPreflightBead`, `buildBeadsPreflightReport`, `inspectBead`, `stagedPathNames`, and related helpers.
- `cmd/burpvalve/beads_test.go`: classification, admin guard, inspect fields, and br-absence tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: robot help/update assertions if new fields or flags appear.

Reuse contract:
- Extend `buildBeadsPreflightReport`; do not build a separate preflight planner.
- Reuse `normalizeCommitBeads` for multi-bead and `--bead-rationale` enforcement.
- Extend `inspectBead` to decode `issue_type`, `labels`, `closed_at`, and `close_reason` from `br show --json` while preserving existing id/title/status behavior.
- Reuse `stagedPathNames` as the staged-payload source of truth.
- Close-context severity differs from preflight: an in-progress bead is expected/advisory at close time, while missing `br` and empty staged payload on delivery path are fatal.

Binding classification rules:
- Admin path is allowed only when the staged payload consists exclusively of `.beads/**` paths, or when no payload is staged yet in the read-only preview context.
- `--admin-only` with any staged path outside `.beads/**` is a hard error that names the offending paths.
- Delivery is everything else: implementation, docs, config, tests, templates, generated AGENTS docs, or any mixed payload with non-Beads paths.
- `issue_type` and `labels` are warnings only. Type `docs` with a docs payload is delivery. Type `docs` with only `.beads` staged is admin. Payload wins whenever metadata disagrees.
- Beads-specific commands block with structured JSON and exit 2 when `br` is absent. Core Burpvalve remains usable without Beads.

br command hygiene:
- Classification/preflight is read-only and must not run `br close`, `br sync`, `git add`, or `git commit`.
- Future mutation code must never pass `--force`, `--bypass-policy`, `--bypass-reason`, or any bypass option to `br`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `--admin-only` hard-errors with named non-Beads staged paths.
- Delivery/admin classification uses staged paths and reports metadata disagreement as warning only.
- `inspectBead` captures issue type, labels, closed time, and close reason for later state-machine reporting.
- Existing `beads preflight` read-only behavior and current tests still pass.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'BeadsPreflight|AdminOnly|Classification|InspectBead|BR'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Robots|Help|Beads'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed Beads preflight and verifier-orchestration foundations it uses. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m bcm-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses when the payload is delivery work, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. Never fabricate verification evidence, and never gather evidence before `.beads/issues.jsonl` is staged for a closure flow.
close_reason: Implemented and committed bcm-02 preflight classification and admin guard in 3fbdd1c.

title: lexicon: final consistency pass and cheap naming alignment
description:
Background:
The lexicon is cross-cutting. After the canonical glossary, README, CLI/help/report, and prompt/skill beads land, this final bead checks the whole repository for accidental drift before OSL launch scrub work begins. It also allows cheap naming alignment only where it is clearly low-risk.

Depends on:
- D-LEX decisions and docs/vocabulary.md.
- README lexicon section and pitch.
- Help/error/blocked-report wording sweep.
- Prompt bank and skill wording alignment.

Scope:
- Run a repo-wide lexicon consistency pass.
- Confirm product prose uses work unit for generic atomic work and Beads/br surfaces keep bead when tool-specific.
- Confirm README pitch still names backpressure.
- Confirm help and errors remain actionable after friendly wording.
- Confirm robot JSON, schema, prompt IDs, and prompt response fields remain stable and formal.
- Review new or still-fluid docs and plan filenames for cheap lexicon alignment where it improves clarity.
- List any proposed rename with compatibility risk, execute only cheap/non-stable renames, and defer stable path/schema/flag/API renames.
- Record deliberate exceptions in the close reason.

Out of scope:
- No mass rename of stable paths such as backpressure/attestations/, .beads/, prompt IDs, config keys, command names, schema files, generated artifact paths, or feature compatibility surfaces.
- No OSL launch scrub, license flip, history rewrite, public visibility change, or release cut.

Acceptance criteria:
- README, docs/vocabulary.md, D-LEX decisions, CLI help/error text, blocked-report wording, prompt bank, and skill wording agree on definitions.
- feature remains stable in CLI/schema/machine-readable compatibility surfaces.
- burp language is limited to valve refusals.
- Beads surfaces remain Beads-specific and explain their relationship to work units where needed.
- Any cheap naming change is justified; stable renames are explicitly deferred.
- Full tests pass, or any unrelated blocker is explicit and not caused by the lexicon changes.

Verification:
- git diff --check
- rg -n 'backpressure|valve|burp|burped|seal|attestation|work unit|feature|bead' README.md docs cmd internal skill backpressure
- rg -n 'attestations|\.beads|--feature|feature_name|staged_payload_hash|feature x condition|prompt' README.md docs cmd internal skill backpressure
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/backpressure ./internal/scaffold
- go test ./...

Attestation: backpressure/attestations/bb20ab8167dcc8835b62f66efdba71e2907f0703ab6b929139552af4b129d026.json. Verifier refs: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3423 judgment/high pass; ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3424 mechanical/shared pass/not_applicable. Deliberate exceptions: stable compatibility surfaces such as --feature, feature JSON fields, schema/robot JSON, prompt IDs, commands, generated paths, .beads, and attestation paths were not renamed; cheap naming was limited to README/docs vocabulary prose.

title: lexicon: record D-LEX decisions and vocabulary glossary
description:
Background:
The owner approved the Burpvalve lexicon plan Draft 2 on 2026-07-03. This bead establishes the durable source of truth before any README, help, prompt, or skill wording changes. It must land before the OSL launch scrub to avoid double-touching launch-facing prose.

Owner D-LEX rulings to record:
- D-LEX-1: backpressure remains the anchor concept and must appear in pitch-level explanations.
- D-LEX-2: burp is the official noun and verb for a gate refusal; the valve burped a commit back means the commit was refused.
- D-LEX-3: seal is adopted for the evidence artifact, but a seal is explicitly a type of attestation; seal and attestation are interchangeable, and attestation is not erased.
- D-LEX-4: valve is the friendly name for the commit gate.
- D-LEX-5: work unit is the product concept for the atomic change being checked; bead is Beads/br tool vocabulary only; feature remains stable CLI/schema binding vocabulary.
- D-LEX-6: no explain-terms command; glossary lives in README and docs/vocabulary.md, with inline short definitions in help where terms appear.
- D-LEX-7: use the vocabulary where useful, not obsessively; no mass rename of stable paths, schemas, flags, or machine-readable contracts.

Scope:
- Add D-LEX decisions to docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md.
- Cite the 2026-07-03 owner directive as provenance for the D-LEX section.
- Create docs/vocabulary.md as the canonical glossary.
- Define backpressure, valve, burp, seal, attestation, work unit, feature, and bead.
- Define seal as a Burpvalve attestation and say seal/attestation are usable interchangeably.
- Define feature as the stable CLI/schema binding term for current compatibility-bearing surfaces such as --feature, feature JSON fields, Go Feature structs, response/template fields, feature_name, and feature/diff-cluster binding terminology.
- Define bead as a Beads/br tracker issue, one possible tracker-backed work unit, not the generic product concept.

Out of scope:
- No command behavior changes.
- No README/help/prompt/skill wording sweep beyond links or references needed for this canonical glossary.
- No renames of backpressure/attestations/, .beads/, schema fields, flags, or Go identifiers.

Acceptance criteria:
- docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md contains D-LEX decisions matching the owner rulings and cites the 2026-07-03 owner directive.
- docs/vocabulary.md exists, is concise, and is link-ready from README.
- docs/vocabulary.md explicitly protects attestation, feature, and Beads vocabulary boundaries.
- Seal is defined as an attestation; attestation remains valid terminology.
- No code, command behavior, schema field, flag, or path is changed.

Verification:
- git diff --check -- docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md docs/vocabulary.md
- rg -n 'D-LEX|2026-07-03|backpressure|valve|burp|seal|attestation|work unit|feature|bead' docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md docs/vocabulary.md
- go test ./... only if touched files or generated docs hooks require it; otherwise record not_applicable for code tests with reason.

Verifier evidence: BrightOwl judgment/high in Agent Mail 3239; ScarletMarsh mechanical/shared in Agent Mail 3238. title: land: 20 E2E harness and residual test sweep
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 20 lands the end-to-end test harness and final residual test hunks from files that were intentionally split across earlier units.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. title: oxp: defer option-A CLAUDE.md orchestrator mode truth table
description:
Background:
Revision 3 explicitly removes option A from the current orchestrator-experience implementation. Option A means replacing the current `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md` symlink mode with a real `CLAUDE.md` containing an orchestrator operating contract. Prior review found that current scaffold code treats `CLAUDE.md` as a symlink target across target aliases, apply, repair, inspect, and tests, so option A is not a template-only choice.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Option A is deferred wholesale. It is removed from this plan's increments entirely and becomes an explicitly-deferred backlog bead (p4) at conversion time. No migration truth table is designed now; the deferred bead records that the truth table (review-2 ambiguity 3's nine cases) is the first deliverable if ever activated. This plan ships option B only."

Deferred scope:
- Do not implement option A in the current oxp plan.
- Do not add `claude-md` behavior beyond rejecting it as not yet supported in the config schema work.
- Do not alter current `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md` symlink behavior except as required for option B's separate `ORCHESTRATOR.md` target.

First deliverable if activated later:
Create the nine-case migration/repair truth table before any code changes. The required cases are:
1. Existing `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md` symlink.
2. Missing `CLAUDE.md`.
3. Existing regular `CLAUDE.md` with coder-agent content.
4. Existing regular `CLAUDE.md` with orchestrator content.
5. Existing stale generated orchestrator-mode `CLAUDE.md`.
6. `--no-agents` with orchestrator Claude mode requested.
7. `burpvalve repair CLAUDE.md`.
8. `burpvalve repair orchestrator`.
9. `burpvalve setup` conflict/readiness reporting.

Acceptance criteria for future activation:
- The truth table states exact inspect/apply/repair/TUI/robot/test behavior for all nine cases before implementation starts.
- Drift and shared-rule duplication risks are handled structurally, not by vague grep checks.
- Compatibility with repos where Claude is a coder is preserved.
- Existing symlink mode remains default unless a later owner decision changes it.

Status and priority:
- Keep this bead p4/backlog/deferred until explicitly pulled forward.

title: osl: audit GitHub issues releases workflows and About metadata
description:
Background:
GitHub surfaces flip with the repository: issues, comments, labels, milestones, workflow logs/artifacts, release notes/assets, and About metadata. These can expose stale private roadmap or context even if the git tree is clean.

Scope:
- Audit all GitHub issues, open and closed, including comments and attachments.
- Audit labels, milestones, projects/discussions if present, workflow run logs/artifacts, generated release notes, old release notes/assets, and repo About metadata.
- Decide per finding: edit, delete comment/asset/run if possible, close/update, or accept.
- Write public repo About description, topics, and homepage as part of launch.
- Reconcile open issues: every open issue must be real public roadmap or closed/updated before flip.

Acceptance criteria:
- GitHub issue tracker audit covers open and closed issues/comments.
- Labels/milestones/About metadata are public-ready.
- Workflow run logs/artifacts are checked for leaks; old runs deleted if needed.
- Open issue list is reconciled before flip.
- No stale private roadmap issue is misleadingly public.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
notes:
2026-07-05 audit round (BronzeDeer orchestrating; workers MagentaBeaver/GrayCat/CloudyEagle via NTM):
- Track A issues audit: 35 findings. D-OSL-18 local-path scrub EXECUTED by CloudyEagle: 11 edits across issue bodies/comments, before/after evidence + zero-residual rescan proof in scrub-report-A.json (session scratch); no secrets/credentials found.
- 19 legacy-prefix findings DEFERRED by design: issue-side project-readiness-kit-* references must be edited in the same window as the D-OSL-15 tracker prefix rename (last pre-flip scrub step) to avoid tracker/issue divergence. Carry into preflip-gate checklist.
- Track B surfaces: workflow logs = hosted-runner boilerplate only (accept, no run deletion needed); artifacts clean; labels = accepted defaults; no milestones; About metadata currently empty, approved D-OSL-11 values recorded for flip-time apply; v0.1.2 release notes findings in findings-B-surfaces.json.
- Track C releases: full inventory + v0.1.0/v0.1.1 deletion plan + post-deletion verification checklist prepared (findings-C-releases.json) for the D-OSL-4 flip-window step.
Remaining before this bead closes: open-issue roadmap reconciliation execution, legacy-prefix issue edits at rename window, About metadata apply at flip.
close_reason: Audit complete with evidence. title: land: 21 docs and skill refresh
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 21 lands the broad docs/skill refresh after behavior is in place. This keeps user-facing documentation consistent with the landed features without mixing docs polish into earlier feature commits.

Exact payload file list:
- `README.md`: feature docs, not D4-only arrow changes already owned by unit 19.
- `docs/README.md`
- `docs/CHANGELOG_RESEARCH.md`
- `docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md`: dogfooding findings from the July review/orchestration round; this file is present in the working tree and must not remain unaccounted.
- `docs/matt-pocock-skill-patterns.md`: residual docs refresh not owned by unit 19.
- `docs/setup-pre-commit-patterns.md`: residual docs refresh not owned by unit 19.
- `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md`: residual skill docs refresh not owned by unit 19.
- `skill/burpvalve/INSTALL.md`: residual install docs refresh not owned by unit 19.
- `plans/GOAL.md`
- `templates/backpressure/attestations/README.md`
- `templates/log/README.md`
- `templates/log/backpressure/failed/README.md`
- `internal/scaffold/templates/backpressure/attestations/README.md`
- `internal/scaffold/templates/log/README.md`
- `internal/scaffold/templates/log/backpressure/failed/README.md`
- Any other scaffold template README hunk named by the plan and not already owned by units 13 or 18.

Implementation notes:
- This docs commit may reference issues and features delivered earlier in the sequence, but it must not claim #13 or #14 are fully closed.
- Keep D5 public-launch boundary: do not add license/open-source/visibility changes.
- Keep release-v0.1.2-specific changelog/tag work for the release plan.
- Treat `docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md` as durable review/finding context, not as a binding plan; binding decisions remain in `docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md` and the plan files.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- README, docs index, skill docs, and scaffold README templates describe the landed behavior accurately.
- The dogfooding findings file is either tracked in this docs unit or explicitly removed/omitted by the owner; it is not left as an unexplained untracked artifact.
- No stale project name or obsolete workflow language remains except documented historical context.
- #13/#14 boundary language remains correct; #13 and #14 stay open.
- `burpvalve ci` passes on the final tree after this unit.

Test commands:
- `go test ./...`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve ci`
- `test -s docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md`
- `rg -n "Dogfooding|Burpvalve product findings|beads_rust|project-readiness-kit|setup-command-project|v0\\.1\\.2|license|distribution|#13|#14" README.md docs skill/burpvalve plans/GOAL.md`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-21-verify`, listed tests, `git stash pop`.

Final tree clean; ci --feature validated attestation dee0e0e163adcfb10fa8adb760542277372225c4916b9ae5886dc86d74bd7b7a; raw ci remains blocked by multi-cluster feature inference and is reported as a dogfooding caveat.

title: lint: Unit B output vocabulary for no-op advisory enforced and coverage
description:
Background:
Issue #8 established that Burpvalve must distinguish no-op lint from real enforcement. Issue #17 extends that model with optional/advisory command failures and reduced coverage when the user declines scoped setup. This bead defines the vocabulary so humans and agents do not over-read green output.

Scope:
- Keep per-command statuses such as `passed`, `failed`, `timeout` unchanged.
- For `required: false` commands, a failure keeps overall `status: passed` but adds `advisory_failures: [<ids>]` and human output prints `advisory: <id> failed (does not block)`.
- Preserve no-op vocabulary from issue #8: `not_enforced` and `enforcement_level: scaffold-only` when no deterministic commands exist.
- Preserve enforced vocabulary: `enforced: true` with `evidence_strength: command-output` only when commands actually run.
- Add coverage output: `coverage: full` when `declined_roots` is absent/empty and `coverage: partial` plus `unchecked_roots` when reduced scope was recorded.
- Human output appends `coverage: partial -- unchecked roots: ...` to the summary line when applicable.
- Coverage must not change `status`, `enforced`, or `evidence_strength`; commands that ran still ran, but agents get reduced-evidence context.

Tests and commands:
- Add tests for no-op, required pass/fail, optional pass/fail, advisory failures, enforced command-output, and partial coverage output.
- Run `go test ./internal/backpressure ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*Lint.*NoOp|Test.*Advisory|Test.*Coverage|Test.*Enforcement' -count=1` or the actual selected test names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- Burpvalve never claims enforcement for wishlist prose.
- `burpvalve lint` reports no-op, advisory, and enforced states distinctly.
- Optional command failures are reported as advisory and do not block.
- `coverage: partial` appears in JSON and human output when `declined_roots` is non-empty, while `status`/`enforced` are unaffected.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Completed in commit e04e44c

title: rel: commit release prep and push annotated v0.1.2 tag
description:
Background:
Revision 2 resolves the release trigger: do not use `workflow_dispatch`; push an annotated `v0.1.2` tag after pushing the release-prep commit to `main`. This bead owns the irreversible release trigger and must only run after docs, changelog, dist untracking, package validation, and pre-release smoke are complete.

Scope:
- Confirm `release.yml` still triggers on pushed `v*` tags.
- Commit release-prep changes in a single coherent release commit after prior beads have staged the correct files.
- Push `main` before creating/pushing the tag.
- Create an annotated tag, not a lightweight tag.
- Push exactly `v0.1.2`; do not use `workflow_dispatch`.

Exact sequence:
```bash
git status --short
git add docs/CHANGELOG.md docs/CHANGELOG_RESEARCH.md docs/release-install.md README.md internal/attestations/attestations.go .beads/issues.jsonl
git add -u dist
git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"
git push origin main
git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"
git push origin v0.1.2
```
Adjust the `git add` file list only to match the actual release-prep files; do not add build archives, repo-local binaries, `.ntm/`, or unrelated work.

Acceptance criteria:
- Release-prep commit is on `main` and contains only release-prep changes plus `.beads` sync if this plan's beads are being committed as part of release prep.
- Tag `v0.1.2` is annotated (`git cat-file -t v0.1.2` returns `tag`).
- Tag points at the release-prep commit that was pushed to `main`.
- `release.yml` starts from tag push; no `workflow_dispatch` run is used.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
close_reason: Completed release tag checkpoint. Pushed main to e04e44c17b7f2d30ea43b18657bbb877fae501e8; created annotated tag v0.1.2 pointing at e04e44c; pushed refs/tags/v0.1.2; Release workflow run 28594917766 triggered by tag push and completed successfully. Release URL: https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/releases/tag/v0.1.2. No workflow_dispatch used. No release-prep commit was created because no unstaged/staged release-prep residue remained; existing staged files in shared worktree were unrelated chain work.

title: Add guided burpvalve config init TUI
description:
Background:
Burpvalve configuration should be approachable for humans, but the durable schema, precedence, validation, and config show contract belong in the config foundation bead. This bead turns that foundation into a guided Bubble Tea question-and-answer flow without inventing a second config model.

Goal:
Make burpvalve config init a friendly interactive flow for creating or updating global/project configuration, while preserving the noninteractive robot-safe contract from the config foundation.

Scope:
- Commands: burpvalve config init, burpvalve config init --global, and burpvalve config init --project.
- If neither --global nor --project is supplied in an interactive terminal, ask which scope to configure and explain the tradeoff in plain language.
- Use the config foundation schema, loader, validator, source attribution, and writer; do not create another config format.
- Start by showing current effective values and where they came from.
- Group questions into beginner-readable sections: install locations, shell/completion behavior, default repo scaffold pieces, repair defaults, output/color/confirmation behavior, and advanced coordination choices such as Beads/NTM/repo-bin fallback.
- Keep default answers conservative: preserve existing values, inherit when possible, and avoid writing machine-detected guesses unless the user confirms them.
- Allow users to skip sections; skipped fields must stay unset or unchanged rather than being filled with guessed values.
- Show the exact resulting JSON or JSON diff before writing and ask for final confirmation defaulting to No.
- Existing config should be merged and preserved unless the user confirms changing selected fields.
- Support --force, --json, and --robots for noninteractive creation/update using the same confirmation rules as other mutating Burpvalve commands.
- Handle cancel/escape/interruption without partial writes.
- Style the TUI consistently with the rest of Burpvalve, including color auto/always/never and no-color mode.
- Do not run setup, init, repair, completion, install, or hook writes from this command; it writes only the chosen config file.
- Downstream command consumption of saved defaults is handled by project-readiness-kit-approved-improvement-program-xw1.1.
design:
Plan audit revision:
- This bead is only a config writer. It must not initialize a repo, repair scaffold files, install completions, write hooks, or mutate project state beyond the chosen config file.
- The first screen should make global vs project config obvious: global remembers personal defaults; project config travels with the repo and overrides global settings.
- Present detected values as suggestions with source evidence, not as facts to silently persist.
- Section skipping must preserve unset or existing fields so future defaults can evolve and users are not trapped by accidental guesses.
- Show the final JSON or diff before writing and default confirmation to No.
- The same schema and validation must power TUI, --force --json, and --robots so humans and agents do not get divergent config behavior.
- After writing, print exact follow-up commands such as burpvalve setup, burpvalve init, burpvalve completion verify, or burpvalve config show without running them automatically.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests cover scope selection, global config init, project config init, existing config update, skipped sections, confirmation required, cancel/no-change behavior, --force JSON mode, robots input, and styled summary rendering.
- config init help explains when to choose global vs project config, what will be remembered, what stays project-local, and how project config overrides global config.
- No config write happens without confirmation unless --force or robots confirm=true is provided.
- config init does not run setup/init/repair/completion/install as a side effect; it only creates or updates the chosen config file.
- Downstream command consumption of saved defaults is handled by project-readiness-kit-approved-improvement-program-xw1.1, not hidden inside this bead.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented guided config init TUI/config writer with global/project help, final JSON confirmation defaulting No, merge-preserve updates, robot/file confirmation rules, skipped-section and cancel/no-write tests. Verified by focused config/charmui tests, go test ./... -count=1, make build, git diff --check, manual help/robot help checks, and read-only verifier Rawls PASS.

title: lint: parallelize setup inspect probes separately
description:
Background:
Decision D9 also approved parallel setup/inspect probes, but revision 2 explicitly says this is a separate bead outside the wizard chain. It should improve readiness inspection latency without widening the lint wizard implementation.

Scope:
- In `internal/scaffold/inspect.go`, run independent external probes concurrently where safe: git checks, `br doctor`, `ntm --robot-capabilities`, and similar readiness probes already present there.
- Preserve report structure and JSON output ordering exactly as today.
- Bound concurrency reasonably and keep timeouts/error handling equivalent to current sequential behavior.
- Do not add goroutines to unrelated CLI prompt/state-machine code.
- This bead is not required for `burpvalve lint init` wizard correctness, but Unit A still precedes it per the assignment's Unit A blocks everything rule.

Tests and commands:
- Add tests proving JSON ordering is unchanged, probe failures are reported independently, and results match sequential semantics.
- Run `go test ./internal/scaffold ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*Inspect|Test.*SetupProbe|Test.*ConcurrentProbe' -count=1` or actual names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria:
- Setup/inspect probes run concurrently where independent.
- JSON/human report ordering remains stable.
- Probe failure semantics remain unchanged.
- This bead is outside the wizard dependency chain and should not block wizard implementation except through final quality acceptance if the product owner chooses to ship all D9 secondary work together.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Implemented in 99bdc7a

title: land: 06 staged deletes and renames in payload
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 6 fixes staged delete/rename handling and feature detection so Burpvalve sees the real staged payload. This is foundational for issue #15 and for every later attested commit that may include deletes or renames.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/backpressure/core.go`: name-status parsing, `StagedPayloadFile`, deterministic entry sorting. Do not include the `VerifierPolicy` field, which belongs to unit 7.
- `internal/backpressure/core_test.go`: tests for delete/rename payload parsing and deterministic feature/payload behavior.
- `cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go`: only the staged-deletion test functions.

Implementation notes:
- Keep this unit focused on staged payload truth and feature detection.
- Do not include verifier provenance, attestation JSON formatting, lint, or `main.go` command work.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- Staged deleted files and renames are represented accurately in payload entries.
- Payload entries are deterministic and sorted.
- Existing feature detection still passes for ordinary modify/add cases.
- Issue #15 can be referenced as delivered by this unit if tests pass and attestation covers the staged payload.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Staged|Payload|Feature|Rename|Delete'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Staged|Delete|Rename|Commit'`
- `go test ./...`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-06-verify`, listed tests, `git stash pop`.

title: osl: audit tracked beads data
description:
Background:
`.beads/` will become public if tracked. `issues.jsonl` contains local source paths, legacy IDs, and agent/task history. Thresholds decide what is acceptable, but the audit must be explicit.

Scope:
- Audit `.beads/issues.jsonl`, `.beads/config.yaml`, and `.beads/metadata.json`.
- Identify local `source_repo_path` values, legacy IDs, private roadmap material, and agent names.
- Decide whether each is accepted history, scrubbed, or requires a fresh repo/squash path based on owner thresholds.

Acceptance criteria:
- `.beads/` audit has pass/fail results for each tracked file.
- No secret/token/private personal data remains.
- Accepted local paths/legacy naming are recorded under the owner threshold decision.
- Open Beads state is coherent for a public audience or explicitly historical.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Audit findings delivered in Agent Mail 3096; remediation landed in scrub commit 2bf5bbd with attestation backpressure/attestations/8866a766e83eadffb2486f637eec65ba2b8bbd4b7318a4454c20d5602fa7f5c1.json. Closed as tracker hygiene after OSL scrub.

title: lexicon: update help errors and blocked-report wording
description:
Background:
The approved lexicon plan makes refusal language more human without weakening recovery facts. This bead updates human CLI help, user-facing errors, and blocked-report wording. It must distinguish real valve refusals from unrelated failures, and it must protect robot JSON and machine-readable contracts from metaphor-only rewrites.

Depends on:
- Canonical D-LEX decisions and docs/vocabulary.md from the first lexicon bead.

Scope:
- Sweep CLI human help and user-facing errors for gate refusal language, blocked report descriptions, feature/work-unit prompts, attestation/seal text, and Beads/bead phrasing.
- Adopt burp/burped only for refusal paths where the valve rejects a staged or committed payload.
- Add short inline definitions where human help uses lexicon terms, for example:
  - valve (the fail-closed commit gate)
  - burped back (refused by the valve)
  - seal/attestation (the evidence artifact)
  - work unit (the atomic change being checked)
- Keep command names stable: commit, ci, attestations, beads, verifier, hash, setup, repair, init.
- Keep feature compatibility surfaces stable: --feature, feature JSON fields, Go Feature structs, response/template fields, feature_name, staged payload feature binding, and feature/diff-cluster terminology.
- Keep JSON field names stable. Any new friendly message string must be backward compatible and tested.
- Protect robot JSON help and structured result messages from metaphor-only rewrites. Robot JSON may use a lexicon term only when it clarifies recovery and defines the term locally.
- Preserve Beads-specific wording in burpvalve beads ... human help and robot help when the surface is literally about br, .beads/, bead IDs, bead rationale, or Beads close/preflight behavior.

Out of scope:
- No README glossary work except references needed by help text.
- No prompt-bank or skill wording changes except if tests require synchronized fixtures; those belong to the prompt/skill bead.
- No stable schema, command, flag, path, or attestation directory renames.

Acceptance criteria:
- Human help that uses valve, burp, seal, or work unit defines the term inline.
- Refusal text may say the valve burped the commit/work unit back only when the action was actually refused.
- burp/burped do not appear for setup readiness failures, config validation failures, lint not_enforced, command usage errors, unavailable external commands, panics, crashes, or internal errors.
- Robot-help JSON still decodes and retains stable field descriptions for attestations, beads, feature, and staged_payload_hash.
- If a lexicon term appears in robot help, the same string defines it locally.
- burpvalve beads ... human help and robot help continue to say bead/Beads where literally about Beads/br behavior.
- Existing help, blocked-report, commit, and ci tests are updated without weakening assertions.
- No schema, path, flag, or command-breaking rename is introduced.

Verification:
- git diff --check -- cmd internal backpressure
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Help|Commit|CI|Blocked|Attestation|Bead|Feature|Robot' -count=1
- go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Blocked|Attestation|Report|Plan|Feature|Robot' -count=1
- targeted negative tests or grep proving non-refusal paths do not emit burp/burped
- go test ./...
close_reason: Delivered help/error/blocked-report lexicon sweep. Commit 6066575 pushed. Attestation backpressure/attestations/4833bd0cab2156da306b8044946deeedea758100be74a30b8c919f4551a7c7d9.json. Verifier refs BrightOwl 3314 and ScarletMarsh 3313.

title: Tie delivery bead closure to Burpvalve verification and commits
design:
Plan audit revision 4:
- The helper should teach and verify safe order, not perform bead closure by default. Default behavior remains dry-run/read-only.
- Use a preflight/check shape: validate bead existence/status when br is available, classify delivery vs admin-only only from explicit input, show staged payload facts, and print the exact safe sequence.
- Beads integration must be optional at the product level. burpvalve commit without --bead must remain useful in non-Beads projects and must not require br. Beads-specific commands may require br and should fail with clear recovery when br is missing.
- Documentation must explicitly state the non-Beads guarantee, because the implementation being independent is not enough for users or agents to trust it.
- Commit metadata should accept one or more --bead values and record them in one stable queryable attestation field. The same IDs must be visible through attestation list/show/latest.
- The final gate must bind to the exact staged payload that will be committed, including .beads/issues.jsonl after br sync --flush-only.
- If an attestation is generated and then staged, the helper must explain possible attestation drift and require rerun rather than hiding it.
- Coupled delivery should require an explicit rationale in human/robot output so unrelated changes do not get normalized.
- Do not rely on magical admin-only detection. If a bead is issue-only/admin work, require an explicit flag or explicit classification and explain why commit evidence is not expected.
- Do not call br close, br update, git add, or git commit from this bead. Future apply mode would need its own bead and confirmation model.
- Use verifier prompt generation where useful so agents know which feature x condition cells need read-only verifier evidence before closing delivery work.
- The current verifier review found two blockers to resolve before closure: full test suite failure in the E2E rename/delete scenario, and missing README language that ordinary non-Beads commits remain supported without br.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Documentation clearly states delivery bead policy, non-Beads project behavior, admin-only exceptions, coupled-work rationale, exact command sequence, and attestation-drift rule.
- README explicitly says ordinary burpvalve commit without --bead works in non-Beads projects and does not require br.
- Tests cover one delivery bead, one admin-only bead, multiple coupled bead IDs with rationale, missing rationale rejection, invalid bead ID, missing br recovery for Beads-specific commands, bead not closed/ready state guidance, final-payload warning, generated-attestation restage warning, stale bead-state guidance, and dry-run/no-mutation behavior.
- burpvalve commit without --bead remains independent of Beads. burpvalve commit with --bead records metadata and handles missing/invalid bead context according to the documented policy rather than silently dropping it.
- Commit/attestation metadata records bead IDs in a stable queryable field when provided, and attestation query APIs expose those IDs.
- Robot help tells agents how to avoid closing delivery work before evidence is committed and how to report blockers without fabricating verification.
- Helper output includes exact recovery/next commands and does not mutate .beads, git index, or working tree.
- The E2E rename/delete regression reported by the verifier is fixed, or the test is corrected to match Git rename detection semantics while still proving deletes and renames are handled.
- go test ./... and make build pass.
- A verifier subagent reviews the completed bead and reports PASS before the bead is closed.
close_reason: Implemented Beads delivery metadata and dry-run preflight. Evidence: commit --bead records stable bead_ids, multiple beads require --bead-rationale, attestation queries expose bead IDs, beads preflight is read-only and gives safe delivery sequence, ordinary commit remains independent of br, README documents non-Beads behavior and attestation drift. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier Huygens reported PASS after prior blockers were fixed.

title: lint: run lint commands with bounded deterministic parallelism
description:
Background:
Decision D9 approved goroutine work primarily because scoped per-directory lint commands create a workload that benefits from parallel execution. Current production code runs lint commands sequentially. Parallel lint execution ships with the wizard.

Scope:
- Run declared `lint_commands` concurrently with bounded parallelism using errgroup or equivalent.
- Default bound: `min(len(commands), 4)`, deliberately lower than `runtime.NumCPU()` because package-manager scripts and monorepo tools are memory/cache heavy.
- Add/consume `--jobs N`, where `--jobs 1` restores exactly today's serial behavior.
- Reject invalid `--jobs` values: 0, negative, non-integer.
- Preserve deterministic output and JSON result order: emit in manifest order regardless of completion order.
- Preserve per-command timeouts. A timeout in one parallel command must not cancel others.
- Implement explicit `serial: true` two-phase scheduling: phase 1 runs all non-serial commands with bounded parallelism; phase 2 runs all serial commands one at a time in manifest order relative to each other. A serial command listed first in manifest runs after the parallel batch; document this when the wizard sets `serial: true`.
- Never show interleaved stdout/stderr live; buffer per command and print blocks in manifest order. Current buffering limits apply per command; docs note N parallel commands buffer N outputs concurrently.

Tests and commands:
- Required tests: `--jobs 1` byte-identical to pre-change serial output; deterministic JSON order under randomized completion; multiple simultaneous failures all reported; required-vs-optional failure handling unchanged; per-command timeout under parallelism; `serial: true` two-phase scheduling.
- Run `go test ./internal/backpressure ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*ParallelLint|Test.*Jobs|Test.*Serial|Test.*Timeout|Test.*Deterministic' -count=1` or actual names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- Concurrency tests from the plan pass, including `--jobs 1` equivalence.
- Parallel lint execution ships with the wizard.
- Output remains deterministic and non-interleaved.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Implemented in 05d45b9 lint: run lint commands in parallel

title: Add E2E scenarios for approved Burpvalve improvements
description:
Background:
The approved feature work now has a reusable E2E harness plus several closed scenario groups. The remaining E2E work should act as a release-gate regression suite for the behavior most likely to break across package boundaries.

Goal:
Complete the approved E2E coverage matrix by finishing the remaining bead delivery/attestation and explain/recovery workflow scenarios, then run a final umbrella verification pass.

Scope:
- Already covered by closed child beads: setup/config/install/completion, commit/lint/payload/verifier scenarios, and the harness foundation.
- Remaining child work: bead delivery and attestation workflows, then explain and recovery workflows.
- Organize scenarios by user workflow rather than implementation package, so failures point to what the user or agent was trying to do.
- Keep TUI-specific behavior in the attestation TUI bead unless a deterministic non-flaky smoke test is added.
- Guard against unexpected root-level stray files created by CLI flows, demos, or hook execution.

Out of scope:
- New feature work beyond proving the approved behavior.
- Exhaustive fake-br edge duplication at E2E level when focused unit tests give stricter and faster coverage.
design:
Plan audit revision 5:
- Keep this as an umbrella only. Child beads own implementation and close independently with evidence.
- Active order: close .3 bead delivery/attestation E2E first, then close .4 explain/recovery E2E, then run umbrella verification.
- The coverage matrix should prove user workflows end-to-end: setup/config/install, commit/lint/verifier, bead delivery/attestations, and explain/recovery. Closed child beads count as coverage but may be reopened if final verification finds regression.
- Avoid TUI-heavy snapshots here unless deterministic and non-flaky; TUI-specific proof belongs in the attestation TUI bead.
- Every scenario must assert no unexpected untracked root files or explicitly assert and clean the expected generated artifacts.
- E2E failures should include enough transcript or artifact path information to debug without rerunning blindly.
- Prefer several small scenario tests over one giant flow so a failure points to the user action that broke.
- Tests must use hermetic HOME/XDG/PATH and a freshly built Burpvalve binary, never the developer machine global state.
- Cover both Burpvalve-with-Beads and Burpvalve-without-Beads paths so Beads integration does not become an accidental product requirement.
- The umbrella closes only after .3 and .4 are closed, go test ./... passes, make build passes, git diff --check passes, br dep cycles --json is empty, and a verifier subagent reviews the final evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- All child scenario beads are closed with verifier review where required.
- Each E2E scenario asserts exact exit code, key output fields, and final git status or exact expected generated artifacts before cleanup.
- E2E tests cover both human text where stable and JSON robot contracts where appropriate.
- Lint no-op scenarios assert that Burpvalve reports no configured lint command as not_enforced rather than a normal lint pass.
- Attestation formatter scenarios assert valid pretty JSON and exactly one trailing newline for both passing and blocked reports.
- Config scenarios prove project config overrides global config without rewriting it.
- Bead-delivery scenarios prove final attestation is generated against the staged payload to be committed, or else the tool warns to rerun verification.
- Install/completion scenarios prove fresh install, cancel/no-partial-write, PATH/global-vs-repo-local, and shell-reload guidance.
- Non-Beads scenarios prove core setup, commit, lint, explain, and attestation commands remain usable without br.
- The harness guards against zero-byte stray files named after workflow words or help text.
- go test ./..., make build, git diff --check, and br dep cycles --json pass.
- A final verifier subagent reviews the E2E umbrella before it is closed.
close_reason: Closed all child E2E scenario beads (.1 setup/config/install/completion, .2 commit/lint/payload/verifier, .3 bead delivery/attestations/query/drift, .4 explain/recovery). Added final umbrella fixes for hermetic no-br core coverage, lint final-state assertions, and setup --no-beads/--no-ntm readiness filtering. Verification before close: go test ./... -count=1, make build, git diff --check, br dep cycles --json all passed. Read-only verifier Dirac PASS.

Files/patterns: internal/lintconfig/, cmd/backpressure-commit/, templates/backpressure/manifest.yaml, templates/backpressure/lint-rules.md, .githooks/pre-commit template. Commands may be declared in manifest.yaml with id, command, required, paths, timeout_seconds. Lint mode should print skipped wishlist rules separately from enforced command failures and fail closed only for required declared commands that fail.

title: dogfood mode for orchestrator findings log
description:
Background:
The owner requested a light dogfood mode before v0.1.3 release. Burpvalve already has an orchestrator contract surface through the explicit ORCHESTRATOR.md target / option-B path. The missing product surface is an opt-in dogfooding block for orchestrators: when enabled, the generated orchestrator contract should instruct the orchestrator to log all issues and complications encountered during use into a reviewable findings log. This is a dogfooding discipline feature, not a workflow engine.

Owner spec:
- Add an option that adds a dogfooding prompt block to the orchestrator contract file, including the ORCHESTRATOR.md target and/or CLAUDE.md orchestrator surface.
- The block instructs the orchestrator to LOG ALL ISSUES AND COMPLICATIONS that come up during use.
- Findings should go into a reviewable findings log modeled on docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md: numbered entries with Why and How-to-apply.
- Keep it light: template block plus opt-in switch.
- Add init flag and config default, matching the option-B pattern.
- Add scaffold tests plus help/robot text.
- Use ~/Projects/outbound-agent/CLAUDE.md as read-only voice reference: terse orchestrator contract, direct operational rules, no marketing prose.

Scope:
- Add a config default for dogfood mode under the same defaults/init family used by orchestrator option-B behavior.
- Add an init/setup/robot flag or field that opts into the dogfooding block when generating the orchestrator contract.
- Update the ORCHESTRATOR.md scaffold/template rendering to include the dogfood block only when opted in. If a CLAUDE.md orchestrator mode surface exists in current code, wire the same block there; if option-A CLAUDE mode is still deferred, do not implement option A just for this bead.
- The dogfood block should name the findings log path and entry shape, but should not auto-create log entries or monitor sessions.
- Update help and robot docs for the new switch/default.
- Add focused tests for config parsing/default reporting, scaffold/rendering with and without the dogfood block, and help/robot exposure.

Non-goals and hard gates:
- No visibility flip, no publishing, no release cut.
- Do not rewrite history.
- Do not add a workflow engine, NTM automation, Agent Mail sending, or automatic findings capture.
- Do not implement deferred option-A CLAUDE.md orchestrator mode unless it already exists; preserve existing option-B boundaries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default behavior remains unchanged: no dogfood block appears unless the switch/config is enabled.
- With the switch enabled, generated orchestrator contract text includes a clear dogfooding section instructing the orchestrator to log issues and complications in a findings log using numbered entries with Why and How-to-apply.
- Config default and explicit init flag follow the existing option-B precedence style: explicit CLI/robot input overrides project/global config, and sources are reportable where existing config output reports defaults.
- Help and robot docs mention the new switch.
- Tests prove enabled/disabled behavior and config/default parsing.
- Full gate runs before commit; close reason must include commit ref and attestation path.

title: osl: decide old release deletion or veto
description:
Owner decision required before old release handling.

Question:
Delete v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 GitHub releases and assets before the visibility flip, or keep them public despite proprietary-labeled packaged skills?

Recommendation:
Delete v0.1.0/v0.1.1 GitHub releases and assets before flip. Tags may remain because tag source trees show private-era source without downloadable proprietary-labeled skill artifacts. Add one line to launch release notes that earlier versions were private pre-releases.

Owner veto path:
If owner vetoes deletion, explicitly accept that proprietary-labeled artifacts may remain downloadable publicly forever.

Acceptance criteria:
- Owner chooses delete or keep.
- If delete: exact releases/assets to delete are listed and deletion is authorized.
- If keep: risk acceptance is recorded, including proprietary-labeled artifact exposure.
- Release-note wording for private pre-releases is approved if deleting.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.

Cross-review polish binding clarification:
This bead is an OWNER decision, not an implementer task. The owner must choose what happens to existing v0.1.0/v0.1.1 GitHub releases before any public visibility flip.

Decision options:
- Delete v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 GitHub releases/assets before flip; leave tags unless the owner separately requests tag deletion.
- Keep v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 releases/assets public and explicitly accept the proprietary-labeled artifact exposure risk.
- Defer public launch until old release handling is investigated further, naming the missing fact that blocks the decision.

Recommendation:
Delete the v0.1.0/v0.1.1 GitHub releases/assets before flip, leave tags in place, and mention in launch release notes that earlier versions were private pre-releases.
close_reason: Owner ruling recorded in docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md D-OSL-4: delete v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 GitHub releases and assets before any visibility flip. Recording only; no release deletion executed.

title: oxp: document calibrated NTM peer-pane verifier patterns
description:
Background:
The landing round proved a calibrated NTM/Agent Mail verifier pattern: peer panes can provide independent read-only verification when native subagent support is uncertain or cross-pane independence is desired. Existing docs must stop treating per-cell NTM fanout as blanket discouraged while still preserving Burpvalve's boundary as a contract/evidence tool.

Approved plan guidance:
- Extend `docs/ntm-bridge.md` and skill references with proven patterns.
- Peer-pane verification is a first-class independent-verifier pattern, with costs and disciplines stated.
- Native subagents remain valid where tooling suffices.
- Burpvalve never spawns panes and never implies automatic per-cell NTM fanout.

Scope:
- Update `docs/ntm-bridge.md` with calibrated guidance.
- Document verifier packets over Agent Mail, peer-pane verification, per-pane monitoring, pane identity verification, small reviewer/coordinator swarms, and evidence preservation.
- State costs: coordination overhead, transcript hygiene, packet quality, disagreement handling, and latency.
- Preserve top-level boundary: NTM/Agent Mail/beads are coordination substrates; Burpvalve owns contracts, prompts, and evidence surfaces.
- Mention that orchestrator messages are audit trail but are not feature x condition evidence unless verifier policy explicitly names that actor/kind.
- Cross-reference prompt bank entries where relevant once the prompt bank exists.

Acceptance criteria:
- `docs/ntm-bridge.md` no longer blanket-discourages peer-pane verifier fanout.
- It clearly says peer-pane relay is first-class when independence or runtime uncertainty warrants it.
- It also says native subagents remain acceptable and Burpvalve does not spawn/manage panes.
- It includes pane identity/monitoring discipline and disagreement protocol.
- Docs remain paste-safe and avoid shell-hazardous arrow chains.

Tests/checks:
- `rg -n "peer-pane|independent verifier|Agent Mail|identity|disagreement|Burpvalve never spawns" docs/ntm-bridge.md`
- `rg -n -- ' -> ' docs/ntm-bridge.md` should not find unsafe workflow chains.
- `go test ./...` if any docs tests or generated docs checks exist in the implementation branch.

title: Add verifier provenance and manifest-level verifier policy
description:
Background:
GitHub issues #11, #12, and #13 all point to the same core gap: subagent_confirmed is too blunt. Burpvalve needs to distinguish independent verifier evidence, main-agent evidence, CI evidence, human evidence, runtime/model identity, and condition-level strictness without encouraging fabricated confirmations.

Goal:
Introduce a verifier policy and provenance model for every feature x condition cell.

Scope:
- Extend response and attestation schemas with verifier metadata: verifier.kind, verifier.agent, verifier.model, verifier.runtime, verifier.separate_context, verifier.transcript_ref, verifier.evidence_ref, and verifier.created_at.
- Use explicit verifier.kind values such as independent_subagent, main_agent, ci, human, none, and unknown rather than boolean-only confirmation.
- Add manifest-level verifier_policy per condition, such as independent_required, main_agent_allowed, ci_allowed, human_allowed, or optional.
- Treat independent_required as the default for conditions that require external verification; do not silently relax it when metadata is missing.
- Preserve read compatibility with existing subagent_confirmed responses during a transition. New templates, robot help, and docs should prefer verifier fields; legacy subagent_confirmed should be mapped to independent_subagent only when it is true.
- Passing validation must enforce manifest policy without forcing agents to lie about subagents.
- Blocked messages should explain whether the fix is to spawn an independent verifier, adjust policy, provide CI evidence, provide human evidence, or accept a block.
- Avoid storing private full transcripts by default; references should be paths, hashes, or short evidence excerpts unless the user explicitly includes more.
- Keep the schema small enough that agents can fill it reliably; free-form rationale belongs in evidence/message fields, not in policy fields.

Acceptance criteria:
- Response template includes verifier provenance fields and policy hints for each condition.
- Robot help documents the new schema, allowed policy/kind values, and compatibility behavior for subagent_confirmed.
- ValidatePassing enforces independent_required when configured or defaulted.
- Tests cover independent verifier pass, main-agent evidence allowed, main-agent evidence blocked, CI evidence allowed, human evidence allowed, optional/no-verifier policy, missing verifier metadata, unknown verifier kind, and legacy response compatibility.
- Blocked report JSON includes machine-readable recovery next_steps for policy failures.
- docs/attestation-schema.md and README are updated.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented verifier provenance and manifest-level verifier policy. Evidence: response templates include verifier_policy plus verifier provenance placeholders; robot help documents verifier schema, policy/kind values, and legacy subagent_confirmed compatibility; artifacts carry verifier policy/provenance; ValidatePassing enforces independent_required by default/config and optional allows none but blocks unknown; blocked reports include top-level next_steps; docs/attestation-schema.md, README, and skill guidance updated. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; read-only verifier subagent Leibniz rechecked and reported PASS.

title: E2E commit, lint, payload, and verifier scenarios
description:
Background:
Commit-time behavior is Burpvalve's core backpressure loop. Unit tests are useful, but real staged payloads and hooks are where regressions in deletes, renames, lint truthfulness, and verifier policy become visible.

Goal:
Add E2E scenarios for real commit/hook flows, staged payload classification, lint evidence, and verifier policy enforcement.

Scope:
- Staged deleted path and renamed path commit flows.
- Lint no-op evidence strength, enforced lint pass, and enforced lint fail.
- Verifier policy/provenance pass and block cases using generated or fixture verifier responses as appropriate.
- Real git pre-commit hook path for at least one passing and one blocking scenario.
- No unexpected root-level files after hook execution.
- Do not block this bead on verifier prompt generation; prompt generation has its own tests in the verifier prompt bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests assert exact exit codes, key JSON fields, key human output, generated artifact paths, and final git status.
- Lint no-op is reported as missing/not configured evidence, never as a normal pass.
- Deletes and renames appear in the staged payload evidence with correct status.
- Verifier policy failures produce blocked reports with useful next_steps.
- Slow cases are either in go test ./... or documented under make test-e2e.
design:
Plan audit revision 4:
- This bead should prove commit-time pressure with real Git state and hooks, not prompt-generation UX.
- Include direct burpvalve commit invocations and at least one real git commit through the generated hook.
- Deletes, renames, lint no-op, lint pass/fail, verifier policy pass/block, stale payload, and formatter-safe artifacts should each have exact expected JSON fields.
- Keep scenario fixtures minimal so a failure identifies the broken pressure condition rather than a giant omnibus flow.
- Rename coverage must respect Git similarity semantics. If the fixture changes too much and Git reports add/delete, fix the fixture to preserve rename detection rather than weakening the product assertion.
- Lint no-op must be graded as missing evidence, not success, even when other commit conditions pass.
- Verifier policy failures must produce blocked reports that burpvalve explain can later consume.
- Every scenario should assert no accidental unstaged/untracked root-level files after the command finishes, while allowing explicitly expected artifacts.
- If any scenario is too slow for go test ./..., wire it under a named make test-e2e target and document that explicitly.
- Do not rely on live subagents in E2E. Use generated/fixture verifier responses for deterministic policy cases and reserve live verifier review for bead closure.
- Exercise both human-facing hook output and robot JSON where each is stable; do not assert full prose transcripts when a structured field is the contract.
- Use this bead to catch the current verifier failure: the E2E rename/delete test must pass under go test ./... and prove both statuses intentionally.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests assert exact exit codes, key JSON fields, key human output, generated artifact paths, and final git status.
- Lint no-op is reported as missing/not configured evidence, never as a normal pass.
- Lint pass, required lint fail, optional lint fail if supported, and timeout/malformed lint config behavior are covered at the E2E level only where the lower-level lint command already supports them.
- Deletes and renames appear in the staged payload evidence with correct status; the rename fixture must be stable enough for Git to report a rename rather than accidental add/delete.
- Verifier policy failures produce blocked reports with useful next_steps.
- Hook scenarios prove the generated pre-commit hook calls the intended Burpvalve binary and does not depend on unchecked repo-local binaries.
- go test ./... passes, including cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go.
- Slow cases are either in go test ./... or documented under make test-e2e.
- A verifier subagent reviews this bead after implementation before it is closed.
close_reason: Added and tightened E2E commit, lint, payload, and verifier scenarios. Evidence: E2E covers real hook behavior, staged delete and rename classification with old_path/raw Git status, lint no-op/pass/required-fail, verifier policy block/pass, generated artifact paths, exact blocked exit codes, and clean git status. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier Euler reported PASS.

- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Verifier|Policy|Template|Artifact'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Response|Template|Verifier|Commit'`
- `go test ./...`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-07-verify`, listed tests, `git stash pop`.
