title: osl: decide history audit thresholds
description:
Owner decision required before full audit interpretation.

Question:
Confirm public-history thresholds. The plan recommendation is: secrets/tokens/creds always block; personal data beyond the owner's own name/username blocks; local machine paths, legacy `project-readiness-kit` naming, and agent names are accepted as harmless history unless owner vetoes.

Why this matters:
Without thresholds, agents can waste time trying to scrub harmless provenance or, worse, normalize away a real secret as historical noise. This decision controls audit pass/fail and whether history can publish as-is.

Acceptance criteria:
- Owner accepts or modifies the threshold list.
- The accepted threshold says what to do with local paths, legacy names, and agent names.
- The accepted threshold says whether squash-relaunch is even on the table.
- Audit beads cite this threshold when passing/failing findings.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.

Cross-review polish binding clarification:
This bead is an OWNER decision, not an implementer task. The owner must choose explicit audit thresholds before agents interpret history findings.

Decision options:
- Accept the recommended thresholds exactly: secrets/tokens/credentials always block; personal data beyond the owner's own name/username blocks; local machine paths, legacy `project-readiness-kit` naming, and agent names are harmless history unless the owner vetoes.
- Modify the thresholds by naming the exact additional categories that block publication or require cleanup.
- Require a squash-relaunch/history rewrite, with the owner explicitly accepting provenance loss and naming the reason.

Recommendation:
Accept the recommended thresholds exactly, then let audit beads cite those thresholds when passing or failing findings. Escalate to squash-relaunch only for actual secrets, unacceptable personal data, or another owner-named hard blocker.
close_reason: Owner ruling recorded in docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md D-OSL-3: secrets/tokens always block; personal data always blocks; local machine paths always block; legacy project-readiness-kit naming always blocks and must be scrubbed; agent names accepted pending owner veto. Recording only; no audit cleanup executed.

title: land: 02 fill and track operating contract
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 0 commit 2 fills the repo-local operating contract so future coding agents have concrete project rules before the live gate is tracked. Decision D2 requires `AGENTS.md` to stop being a TBD shell and `CLAUDE.md` to be tracked as the compatibility symlink.

Exact payload file list:

Implementation notes:

- The operating contract should name the project purpose, build/test/lint/run commands if known, atomic commit expectations, backpressure rules, and durable decision location.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is in the Phase 0 bootstrap-exception window. Commits 1-3 cannot honestly carry final Burpvalve attestations because the tracked hook/backpressure scaffold is not complete until commit 3. Still run staged-content verification: stage only this bead's payload, run `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`, execute the listed test commands against the staged slice, then `git stash pop`. The tracked hook introduced in commit 3 must invoke the gate through `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` rather than the stale `bin/burpvalve`. From commit 4 onward, use the full attestation choreography: stage exact payload, gather real verifier responses, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>` (plus `--bead-rationale` if multiple beads are deliberately combined), stage only the attestation path named by the tool, rerun the gate, then commit. Do not stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` or other generated files after attestation creation because that changes the payload hash.

title: oxp: add ORCHESTRATOR.md scaffold target and structural drift guard
description:
Background:
Option B is the shipped contract-file design: keep `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md` and add a separate `ORCHESTRATOR.md` that an orchestrating agent can read. This avoids vendor-specific role confusion while preserving one canonical coder contract.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Option B installation rule (exact): the orchestrator target is NOT added to `standardScaffoldTargets`. It is created only when one of: (a) explicit positional target (`burpvalve init orchestrator`); (b) config `defaults.init.orchestrator` set to a non-off value; (c) the init TUI checkbox, which is OFFERED (visible, unchecked by default) whenever the Beads or NTM checkbox is selected in the same run. Never installed silently."

Binding drift guard:
"Drift guard is structural, not grep: `ORCHESTRATOR.md` must link to the `AGENTS.md` gate/Definition-of-Done sections and MUST NOT contain its own copies of gate rules, DoD bullets, or verdict vocabulary except by reference; the template contract test asserts the forbidden section headings are absent, not merely that strings differ."

Scope:
- Add a new scaffold target and aliases: `orchestrator`, `ORCHESTRATOR.md`, and normalized variants.
- Do NOT add the target to `standardScaffoldTargets`.
- Add embedded `ORCHESTRATOR.md` template with orchestrator duties: mission pattern, tick loop, audit/rollback duties, autonomy-by-documentation posture, Agent Mail/NTM/beads boundaries, and links/references to AGENTS gate/DoD sections.
- Add an `AGENTS.md` pointer line only when the orchestrator target is active.
- Add template contract tests enforcing forbidden duplicated section headings/rule vocabulary and required links to AGENTS gate/DoD sections.

Non-goals:
- Do not implement `CLAUDE.md` as orchestrator contract.
- Do not duplicate core gate rules, Definition of Done bullets, or verifier verdict vocabulary in `ORCHESTRATOR.md`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `burpvalve init orchestrator` creates `ORCHESTRATOR.md` without adding it to the standard scaffold.
- `ORCHESTRATOR.md` clearly tells a fresh orchestrator how to operate without becoming the source of truth for coder gate rules.
- `AGENTS.md` pointer appears only when orchestrator target is active.
- Template tests fail if duplicated forbidden headings/rules are introduced.

Tests:
- `go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Orchestrator|Targets|Template|Agents|Drift|Duplicate|Forbidden'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Init|Repair|Setup|Orchestrator|Help|Robots'`
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented in commit 932abfc (oxp: add orchestrator scaffold target); attestation backpressure/attestations/cb6acd320561b4a9a18bae16d49aefd1cc9c75f8642b893db50ccb8820aa22b8.json validates the scaffold target/template payload.

title: oxp: refactor Burpvalve skill to cite prompt bank with bootstrap fallback
description:
Background:
The skill currently carries workflow text directly. After `burpvalve prompts` exists, the skill should cite canonical prompt-bank entries so agents use versioned prompts rather than stale copied prose. However, there is a first-install window where an agent has the skill but not yet the binary, so the skill must retain enough bootstrap guidance to get the binary installed and available.

Approved plan requirement:
- The skill references the bank instead of restating workflows.
- It retains enough bootstrap text to guide an agent that has the skill and not yet the binary.
- NTM pattern documentation is reflected in skill references without making Burpvalve a workflow engine.

Scope:
- Update the Burpvalve skill package docs to reference `burpvalve prompts` canonical entries for commit choreography, verifier briefs, verifier bootstrap, prompt relay, marching orders, orchestrator tick, and bead conversion.
- Keep concise bootstrap instructions for cases where the skill exists but `scripts/bin/burpvalve` or global `burpvalve` is not available.
- Preserve command model, human vs agent use, JSON/robot guidance, commit gate rule, validation guidance, and no-fabricated-verification rule.
- Link or mention `docs/ntm-bridge.md` calibrated peer-pane guidance without implying Burpvalve spawns/monitors panes.
- Ensure skill text remains accurate if `prompts` command is unavailable: it should explain how to install/check the binary first.

Acceptance criteria:
- The skill does not duplicate long prompt bodies that now live in the prompt bank.
- A first-install agent can still find the binary/install path and run setup/config/init/commit/lint correctly.
- The skill tells agents where to get canonical prompts once the binary is available.
- No claims that Burpvalve sends Agent Mail, spawns NTM panes, or self-certifies verifier evidence.

Tests/checks:
- `rg -n "burpvalve prompts|verifier-bootstrap|commit-choreography|orchestrator-tick" skill/burpvalve <scrubbed-local-path> 2>/dev/null || true` adapted to the actual package path.
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve prompts list` once prompts command exists.
- Skill/package validation commands from the package self-test if this implementation touches packaged skill files.
- `go test ./...`

description:

title: vorch: 02 recorded verifier authorization UX
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 1 also records standing pre-authorization as a human/config fact instead of unconditional boilerplate. The repo owner may authorize read-only verifier subagent spawning once; that authorization is policy metadata, not verification evidence.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: config show/init and init/setup surfaces that read/write verifier defaults; robot help and JSON output for verifier defaults.
- `cmd/burpvalve/config_test.go`: config show/init TUI/robots tests for verifier defaults, source reporting, and revocation.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: robot/help assertions for verifier config fields.
- `internal/scaffold/agents_template.go`: generated AGENTS verifier section rendering.
- `internal/scaffold/template_contract_test.go`: template contract assertions for verifier section.
- `templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl` and `internal/scaffold/templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl` if the template copies are still source-of-truth in the target revision.

Binding behavior:
- `init` asks once, explicitly: Are agents in this repo authorized to spawn read-only verifier subagents for backpressure checks?
- The answer stores `authorized`, `authorized_at`, and `authorization_scope` in config using the schema from bead 01.
- `config init` TUI and `--robots` can set/revoke the verifier block and must respect confirmation behavior.
- `config show` exposes effective verifier defaults and source rows.
- Generated `AGENTS.md` gains a composable verifier section only when verifier preferences were configured. It is independent of the NTM section because a repo may use verifiers without NTM.
- The AGENTS prose must state D7: authorization permits spawning; it is never per-cell evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
- A terminal init/config flow can record authorization; robot input can record or revoke it; project config can revoke global authorization.
- Effective config output includes verifier settings and source rows such as `defaults.verifier.authorized`, `defaults.verifier.spawn_method`, and `defaults.verifier.transcripts`.
- AGENTS generation includes the verifier section when configured and omits it when unconfigured.
- No prompt or attestation code treats the authorization string as verifier evidence.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Config|Init|Robots|Verifier|Help'`
- `go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'AGENTS|Verifier|Template|Contract'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.
close_reason: Implemented recorded verifier authorization UX in commit f27ae98

title: osl: coordinate final open-source launch
description:

Goal:
Publish Burpvalve as a public open-source project with coherent license, package contents, installer, README, SECURITY.md, audit record, release artifacts, and anonymous verification. The launch must avoid public exposure of stale proprietary/private metadata.

Acceptance criteria:

- Public installer works anonymously through both documented paths.
- `go install` is absent from public docs.
- SECURITY.md names a working private channel.
- Anonymous post-flip verification suite passes.
- `br dep cycles` remains empty.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
notes: D-OSL-6 amendment: owner is not against outside contributions. Launch readiness should consider a real CONTRIBUTING.md and contribution policy. Treat this as a probable new small OSL follow-up bead; do not silently fold contribution-policy design into unrelated launch cleanup.
close_reason: Open-source launch complete: license/package/README/SECURITY coherent (MIT), audit recorded, old releases deleted with tags preserved, installer verified anonymously via both paths, go install absent from public docs, SECURITY.md channel live (PVR enabled+verified), anonymous post-flip suite passed with one finding fixed forward as v0.2.1, dep cycles empty. Launch executed under D-OSL-20 with fail-closed escalations throughout.

- scripts/pane_wake.py
- scripts/attestation_summary.py
- scripts/append_finding.py
- SELF-TEST.md
- .MAINTAINER-CHECKS.md, unlinked from SKILL.md

Binding SKILL.md contract:
- H1 is # burpvalve-orchestrator.
- Core-rule block appears near the top: coordinate evidence flow, do not manufacture evidence; one atomic payload per gated commit; terminal wakes route attention only and are not verifier verdicts.
- Quick Start provides the shortest safe coordinator loop.
- Decision tables cover role/action choice, native subagent vs peer-pane relay, hold conditions, and script usage.
- Reference map links every references/, examples/, scripts/, and SELF-TEST.md resource exactly once and does not link .MAINTAINER-CHECKS.md.
- Frontmatter is upload-safe: name burpvalve-orchestrator, folded description under 500 parsed characters, category other, tags ctx-cli and tool-git, license MIT, distribution forbidden.
- MIT is used because the generated scaffold content ships from the MIT-licensed Burpvalve repo; distribution forbidden is used because this is project-local scaffold output, not a cloud-published JSM package.

Binding script contract:
- Python standard library only; no binaries.
- Every script supports --help.
- Dry-run is the default wherever a script can write or run an external command.
- Mutation or command execution requires explicit --write or --execute.
- pane_wake.py invokes ntm --robot-capabilities and a fresh ntm --robot-snapshot before printing or running a wake; it must not guess pane indices.
- attestation_summary.py reads Burpvalve attestation JSON and prints feature, payload hash, verdict counts, verifier provenance summary, and transcript refs.
- append_finding.py appends a dated finding skeleton only with explicit --write and refuses missing/unrecognized findings logs.

Alias boundary:
- Add internal route constants and template plumbing only. Do not expose public aliases such as claude-skill or orchestrator-skill unless Unit 3 behavior lands in the same commit. Public alias exposure belongs with route-aware behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Standard scaffold target resolution is unchanged and does not include the orchestrator skill route.
- Current public target aliases still normalize as before.
- Template contract tests prove every required package resource exists and is linked correctly.
- Template contract rejects binary files under the generated package.
- Generated script tests prove --help, dry-run default, and explicit mutation/execute flags.

Test commands:
- go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Target|Template|Claude|Skill|Resource|Script|SelfTest|Maintainer|NoBinary'
- go test ./...

Non-goals:
- No public CLI flag, TUI, robot input, init/repair/apply behavior, or route inspection in this bead.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.

title: osl: execute current-tree scrub
description:
Background:
D-OSL-7 changes the launch history strategy to squash-relaunch: the public repository is created from a scrubbed current tree as a fresh initial commit, while this private repository keeps full history and seal bindings. That resolves the full-history publication blocker, but makes the current tree the launch-critical artifact.

Scope:
- Execute the current-tree scrub before the public initial commit is created.
- Scrub HEAD for the audit counts already identified: 670 legacy-naming lines and 243 local-path lines.
- Remove or rewrite launch-blocking `project-readiness-kit` legacy naming, local machine paths, private/proprietary wording, stale demo wording, and tracked metadata that would appear in the public initial tree.
- Apply ScarletOwl's tracked Beads data cleanup shape from the z8k audit: choose and document either a rename-map approach or regeneration approach before mutating Beads data. The choice must preserve launch readability and avoid breaking active execution unexpectedly.
- Treat renaming the `project-readiness-kit` bead-ID prefix as the LAST pre-flip step because it is disruptive while launch work is still in flight.
- Fold in demos audit r8y findings: decide or scrub generic `<scrubbed-demo-home>` demo paths, refresh stale generated demo assets, clarify demo-tool `go install` wording, and keep any owner-approved exceptions explicit.
- Fold in D-OSL-10: replace test fixture `test@example.com` uses with `michael-bltzr@users.noreply.github.com`.
- Keep all work behind D-OSL-5 hard no-launch gate; this bead does not authorize repo rename, public repo creation, visibility flip, issue filing, or release publication.

Acceptance criteria:
- Current tree scan after scrub shows no launch-blocking legacy `project-readiness-kit` naming except any explicitly documented and owner-accepted internal/private residue.
- Current tree scan after scrub shows no local machine paths or private personal paths intended for public launch.
- Beads data cleanup decision is recorded, implemented, and verified; any bead-prefix rename occurs only as the final pre-flip scrub step.
- Demo sources and generated assets are either refreshed and visually reviewed or explicitly accepted with documented rationale; `<scrubbed-demo-home>` path handling is resolved.
- `test@example.com` no longer appears in public-tree test fixtures; fixture email uses `michael-bltzr@users.noreply.github.com`.
- The scrubbed tree is ready to become the fresh public initial commit for `clicksopendoors/burpvalve` after owner flip authorization.

description:

title: ifr2-C2: add read-only payload ownership accounting command
description:
Background:
This bead is Unit C2 from the approved issue-followups round-2 plan. It builds on C1 by adding the read-only payload accounting command that maps staged and optionally untracked paths against explicit ownership records.

Scope:
- Add a read-only command, tentatively burpvalve account payload, with JSON and human output. The exact command name may change during implementation review, but the contract must not.
- Read ownership records from stdin JSON and/or --ownership-file <path> using the C1 schema.
- Apply source precedence: stdin wins over --ownership-file for the same unit/path/kind claim.
- Classify staged paths and optionally untracked paths against declared ownership records.
- Work in non-Beads repos; Beads absence must not fail the command unless Beads-specific enrichment is explicitly requested in a later bead.

Binding command behavior:
- Reports owning bead or unit id when declared.
- Reports ownership kind: whole_path, function, test, hunk, generated, admin, or exception.
- Reports source of each ownership claim.
- Reports conflicts when two active units claim the same path incompatibly.
- Reports unowned staged and untracked paths.
- Reports generated evidence exceptions.
- Reports split-file paths that require human/agent hunk discipline.
- Does not mutate Beads, stage files, reserve files, or decide claims. It is an audit/readiness helper.

Binding active-unit rule for this bead:
- Active units are explicit ownership records in input. Beads enrichment is reserved for C3 and must not be required here.

Binding untracked behavior:
- For untracked paths, output whether each path is ignored by git, under a generated evidence prefix, covered by explicit ownership/exception record, or unowned.
- Ignored generated-response scratch paths and untracked non-ignored source paths must be distinct statuses.

Likely file touches:
- cmd/burpvalve/main.go
- cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go
- new cmd/burpvalve/account_test.go or similar command tests
- internal ownership/accounting package from C1
- docs/result-contract.md if JSON result shape is public.

Tests required:
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run "Account|Payload|Owner|Staged|Untracked|Robots|Help"
- go test ./internal/... -run "Account|Payload|Owner|Split|Generated|Untracked"
- go test ./...

Required scenarios:
- staged path with one owner reports owned;
- staged path with no owner reports unowned;
- staged path with two incompatible owners reports conflict;
- split-file path with declared function/test ownership reports shared_declared and names all owners;
- generated evidence JSON reports generated_exception;
- untracked ignored path reports ignored_untracked;
- untracked non-ignored path without ownership reports unowned;
- ignored generated-response scratch path and untracked non-ignored source path produce distinct statuses;
- command is read-only: repeated runs do not change git status.

Acceptance criteria:
- JSON and human command output make ownership coverage, conflicts, unowned paths, generated exceptions, and split-file declarations clear.
- Command works in repos without Beads.
- Command has robot/help coverage and no side effects.
- No implicit durable repo-local ownership config is introduced.

Non-goals:
- No Beads enrichment or Beads-derived active-unit claims; that is C3.
- No AST/hunk parser unless it is trivial and does not widen blast radius; split-file support is declarative in round 1.
- No mutation of git index, working tree, Beads, or Agent Mail reservations.

title: Add config schema, precedence, and config show foundation
description:
Background:
Burpvalve needs a lightweight durable configuration layer before guided human flows and install/completion defaults can be cleanly built on top of it. The foundation should be boring, inspectable, and safe: global preferences are remembered globally, project preferences can override them, and commands can explain where each effective value came from.

Goal:
Define and implement the config schema, precedence rules, validation, noninteractive inspection, and noninteractive write contract. This bead creates the substrate; full interactive prompting belongs in the guided config init bead, and command-specific prompt behavior belongs in the beads for those commands.

Scope:
- Define explicit config file locations: global XDG-style config and project-local config in the repository root.
- Define a versioned schema for skills dir, bin dir, shell, completion update behavior, default init pieces, default repair pieces, Beads, NTM, repo-local bin fallback, tool docs, hooks, color preference, and confirmation behavior.
- Preserve a real distinction between unset/inherited values and explicit false values, so project config can override global config in either direction.
- Implement config loading, merging, validation, normalization, and source attribution without requiring any config file to exist.
- Add burpvalve config show with readable human output, stable --json output, and --robots documentation.
- Add noninteractive config writing for global/project config using --force/--json or --robots confirm=true. If the terminal is interactive and no noninteractive input is supplied, later guided config init work may take over.
- Make existing command code able to read effective config defaults without making config mandatory; this foundation only has to wire defaults where the stable call path already exists, while later command-specific beads must finish prompt/default behavior for their own flows.
- Preserve unknown user-managed data only if the schema explicitly supports it; otherwise reject unknown fields with recovery steps rather than silently dropping them.
- Do not store secrets, transient shell state, or machine-discovered values unless the user explicitly selected them.
- All writes should be stable, pretty JSON with a trailing newline and no partial file left behind on validation failure.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover absent config, global-only config, project-only config, project overriding global config, explicit false overriding true, invalid JSON, unknown fields, unsupported schema version, bad path strings where validation is possible, source attribution, and no-write-without-confirmation.
- config show prints config file paths, whether each file exists, effective values, and value sources in readable human output and stable JSON.
- Existing setup/scaffold inspection can read effective defaults from config without making config mandatory; later beads that own init, repair, completion, and install prompts must consume the same config loader instead of inventing parallel state.
- Robot help documents config input/output schemas and confirmation rules.
- README and skill docs explain global vs project config clearly, including how explicit project values override global values.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented config foundation: versioned config schema with validation/normalization, global+project precedence with source attribution and explicit false overrides, config show human/JSON output including found/missing config paths, noninteractive config init writes with confirmation rules, setup/scaffold default loading, robot help schemas, and README/skill docs. Verification: go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/config ./internal/scaffold -count=1 passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier subagent Banach rechecked and reported PASS.

title: ifr2-A: warn on stale-risk repo-local Burpvalve binaries in setup and repair
description:
Background:
This bead implements Unit A from plans/issue-followups-round-2.md. It covers issue draft 3 and dogfooding finding A3. A3 exposed silent hook provenance ambiguity: a repo could pass or fail a gate without making clear whether the active hook would run current source, PATH burpvalve, or stale repo-local bin/burpvalve.

Binding contract:
- burpvalve setup --json and human setup output expose the command path a hook would use and warn when that path is a repo-local fallback with stale-risk facts.
- burpvalve repair preserves setup warning facts when repair installs, preserves, or detects repo-local bin/burpvalve.
- Warning severity is diagnostic, not fatal, unless an existing required check is already blocked. A possibly stale but valid repo-local fallback should make provenance explicit, not make a healthy repo fail setup.
- Warning facts name hook_command_source, repo_local_path, repo_local_exists, repo_local_ignored, path_command, freshness_status, comparison_basis, and warning_code.
- Freshness status values are exactly fresh, stale, unknown, and not_applicable. Partial facts never produce a safe/fresh claim.
- Owner ruling: first increment uses mtime/source freshness plus short-timeout version check when available; unavailable, timed out, conflicting, symlink, non-regular, or unreadable facts remain unknown.
- If this repo contains cmd/burpvalve, compare bin/burpvalve mtime with newest tracked source in cmd/, internal/, go.mod, go.sum, internal/scaffold/templates/, templates/, and install/release scripts that affect the built binary. Exclude .git, bin/, package/build output, generated logs, backpressure/attestations/*.json, and untracked work from the freshness mtime set. If relevant source dirt exists outside that set, emit dirty_source_unknown.
- Compare repo-local binary mtime with PATH command mtime only when both are regular files. Optionally compare burpvalve --version output from both commands using a short timeout.
- Recovery text presents explicit choices: run from source for this repo, install/use PATH, or intentionally keep repo-local fallback. It must not command deletion or replacement.

Likely file touches:
- internal/scaffold/inspect.go
- internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go
- internal/scaffold/repair.go
- internal/scaffold/repair_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/init_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go
- docs/result-contract.md if setup JSON fields or warning semantics change.

Avoid unless tests prove setup and hook semantics already disagree:
- hook templates. This bead warns about what hooks would run; it should not alter hook command resolution as a side effect.

Tests required:
- go test ./internal/scaffold -run "Inspect|Repair|RepoLocal|Tool|Hook|Setup"
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run "Init|Setup|Repair|Explain|Robots|Help|RepoLocal|Hook"
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run "E2E|Setup|RepoLocal" if E2E fixtures are extended
- go test ./...

Required scenarios:
- no repo-local binary and PATH command present: no stale warning;
- repo-local binary present but hook would use PATH: report repo-local fact but do not warn as active hook source;
- repo-local binary present and hook would use repo-local: warning names source and path;
- repo-local binary ignored by git: warning includes ignored/conflict fact;
- repo-local binary older than source tree or PATH command: warning says stale;
- mtime/version unavailable, version check timeout, symlink command, or non-regular command: warning says unknown rather than fresh;
- source dirt present outside the mtime comparison set: comparison_basis records dirty_source_unknown;
- repair result preserves warning facts after installing or preserving bin/burpvalve.

Acceptance criteria:
- setup/repair JSON and human output make active hook command provenance explicit.
- Stale-risk repo-local fallback produces a warning without forcing workflow.
- Freshness classification is conservative and uses only the four approved statuses.
- Robot fields are stable and documented where result contract changes.

Non-goals:
- No forced binary deletion/replacement.
- No hook command resolution redesign.
- No ownership accounting helper.
- No release/install workflow changes.

title: land: 13 hook feature-context templates
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 13 updates hook feature-context templates. It follows setup/init readiness so generated hooks and templates can assume the tracked scaffold and config behavior exist.

Exact payload file list:
- `templates/githooks/pre-commit`
- `internal/scaffold/templates/githooks/pre-commit`

Implementation notes:
- Keep hook behavior source-based (`go run ./cmd/burpvalve`) and preserve environment forwarding required by the gate.
- The root `.githooks/pre-commit` belongs to land: 03 tracked live gate, not this unit.
- Do not stage `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`, `cmd/burpvalve/init_test.go`, or unrelated hook tests here unless the owner revises the land plan; revision 2 assigns this unit to template copies plus scaffold template-contract tests.
- Do not introduce new verifier orchestration, lint wizard, or bead closure behavior.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both template copies agree on source invocation and env forwarding.
- Generated/repaired hook behavior is covered by scaffold template-contract tests.
- Feature context variables needed by commit evidence are preserved.
- The root tracked live hook remains owned by land: 03 and is not duplicated into this commit.

Test commands:

title: bcm: 06 attestation-keyed drift check
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 5 adds `burpvalve beads drift`, an advisory check redesigned around attestations rather than close reasons. Because close happens before commit, close reasons cannot contain commit SHAs; reliable linkage is the attestation `bead_ids` plus git history.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: `beads drift` command, flags, result-contract JSON, human output, robot/help docs.
- `cmd/burpvalve/beads_test.go` or new `cmd/burpvalve/beads_drift_test.go`: drift fixtures and br-list parsing tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go`: properly attested closure and dirty-tree possible-drift fixture if integration coverage is appropriate.
- `internal/attestations/query.go` and `internal/attestations/attestations_test.go` only if attestation query helpers need an extension for efficient bead ID lookup.

Binding drift design:
- Command: `burpvalve beads drift`.
- Advisory only. It does not close beads, stage files, write files, or block direct `br` use unless a project separately wires it into lint/CI.
- Uses `br list --status closed --json` and `closed_at` within a configurable window, default 7 days.
- Correlates recently closed beads against attestation `bead_ids` under `backpressure/attestations/` and commit messages in git history.
- Also considers current tree dirtiness.
- If a recently closed bead appears in no attestation bead IDs and no commit message while the tree is dirty, report `possible` drift. Wording must not claim matched changes.
- A properly attested closure produces no drift flag.
- The existing stale-payload e2e test is not sufficient; add a new closed-beads-plus-dirty-tree fixture.
- Inverse drift, meaning in-progress beads whose work appears committed, is deferred until bead-file linkage exists.

br command hygiene:
- `beads drift` may read from `br list --status closed --json`; it must not pass `--force` or bypass flags, and it must not mutate Beads.
- If it shells to git, commands are read-only and named in diagnostics where useful.

Acceptance criteria:
- Drift fixture with recently closed bead, no matching attestation/commit, and dirty tree reports possible drift.
- Properly attested bead closure reports no drift.
- Clean tree with closed unmatched bead is advisory/nonfatal and does not overclaim.
- Malformed or missing `closed_at` entries are handled with warnings, not crashes.
- JSON output follows result contract with structured `next_steps`.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'BeadsDrift|Drift|ClosedDirty|AttestedClosure|Robots|Help'`
- `go test ./internal/attestations -run 'Bead|Query|Drift'` if attestation helpers are changed.
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed Beads preflight and verifier-orchestration foundations it uses. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m bcm-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses when the payload is delivery work, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. Never fabricate verification evidence, and never gather evidence before `.beads/issues.jsonl` is staged for a closure flow.
close_reason: Complete bcm-06 attestation-keyed drift check

title: cos: CLI TUI robot route selection for Claude orchestrator
description:
Background:
This is Unit 4 of the approved Claude orchestrator skill plan. Scaffold internals now understand Claude routes; this bead exposes the route choice to humans and agents through CLI flags, TUI prompts, robot input/output, help text, and config wiring.

Goal:
Make the Claude route choice obvious on every setup surface while preserving compatibility with --no-claude and --no-claude-symlink.

Exact payload file list:
- cmd/burpvalve/main.go
- cmd/burpvalve/init_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/config_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go
- internal/charmui/init.go or equivalent TUI files
- internal/charmui/*test.go
- docs/result-contract.md if robot/setup JSON fields change
- backpressure/attestations/<gate-hash>.json generated by Burpvalve

Binding command surfaces:
- burpvalve init --help documents --claude-route agent-symlink|orchestrator-skill|none.
- burpvalve repair --help documents --claude-route preserve|agent-symlink|orchestrator-skill|none.
- burpvalve repair exposes an explicit regular-CLAUDE.md adoption selector, for example --adopt-claude-md, and robot input such as adopt_claude_md: true. Exact name may vary if tests/help make a clearer choice, but behavior must be explicit.
- burpvalve setup --json reports detected route, expected route, route source, missing route pieces, conflicts, and planned changes.
- burpvalve config init --robots documents defaults.init.claude_route and defaults.repair.claude_route.
- burpvalve init --robots and repair --robots accept claude_route.
- JSON results include claude_route, claude_route_source, created, repaired, skipped, and conflicts entries precise enough for agents.

Binding TUI contract:
- Interactive init/repair includes a Claude Code route prompt when Claude is not skipped.
- Choices are Ordinary agent, Orchestrator, and No Claude route.
- Prompt copy explains that orchestrator mode changes CLAUDE.md from a symlink into a generated regular bootstrap file and installs .claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/.
- Existing route conflicts are shown before final confirmation.
- Final confirmation includes the selected route in the pieces list.
- If adoption is offered, it is explained separately from route selection.

Compatibility rules:
- --no-claude and --no-claude-symlink map to route none for the current invocation.
- Command-line explicit route wins over config except that contradictory explicit flags fail before mutation.
- --no-agents plus active route follows Unit 3 failure/proceed rules.

Acceptance criteria:
- Human, CLI, robot, and JSON surfaces all expose the route choice clearly.
- Existing ordinary-agent symlink route remains the default for non-interactive init when no config says otherwise.
- Robot examples cover agent-symlink and orchestrator-skill.
- Help text states ORCHESTRATOR.md is still a separate optional sidecar target.

Test commands:
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Init|Repair|Setup|Config|Help|Robots|Claude|Route|Adopt|Agents|Orchestrator'
- go test ./internal/charmui -run 'Init|Repair|Claude|Route|Confirm|Adopt'
- go test ./...

Non-goals:
- No changes to generated skill package templates beyond wiring if an internal API name changes; package content ownership is Unit 2.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.

Ruling 3262 scope reduction:
- The explicit regular-CLAUDE.md adoption selector is no longer owned by this bead; minimal repair `--adopt-claude-md`, matching repair robot input, and required route plumbing moved into `project-readiness-kit-cos-route-aware-scaffold-7ae1` so every landed commit keeps `go test ./...` green.
- This bead still owns the remaining Unit 4 surfaces: human TUI route prompt, remaining robot/setup JSON fields and output polish, route confirmation flow, fuller help/config tests, and any non-minimal route UX not required by the Unit 3 split blocker.
- Do not reimplement or duplicate the adoption selector unless the earlier bead failed to land it; build on the landed option instead.

Attestation: backpressure/attestations/3be832aa031b19cef17bc4ebbab0eefb271f5eedce4c2167bcba548fc34c2242.json. Verification: BrightOwl failed v1 robot-help discoverability in Agent Mail 3322; v2 fixed robot input/output docs and help tests; BrightOwl pass in 3328, ScarletMarsh pass in 3326 with full-test addendum 3327.

title: vorch: 04 verifier begin bound response file
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 3 starts the ingestion flow with `verifier begin`. A single per-cell submit cannot satisfy current response validation because atomicity and all enabled condition entries are top-level requirements. Begin creates the hash-keyed response file that later submits update.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts.go`: bound responses structs/helpers, or move new helpers into `internal/backpressure/verifier_responses.go` if split for clarity.
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts_test.go` and/or `internal/backpressure/verifier_responses_test.go`: begin template and binding tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: `verifier begin` command, flags, robot/help/result-contract output.
- `cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go`: begin CLI tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: begin robot help assertions if separate.

Binding behavior:
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve verifier begin` creates `log/backpressure/responses/<staged-payload-hash>.json`.
- It uses `BuildResponsesTemplate` semantics so all enabled conditions are present as `unknown` stubs in manifest order.
- The file contains a top-level binding block with `staged_payload_hash`, `manifest_hash`, and per-condition `condition_file_hash` values.
- Atomicity is set once by the committing agent through required flags: `--one-feature --atomicity-message "..."`. Begin refuses to create a file unless atomicity is explicit.
- Begin refuses empty staged payloads with a result-contract error and next steps.
- Begin preserves one-atomic-feature scope; no multi-feature support is implied.

Acceptance criteria:
- Begin writes the hash-keyed response file only for a non-empty staged payload.
- All enabled conditions are present, ordered from the manifest, and initialized as unknown.
- Atomicity fields are caller-supplied and preserved for submit; submit must not rewrite them.
- Result JSON follows the existing result contract with `next_steps` on blockers.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'BoundResponse|Begin|Template|Binding|Atomicity'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'VerifierBegin|Begin|Responses|Robots|Help'`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve verifier begin --help`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.
close_reason: Completed in 131f588: verifier begin bound response file

title: rel: package and validate v0.1.2 locally before release
description:
Background:
Before the tag exists, release validation must use a locally built v0.1.2 package from a temporary `DIST_DIR`; using README's `--version latest` flow before release would falsely validate v0.1.1. This bead proves the package content and binary version before any commit/tag/push step.

Scope:
- Run `make build VERSION=v0.1.2` so `./bin/burpvalve --version` reports v0.1.2 for local curiosity checks.
- Run `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`.
- Run `make check-size`. If it warns above 8 MiB, record the size in the v0.1.2 changelog entry because `release.yml` uses generated notes with no custom body.
- Validate the extracted release package, not just source.
- Do not use repo `dist/` for this validation; do not stage any generated archive.

Exact validation command skeleton:
```bash
make build VERSION=v0.1.2
tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"
VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh
make check-size
tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"
tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"
test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"
jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"
"$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version
```

Acceptance criteria:
- Package archive is created under a temp directory, not tracked repo `dist/`.
- Extracted `VERSION` file is exactly `v0.1.2`.
- Extracted skill passes `jsm validate`.
- Extracted packaged binary reports `v0.1.2`.
- `make check-size` passes or the >8 MiB warning is documented in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.
notes: Ruling 2565 amends extracted-package validation: jsm scope is the skill portion only. In extracted archive, temporarily move scripts/bin/burpvalve aside, run jsm validate on the remaining skill package, restore/assert binary separately (present, executable, reports v0.1.2), and diff archive completeness against checked-in skill/burpvalve contents + binary + VERSION. Prior full extracted-package jsm failure is expected because release archive bundles platform binary by design.
close_reason: Completed amended validation per ruling 2565 at HEAD 90f7c1135dabb8a0181aee6720ffaa7eab000c63. Evidence: <scrubbed-temp-path>; archive <scrubbed-temp-path>; extract <scrubbed-temp-path> make build VERSION=v0.1.2, temp DIST_DIR package, make check-size, archive completeness diff, split-scope jsm validate, and binary v0.1.2 assertions passed.

title: Add structured recovery contracts to Burpvalve outputs
description:
Background:
Burpvalve currently emits useful JSON, but several flows still force humans and agents to infer whether a failure is fatal, optional, partial success, or a no-op. Approved direction: improve setup rather than create a separate doctor command. This bead establishes the shared result contract that other features can reuse. It also covers the limited approved fix for GitHub issue #8: lint no-op evidence must be truthful, while the broader guided lint setup belongs to GitHub issue #17 and is not part of this bead.

Goal:
Introduce a structured recovery contract across setup/init/repair/lint/commit outputs so every machine-readable result distinguishes required vs optional failures, fatal vs advisory blockers, partial success, evidence strength, and exact next steps.

Scope:
- Add reusable JSON fields or nested structs such as schema_version, command, status, severity, fatal, partial_success, optional_conflicts, next_steps, recovery_commands, evidence_strength, enforcement_level, and diagnostics where appropriate.
- Use stable status/enforcement enums rather than prose-only states.
- Preserve backward-compatible existing fields where practical; add fields rather than renaming unless the implementation includes a migration note.
- Setup should remain the central inspection/diagnostic surface; do not add a separate doctor command for this work.
- Human text output should summarize the same recovery facts in readable sections.
- Lint no-op must stop looking like equivalent evidence to an enforced lint pass.
- For lint no-op, use a stable machine-readable distinction such as status=passed_noop or enforced=false plus command_count=0 and evidence_strength=none/scaffold-only.
- Human text must not say lint passed when zero executable lint commands were found.
- Do not implement language-specific lint presets or generated lint commands here; that broader feature is tracked separately in GitHub issue #17.
- Document the result contract so future commands do not invent incompatible JSON shapes.

Acceptance criteria:
- setup --json includes exact next_steps for common blockers including missing git repo, hook path unavailable, missing br, missing burpvalve on PATH, and optional NTM unavailable/conflict.
- init --json can report core scaffold created plus optional integration conflict without making agents guess whether the repo is usable.
- lint --json distinguishes enforced pass from no executable commands declared, using a stable machine-readable field.
- lint human output clearly states when no lint command is configured and does not present that state as proof that lint passed.
- commit blocked reports include a recovery hint when failure is caused by verifier policy, missing responses, missing staged attestation, stale hashes, or ambiguous feature detection.
- Human output mirrors the JSON contract without becoming noisy.
- Existing tests are updated and new table tests cover at least one fatal, one advisory, one partial-success, one enforced lint pass, one enforced lint fail, and one lint no-op case.
- README or docs describe the contract fields and examples.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented structured recovery fields across setup/init/repair/lint/commit surfaces. Evidence: setup reports schema_version/command/status/fatal/partial_success/structured next_steps; init/repair report partial_success and conflict next_steps; lint exposes enforced, command_count, evidence_strength, enforcement_level, next_steps and no-op human output says not enforced; commit reports recovery next_steps for feature detection, missing/invalid verifier responses, filesystem write errors, and staging generated attestations. Docs/result-contract.md added and indexed. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; read-only verifier Kuhn reported PASS after recheck.

title: GH-2 validate lint_commands manifest schema before execution
description: Fix GitHub #2 by validating backpressure/manifest.yaml lint_commands before execution. Cover scalar, number, null, wrong field types, missing fields, duplicate ids, absolute/empty paths, and bad timeout. Errors should name the manifest path and field, populate blocked status/message, and avoid empty JSON output.
close_reason: Implemented lint_commands YAML shape/type validation and blocked lint JSON for malformed manifests. Verified scalar, number, null, wrong field types, missing fields, duplicate ids, bad paths/timeouts via tests and malformed temp-repo smoke.

title: vorch: 07 verifier doctor report-only
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 4 adds `burpvalve verifier doctor`, a report-only runtime limits diagnostic. It helps agents understand known runtime subagent limits/depth without making Burpvalve mutate Claude/Codex/NTM configuration.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: `verifier doctor` command, flags, result-contract JSON, human output, robot help.
- `cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go`: doctor CLI tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: doctor robot/help assertions if separate.
- Optional new helper file such as `internal/backpressure/verifier_doctor.go` or `internal/runtime/doctor.go` only if the implementation needs a testable parser boundary; include matching tests if created.

Binding behavior:
- Command name is `verifier doctor`, not folded into `setup`.
- It is read-only and never writes, repairs, or stages files.
- Fixed report checklist: presence/paths of known runtime config files (Claude Code settings JSON, Codex config, NTM config), subagent limit/depth values from formats it recognizes, and `unknown` for anything it cannot parse.
- Unknown/unrecognized formats produce report entries with `supported: false`; the command must not invent exact manual changes for unknown formats.
- JSON output follows the result contract and includes `next_steps`.
- Mutation, if ever added, is a separate later increment behind explicit confirmation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Doctor report names checked paths and returns recognized values where parser support exists.
- Unknown config content yields `supported: false` without claiming unsupported instructions.
- A no-write test snapshots candidate files before/after and proves no content, mode, or mtime changes where practical.
- Human and robot outputs state report-only behavior.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'VerifierDoctor|Doctor|Runtime|ReadOnly|Robots|Help'`
- `go test ./internal/... -run 'Doctor|Runtime|ReadOnly'` if helper package/files are introduced.
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve verifier doctor --json`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.
close_reason: Implemented in 908cfa1; verifier doctor report-only command landed with audit-ready no-write coverage.

title: osl: add SECURITY.md and private vulnerability reporting
description:
Background:
A public security-sensitive commit gate needs a private vulnerability reporting path. The plan requires GitHub private vulnerability reporting as the primary channel if available.

Scope:
- Enable GitHub private vulnerability reporting for the repo.
- Add `SECURITY.md` naming GitHub private vulnerability reporting as the channel.
- If GitHub private vulnerability reporting is unavailable, use a dedicated owner-confirmed security email.
- Keep policy terse and real; do not add broad unsupported SLA claims.

Acceptance criteria:
- `SECURITY.md` exists.
- It names a working private reporting channel.
- GitHub repo settings have private vulnerability reporting enabled, or fallback email is confirmed.
- Public README links or points to SECURITY.md where appropriate.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Completed by commit b10ae74 (pushed): added SECURITY.md with GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting channel and owner-ruled visibility-flip deferral, plus preflip gate acceptance requiring direct PVR evidence before visibility flip; attestation backpressure/attestations/9ef1b661470a7bd9c0b8dbf52a9c7c8975b23b6f5747b680eb6b0de3659952b0.json; verifier evidence BrightOwl Agent Mail 3260 and ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3259.

title: cos: promote orchestration throughput doctrine into templates and prompt bank
description:
Promote the orchestration throughput doctrine proven during the July 3 multi-agent run into the generated surfaces future orchestrators inherit.

Background and reasoning:
- During the COS/IFR/OSL/lexicon queue, the fleet proved a faster and safer pattern: verify early against a hash-bound parked slice, then use the gate window only for mechanics: step-zero index check, stage, confirm hash, run the Burpvalve gate, commit, push, close, release, and wake the next pane.
- Future orchestrators should not rediscover this in chat. The doctrine belongs in scaffold templates, prompt-bank entries, and the Claude orchestrator skill package, so new coordinators inherit the same operating contract.
- This bead intentionally promotes procedure only. It must not change gate semantics, verifier schema, CLI behavior beyond prompt-bank content, release behavior, or task ownership rules.

Exact payload file list:
- `templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl`
- `internal/scaffold/templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl`
- `internal/backpressure/prompt_bank.go`
- `internal/backpressure/prompt_bank_test.go`
- `templates/claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/SKILL.md.tmpl` and/or `templates/claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/references/*.md` where the generated skill's orchestrator procedure is stored
- matching embedded copies under `internal/scaffold/templates/claude/skills/burpvalve-orchestrator/**`
- `internal/scaffold/template_contract_test.go` or adjacent scaffold tests, only as needed to lock the new template/skill doctrine

Required implementation details:
1. `ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl` gains a `Throughput` section that explains:
   - monitor HEAD advancement and queue transitions; do not leave agents parked after their dependencies land;
   - include current queue order in every release wake and handoff message;
   - release wake is authorization for the next queued agent to take the window after a step-zero index check; if the pane ahead is silent for a full tick after release, the next ready pane may self-promote according to the recorded queue;
   - verify-early/gate-fast choreography: packets go out at park time, bound to a staged-payload hash; the gate window is held only for mechanics: confirm index empty, stage, confirm hash equals verified hash, run gate, commit, push, close, release;
   - if hash changes at staging, send a superseding packet instead of forcing stale evidence.
2. `commit-choreography` prompt-bank entry gains a verify-early variant, preserving the stable prompt id and exported prompt protection. It should describe packet-at-park-time, hash-bound verification, and the final gate-window mechanics.
3. Add a new prompt-bank entry named exactly `gate-operator-brief` for the minimal-context gate operator pattern:
   - role-scoped brief;
   - never read or rely on `CLAUDE.md` as the full project contract;
   - never claim implementation work;
   - do mechanics only: check index, stage the named slice or attestation, run the exact gate command, commit/push only when instructed;
   - escalate-don't-judge: if hashes differ, tests fail, verifier evidence disagrees, or scope is unclear, stop and escalate rather than deciding product merit.
4. The generated Claude orchestrator skill references gain the same doctrine where apt, without bloating `SKILL.md` beyond routing/procedure expectations. Put larger doctrine text into `references/` if the package already has that layout.
5. Keep canonical template/export protection intact: root templates and embedded `internal/scaffold/templates/**` copies must match for mirrored files; prompt-bank tests must account for the new stable prompt id without renaming existing ids.

Acceptance criteria:
- The generated ORCHESTRATOR template contains the Throughput section and all listed doctrine points.
- `burpvalve prompts list` includes `gate-operator-brief` and existing prompt ids remain stable.
- `burpvalve prompts show commit-choreography ...` contains the verify-early/gate-fast variant without removing the existing numbered commit flow.
- `burpvalve prompts show gate-operator-brief ...` renders a useful minimal-context operator brief with the role-scope, no-CLAUDE.md, no-claim, and escalate-don't-judge rules.
- Generated Claude orchestrator skill content references the throughput doctrine and gate-operator pattern in the right reference/procedure layer.
- Template mirror checks and prompt-bank export protection tests pass.
- No unrelated dirty work, parked peer slice, `.beads` catch-up, failed logs, or packet files are staged in the final payload.

Test commands:
- `git diff --check --cached`
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'PromptBank|PromptExport' -count=1`
- `go test ./internal/scaffold -run 'Template|Orchestrator|Claude|Skill|Prompt|Throughput|GateOperator' -count=1`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve prompts list --json`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve prompts show commit-choreography --var bead=project-readiness-kit-cos-throughput-doctrine --var feature=project-readiness-kit-cos-throughput-doctrine`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve prompts show gate-operator-brief --var operator=Spark --var queue='EmeraldBrook -> LilacGlacier -> WhiteGorge' --var feature=project-readiness-kit-cos-throughput-doctrine`
- If the slice changes generated skill package files, also run the relevant scaffold package validation tests already present for the Claude orchestrator skill.

Gate execution contract:
- Reserve every touched path via Agent Mail before editing.
- Before staging, write a patch backup to `<scrubbed-temp-path> and announce it.
- Verify early: briefly stage only this slice or use a temp worktree to compute the Burpvalve staged hash, send verifier packets at park time, and wake the assigned verifier panes. Do not hold `GATE-WINDOW` while waiting for verdicts.
- When the queue reaches this bead, acquire `GATE-WINDOW`, run the step-zero `git diff --cached --quiet` check before staging, stage only the payload and then only the generated attestation, confirm the staged hash equals the verifier-bound hash, run the Burpvalve gate with `BURPVALVE_FEATURE` and `BURPVALVE_BEAD` set to this bead id, commit, push `origin main` exactly once immediately after the gated commit, close the bead with commit/attestation/verifier refs, `br sync --flush-only`, release reservations, and wake the next queued pane.
- Never use `--no-verify`, never fabricate verifier evidence, never stash/reset/checkout over peer dirt, and never include peer parked changes in the payload.
close_reason: Completed in commit 7b3f761 (cos: document throughput doctrine), pushed to origin/main. Attestation backpressure/attestations/8bb402383e9676f960cb6aaa400fc9410fed9b8ccdd87d02179fcd1e5c2684aa.json for staged hash sha256:8bb402383e9676f960cb6aaa400fc9410fed9b8ccdd87d02179fcd1e5c2684aa. Verification: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3515 (judgment/high cells, patch-isolated hash reproduction), ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3512 (mechanical/shared cells), IndigoBridge Agent Mail 3514 standby gate-operator pilot brief. Gate output passed; lint remains not_enforced because no executable lint_commands are configured.
