Prompt-bank-only drafting is allowed now because it does not overlap j0h0. Rebase template doctrine edits on landed j0h0 text before verification/gate.

title: ifr2-D: carry supplemental verifiers and adjudication through attestations
description:
Background:
This bead implements Unit D from plans/issue-followups-round-2.md. It covers issue draft 9 and dogfooding finding A17. A17 showed that the committed artifact should preserve the real verification process when primary verifiers, supplemental verifiers, and an adjudicator/ruling all contributed to trust.

Binding contract:
- Response files and passing attestations preserve one primary verifier result, zero or more supplemental_verifiers, and optional adjudication with authority, summary, final verdict when applicable, and audit reference.
- Existing single-verifier artifacts remain valid. Query/explain/doctor surfaces must not reject older artifacts because optional fields are absent.
- verifier submit supports primary-only, supplemental-only, and adjudication-only submission shapes. Supplemental-only may record non-primary evidence; adjudication-only may resolve an already-recorded disagreement.
- Merge rules are deterministic: duplicate supplemental verifier keys replace prior entries with a warning; adjudication replaces prior adjudication with a warning; primary verdict replacement retains supplemental/adjudication data unless a future explicit clear flag exists.
- Owner/FuchsiaBear ruling: adjudication.final_verdict is audit metadata only. It must never turn a primary fail or unknown condition into a passing artifact. Override semantics are future work requiring a separate authority-validation design.
- Stop rule: reuse existing SupplementalVerifier and ResponseAdjudication structs/submit-flow code from internal/backpressure/verifier_responses.go. Do not duplicate schema types.

Likely file touches:
- internal/attestations/attestations.go
- internal/attestations/attestations_test.go
- internal/backpressure/verifier_responses.go
- internal/backpressure/verifier_responses_test.go
- internal/backpressure/artifacts.go
- internal/backpressure/artifacts_test.go
- internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts.go
- internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/attestations_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/explain_test.go
- docs/attestation-schema.md
- docs/result-contract.md

Tests required:
- go test ./internal/attestations -run "Supplemental|Adjudication|Schema|Validate|Legacy"
- go test ./internal/backpressure -run "VerifierSubmit|Supplemental|Adjudication|Artifact|Blocked|Passing"
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run "Verifier|Attestations|Explain|Supplemental|Adjudication|Robots|Help"
- go test ./...

Required scenarios:
- legacy single-verifier artifact validates shape;
- primary plus supplemental verifier survives verifier submit merge and burpvalve commit artifact writing;
- supplemental-only submit adds evidence without overwriting primary;
- duplicate supplemental verifier replaces with warning;
- adjudication-only submit records authority/summary/audit ref;
- primary fail or unknown plus adjudication final_verdict pass still blocks passing artifact validation;
- disagreement case is representable and queryable;
- query/explain output displays supplemental disagreement and instructs hold/escalate rather than hiding it behind the primary verdict;
- legacy single-verifier artifacts remain readable/queryable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Response files, passing attestations, docs, and query/explain surfaces preserve supplemental verifier and adjudication metadata.
- Legacy artifacts remain compatible.
- Adjudication is clearly documented as audit metadata, not acceptance override.
- Existing submit structs are reused instead of parallel schema definitions.

Non-goals:
- No override authority model.
- No replacement of primary-verifier policy.
- No ownership accounting helper.
- No broad verifier packet path accounting beyond depending on the Unit B contract.
close_reason: Implemented supplemental verifiers and adjudication through response files, passing attestations, query/list/explain surfaces, docs, and compatibility tests. Commit abdae36; attestation backpressure/attestations/6a9ca468b8f4b8f262a4ae9808e8d2a85cc1a03f50f50a1d669d5f616d68ac9d.json; verifier packets/replies Agent Mail 3212/3213/3218/3220; gate passed with BURPVALVE_FEATURE=project-readiness-kit-ifr2-d-supplemental-adjudication-schema-1ur.

title: cos: land approved Claude orchestrator skill plan and bead graph
description:
Background:
This is the administrative/docs bead for the owner-approved Claude orchestrator skill plan. The owner approved plans/claude-orchestrator-skill.md after BrightOwl's second-pass review (Agent Mail 3054) with two promoted binding polish items: write/execute helper scripts must be opt-in with dry-run as default, and pane_wake.py must perform its own ntm --robot-capabilities plus fresh ntm --robot-snapshot preflight.

Purpose:
Land the ruled plan and the bead conversion itself as a gated docs/tracker commit before implementation starts. This bead does not implement the feature; it makes the plan and graph durable.

Exact payload file list:
- plans/claude-orchestrator-skill.md
- .beads/issues.jsonl
- backpressure/attestations/<gate-hash>.json generated by Burpvalve for this docs/tracker commit

Required work:
- Ensure plans/claude-orchestrator-skill.md contains the owner ruling: dry-run default and explicit --write/--execute for helper mutation; pane_wake.py invokes ntm --robot-capabilities and fresh ntm --robot-snapshot itself.
- Convert the approved six-unit implementation plan into self-contained beads with dependency structure.
- Run br dep cycles and confirm no cycles.
- Send RusticDog the bead list for quality pass before implementation assignment.
- Land only the plan plus tracker export and the generated attestation.

Acceptance criteria:
- The plan is present and matches the approved route/package contract.
- The cos: implementation beads exist and are dependency-structured.
- br dep cycles reports no dependency cycles.
- RusticDog receives the bead list and dependency summary by Agent Mail.
- The commit payload contains no source implementation changes.

Test commands:
- rg -n 'Dry-run is the default|--write|--execute|pane_wake.py|ntm --robot-capabilities|ntm --robot-snapshot' plans/claude-orchestrator-skill.md
- br list --json --limit 200 | jq '.issues[] | select(.title|startswith("cos:")) | {id,title,status,priority}'
- br dep cycles
- git diff --cached --name-status

Non-goals:
- Do not implement config, scaffold, CLI, TUI, or generated skill changes in this bead.
- Do not assign implementation beads until after the quality pass.\n\nGate and coordination contract for this bead:\n- Claim with br update --status in_progress before implementation.\n- Reserve every touched file with Agent Mail using the bead id as the reason.\n- Stage only this bead payload; before acquiring GATE-WINDOW confirm the index is empty with git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Verify the staged slice in isolation using the repo standard stash --keep-index method when source files are touched.\n- Generate Burpvalve verifier packets from the final staged payload, collect honest hash-bound verifier responses, and hold on disagreement or blockers.\n- Commit through the live Burpvalve hook from source; never use --no-verify and never fabricate evidence.\n- Stage only the attestation file named by the gate, rerun the commit, then push origin main exactly once after the successful gated commit if this repo protocol still requires per-commit push.\n- Close the bead with commit and attestation references, then br sync --flush-only.
close_reason: Completed in commit 863aa43 with attestation backpressure/attestations/99ec6ca50c010a36be44de3e35379694daa65e1362331daa35ec6c6120ef179d.json; verifier evidence BrightOwl Agent Mail 3163 and ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3162/3164; scoped .beads payload fixed after ScarletMarsh scope-control catch.

title: osl: decide license and NOTICE policy second
description:
Owner decision required SECOND, after repo home.

Question:
Choose the open-source license for Burpvalve. Recommendation: Apache-2.0 for explicit patent grant and Go tooling norms. Alternative: MIT for simpler terms.

Also decide NOTICE policy:
If Apache-2.0 is chosen, default is no NOTICE file unless attribution/dependency review requires one. Record the NOTICE decision explicitly.

Acceptance criteria:
- License choice is recorded: Apache-2.0 or MIT.
- NOTICE choice is recorded if Apache-2.0 is selected.
- Decision doc or close reason names why the selected license fits the no-outside-contributions policy.
- License-dependent packaging and metadata beads are unblocked.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.

Cross-review polish binding clarification:
This bead is an OWNER decision, not an implementer task. The owner must choose both the license and, when relevant, the NOTICE policy before license-dependent packaging/metadata work proceeds.

Decision options:
- Apache-2.0 with no NOTICE file unless dependency/attribution review requires one.
- Apache-2.0 with an explicit NOTICE file, if the owner wants a NOTICE even without a dependency-driven requirement.
- MIT, with no NOTICE file unless dependency/attribution review requires one.

Recommendation:
Choose Apache-2.0 with no NOTICE file unless the dependency/license review finds an attribution requirement. Apache-2.0 gives an explicit patent grant and fits Go tooling norms while preserving the no-outside-contributions policy as a maintainer workflow rule rather than a license restriction.
notes: D-OSL-6 amendment: MIT selection from D-OSL-2 stands, but contribution-aversion/no-outside-contributions is no longer part of the rationale. Owner is not against outside contributions. Dependency-review result remains separate: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES is required for dependency attribution/NOTICE obligations.
close_reason: Owner ruling recorded in docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md D-OSL-2: license is MIT, chosen over Apache-2.0; no Apache NOTICE question applies. Recording only; no license files or launch changes executed.

title: oxp: implement ci --commit commit-scoped attestation audit
description:
Background:
Orchestrators need to audit evidence for an arbitrary commit regardless of current tree state. Current CI behavior validates staged state or falls back to the latest commit; that is not enough for post-hoc orchestration, rollback review, or release audit.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"`ci --commit` CLI contract: new `--commit <sha>` flag on `ci`, `robotCIInput` extended to accept it, help + robot docs updated, and JSON output includes artifact path(s) and per-condition provenance (satisfying the audit-discovery need without a separate command for now). With `--commit`, feature identity defaults to artifact-bound discovery from that commit; `--feature` becomes an extra assertion that must match, not a selector."

Implementation boundary from approved plan:
- Current `internal/backpressure/ci.go` validates staged state or falls back to latest commit only.
- Current `GitCommittedReader` is hard-coded to `HEAD^`/`HEAD` plus `git show HEAD:<path>`.
- This feature requires a commit-parametrized reader: `<sha>^..<sha>` diff and `git show <sha>:<path>` content.
- Root commit handling must be explicit.
- Merge commit parent policy is first parent.
- Artifact lookup comes from that commit's tree.

Absorb rule:
Before implementing, check whether any part of this evidence surface already landed in another bead or current-round commit. Absorb already-landed fixes by documenting/using them; do not duplicate commands, readers, flags, or tests.

Scope:
- Add `--commit <sha>` to `burpvalve ci`.
- Extend robot input and robot/help docs to include commit.
- Add commit-parametrized reader and tests for normal commit, root commit, merge commit first-parent policy, delete/rename handling, and missing artifact.
- JSON output includes artifact path(s), attestation provenance, and per-condition provenance sufficient for audit.
- With `--commit`, derive feature identity from the artifact binding by default; `--feature` only asserts that the discovered feature matches.
- Keep existing staged/latest behavior intact when `--commit` is absent.

Acceptance criteria:
- `burpvalve ci --commit <sha> --json` validates the evidence stored in that commit, not the current index or HEAD unless that is the target SHA.
- `--feature` mismatch under `--commit` fails clearly as assertion mismatch.
- JSON output can answer "what evidence did this commit carry" without adding `attestations list --commit` in this increment.
- Root and merge commits are deterministic.
- Existing CI tests still pass.

Tests:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'CI|Commit|CommittedReader|Root|Merge|Artifact|FeatureAssertion|Rename|Delete'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'CI|Commit|Robots|Help|FeatureAssertion|JSON'`
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented and pushed as 0c41470 (oxp: add commit-scoped ci audit).

title: lint: final acceptance coverage for issue-17 first increment
description:
Background:
This final gate confirms the issue-17 first increment is complete and that every acceptance criterion from both plan acceptance lists landed in a bead and implementation evidence. It intentionally does not require the p4 deferred beads to be implemented.

Scope:
- Run a coverage audit against the two acceptance lists in `plans/issue-17-guided-lint-setup.md`.
- Confirm tests cover single-language repo, polyglot repo, declined scoped setup, accepted scoped setup, unknown keys, run_directory cases, legacy `cd`, detect-never-mutates, idempotency, concurrency, and partial coverage output.
- Confirm docs/help/robot surfaces explain `run_directory`, repo-root-relative paths, coverage partial semantics, structural recommendations, presets, and `--jobs`/`serial` scheduling.
- Confirm `go test ./...` passes.
- Confirm `br dep cycles` returns empty and `br sync --flush-only` has been run.

Acceptance coverage checklist:
- Wizard asks scoped-subdirectory question for polyglot/multi-root repos.
- Wizard skips subdirectory questions for single-root/non-polyglot or declined single-root setup.
- Manifest entries explicit, readable, removable.
- `run_directory` validated inside target repo.
- No dependency/script/config install/edit without explicit confirmation.
- No enforcement claimed for wishlist prose.
- No-op/advisory/enforced states distinct.
- Robot output exposes detection facts and proposed manifest diffs.
- Tests cover single-language, polyglot, declined scoped setup, accepted scoped setup.
- Unknown per-command keys rejected.
- `run_directory` positive/negative cases covered.
- Legacy `cd` support unchanged.
- `--detect` never mutates.
- Wizard rerun idempotency zero diff.
- Concurrency tests from plan pass, including `--jobs 1` equivalence.
- `coverage: partial` appears in JSON and human output while status/enforced unaffected.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Final issue-17 acceptance co-signed by EmeraldBrook and WhiteGorge (Agent Mail 2749). Evidence: clean HEAD 6663ffd focused lint acceptance suites passed, go test ./... passed, CLI fixture checks verified single-root, polyglot scoped setup, partial coverage JSON/human semantics, no fake enforcement, help/robot surfaces, br dep cycles clean, br sync --flush-only clean.

title: Make generated attestation JSON formatter-safe
description:
Background:
GitHub issue #10 showed a repo format check failing on generated Burpvalve attestation JSON. Attestations are evidence artifacts, so they must be readable, stable, and hard to accidentally break with broad formatter commands. The approved scope is to fix the generated output and documentation now, while keeping automatic formatter configuration edits visible and opt-in.

Goal:
Generated attestation JSON should be formatter-safe by default and should not surprise either humans or agents reviewing commit evidence.

Scope:
- Emit stable, pretty-printed JSON for all generated attestation/report artifacts under backpressure/attestations and log/backpressure/failed.
- Ensure generated JSON ends with exactly one trailing newline.
- Keep the schema stable enough for agents to parse without normalizing whitespace themselves.
- Prefer structs or stable marshal paths over arbitrary map output for generated evidence where ordering matters to tests/readability.
- Document that attestation JSON is generated evidence and may be ignored by broad source formatters if a project formatter cannot tolerate generated evidence files.
- Do not silently edit .prettierignore, formatter configs, or repository policy files.
- If formatter-ignore editing is added later, it must be a visible prompt/flag with clear output describing the file being changed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Passing attestation JSON and blocked/failure attestation JSON are emitted with deterministic indentation, stable top-level field order where the current Go representation permits it, and exactly one trailing newline.
- Tests assert generated JSON is valid, pretty-printed, and newline-terminated for both passing and blocked artifacts.
- README or docs explain how generated attestations interact with repo formatters without implying users must ignore them.
- No command mutates formatter config unless the user explicitly opts in.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented formatter-safe generated attestation JSON. Evidence: writeArtifact uses json.MarshalIndent and writes exactly one trailing newline; passing and blocked artifact tests assert valid indented newline-terminated JSON; docs and scaffold templates explain generated evidence vs broad formatters; no formatter config mutation was added. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; read-only verifier subagent Hubble reported PASS and its generated-scaffold caveat was resolved.

title: Generate verifier handoff prompts for subagent workflows
description:
Background:
Issue #13 asks for deeper subagent spawn context and prompt guidance. Burpvalve should not spawn native subagents in runtimes where it has no control, but it can generate precise, policy-aware handoff prompts and NTM/native/manual guidance.

Goal:
Provide verifier handoff recipes and prompt generation that tell the main agent exactly what verifier cells to delegate and what evidence each verifier must return.

Scope:
- Primary command shape should be one clear surface, preferably burpvalve verifier prompts, with JSON and human output. Avoid adding multiple overlapping shortcuts unless tests prove they reduce confusion.
- Prompt generation should work from the current staged payload and manifest conditions; optional flags may filter by --feature or --condition.
- Prompts must include feature id, staged paths/statuses, condition file, success criteria, allowed tools/read-only expectation, response schema, verifier policy, and prohibition on fabricated confirmation.
- Include the approved authorization language exactly where relevant: User authorized Burpvalve requesting subagents. Proof demonstrated by installation of Burpvalve. When Burpvalve requires verifier cells, the agent is expected to spawn read-only verifier subagents for the current staged feature/condition cells when its runtime permits repo-level authorization. Do not fabricate subagent confirmation.
- Support lower-case output profiles such as native, ntm, hermes, and manual, but each profile must say what Burpvalve is and is not doing. Do not claim to spawn native subagents unless the runtime integration actually exists.
- For NTM profile, output NTM-safe task briefs or commands that preserve per-cell evidence and do not launch a swarm implicitly.
- Use staged path status metadata so deletes/renames are represented correctly.
- Prefer short, copyable verifier packets over long essays; include JSON output for agents that can route prompts programmatically.
- Generated prompt packets should map cleanly back into verifier metadata fields accepted by the attestation schema.

Acceptance criteria:
- Prompt output is available as human text and JSON.
- Prompt generation respects manifest verifier policy from the verifier provenance bead.
- Tests cover profile selection, unsupported profile, missing feature, no staged payload, staged path inclusion, deleted path inclusion, renamed path inclusion, condition inclusion, read-only instruction text, generated response schema, and exact approved authorization language.
- Skill instructions are updated to tell agents to use generated verifier prompts when available.
- go test ./... passes.
design:
Plan audit revision:
- Treat this as a read-only prompt-packet generator, not a launcher. It may produce native/ntm/hermes/manual instructions, but it must not claim it spawned anything.
- Keep the output small enough to paste into a verifier subagent while still including staged path status, condition text/path, policy, authorization text, and response schema.
- JSON output is the primary agent contract; human output should be copyable and styled, not essay-like.
- If no staged payload or an ambiguous feature split exists, fail with structured recovery that tells the main agent exactly what to stage or which --feature to pass.
- The response_schema emitted here should line up with the commit responses schema so agents can round-trip verifier output into burpvalve commit.
close_reason: Implemented burpvalve verifier prompts as a read-only handoff packet generator with human and JSON output, profile notes for native/ntm/hermes/manual, manifest verifier_policy propagation, staged path status including deletes/renames, response_schema compatible with commit responses, robot help, and skill instructions. Evidence: go test ./internal/backpressure ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'TestBuildVerifierPrompts|TestVerifierPrompts' -count=1 -v passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed. Read-only verifier Carson initially found the exact-language test was too weak; after adding an independent literal assertion, Carson rechecked and reported PASS.

title: PRS-05 Implement NTM registration and verification
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-05 and NTM Registration. Add NTM readiness verification without overclaiming registration. Files/patterns: internal/ntm/, internal/scaffold/ntm*.go, templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl. Behavior: call ntm --robot-capabilities before state-changing NTM commands; document repo basename as base session identity and -- labels for variants; support check mode that reports intended ntm quick <basename>; after any state-changing action, verify with ntm --robot-snapshot and include attention/tail evidence when available. Missing NTM should produce a clear optional blocker/follow-up note, not fake readiness.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: tests with fake NTM runner cover unavailable NTM, capabilities success, quick intent, quick execution, snapshot verification, and failed snapshot blocker; AGENTS.md template includes exact NTM session naming text from GOAL.md; no NTM state transition is treated complete without snapshot evidence; go test ./... passes; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.
close_reason: Implemented NTM check/quick/snapshot verification with tests; verifier subagent Gauss approved with no gaps.

title: Handle staged deletes and renames in payload detection
description:
Background:
GitHub issue #15 reports that staged deleted paths can block commit before response evaluation because Burpvalve attempts to read staged contents for deleted files. Normal migrations, renames, and refactors frequently stage deletes. Prompt generation and verifier handoffs also need the same status-aware view of the staged payload.

Goal:
Replace the current path-only staged payload reader with a status-aware staged payload engine that understands added, modified, deleted, renamed, copied, and type-changed paths.

Scope:
- Use git diff --cached --name-status -z or equivalent porcelain-safe parsing to collect status metadata.
- Do not call git show :path for deleted paths.
- For renames, hash the new staged blob when present and include old path as metadata.
- For deletes, include stable deletion metadata in payload hash so deleted-path commits remain bound to evidence.
- Explicit --feature should avoid unnecessary content-derived inference, but payload hashing still must include deletion/rename metadata.
- Update Plan JSON and any verifier prompt payload context to expose staged path status in a backward-compatible way.
- Treat filenames with spaces, newlines, tabs, and leading dashes as first-class test cases.

Acceptance criteria:
- Regression test stages a deleted file and valid --feature/--responses; commit reaches response validation instead of failing on git show :deleted-path.
- Regression test covers rename old -> new and proves hash stability.
- Regression test covers path names with spaces or shell-sensitive characters.
- Existing payload hash exclusion behavior for generated attestation and blocked report paths remains intact.
- CI committed-reader path handles deletes/renames for committed payload validation if applicable.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented status-aware staged payload handling. Evidence: GitStagedReader and GitCommittedReader use name-status -M -z; deletes skip staged content reads while contributing metadata; renames include old_path/status/git_status plus new content; Plan keeps staged_payload_paths and adds staged_payload_files; tests cover real staged deletion CLI flow, real git mv hash stability, leading-dash/tab/newline paths, generated artifact exclusion, and committed delete/rename reader. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; read-only verifier Newton reported PASS after recheck.

title: lint: implement guided lint init wizard flow
description:
Background:
The human wizard is the main UX for `burpvalve lint init`. It must make scope obvious when scope matters and stay quiet when it does not. It must also make reduced coverage durable rather than silently treating a declined scoped setup as full coverage.

Scope:
- Build the interactive `burpvalve lint init` flow over the Unit A-E model.
- Show detected roots, candidate commands, existing manifest lint commands, selected commands, run directories, scope paths, required/advisory status, timeout values, exact manifest diff, and any recommendation diff.
- Ask the polyglot/scoped setup question when detection finds multiple roots. Recommended default is Yes.
- If user declines scoped setup, stop asking subdirectory-specific questions, propose only repo-root commands, warn with unchecked roots, and write `lint_coverage.declined_roots` plus `declined_at` only after confirmed write.
- If detection sees a single language/tool root, do not ask the scoped-subdirectory question by default.
- For each selected root/scope, ask required/advisory, timeout, scope paths, run directory, local/CI/both intent where represented, and serial scheduling when needed.
- Confirmation defaults to No before any manifest or recommendation write.
- Keep generated manifest entries explicit and removable.

Tests and commands:
- Add tests or scripted TUI/model tests for single-root flow, polyglot accepted scoped setup, polyglot declined scoped setup, confirmation default No, exact diff preview, and no mutation on cancel.
- Run `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*LintInitWizard|Test.*ScopedSetup|Test.*DeclinedScope|Test.*ConfirmDefaultNo' -count=1` or actual test names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- The wizard asks about scoped subdirectory commands when a repo appears polyglot or multi-root.
- The wizard skips subdirectory lint questions when the user chooses a single-root, non-polyglot setup.
- If user declines polyglot setup, wizard warns and records reduced coverage instead of blocking.
- Burpvalve never installs dependencies or edits project scripts/configs without explicit confirmation.
- Tests cover declined scoped setup and accepted scoped setup.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Implemented in 6663ffd lint: add guided lint init wizard

title: oxp: state the orchestrator no-self-execution role split in templates
description: CLAUDE.md.orchestrator.tmpl, ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl, and/or the burpvalve-orchestrator SKILL must state the hard role split: when the orchestrator route is active with worker agents available, the orchestrator does not implement, edit docs, run verifier cells, or execute payload work itself; scope is triage, decision capture, routing, review, gate operation, evidence recording. Workers are the implementers and checkers. Owner ruling 2026-07-05, mirrored in this repo's ORCHESTRATOR.md.

Verifier evidence: log/backpressure/responses/ad4cf8fd914494326457c1234d173f609fff796a519667151ce9b792de86d269.json with 10 pass and 3 not_applicable cells from GrayCat/CloudyEagle. go test ./... passed before gate; hook revalidated staged attestation.

title: installer: copy burpvalve binary into user bin instead of symlinking into skill dir
description:
Background:
The local install had ~/.local/bin on PATH, but ~/.local/bin/burpvalve was a symlink to <scrubbed-local-path> The skills tree was reorganized to <scrubbed-local-path>, leaving the user-bin command broken. This made burpvalve unavailable from other repos even though ~/.local/bin was on PATH.

Owner ruling:
The skill package may live elsewhere, but the executable itself should be installed in the user bin directory. The user bin command should not depend on a symlink into the skill tree.

Immediate local workaround already applied on 2026-07-06:
- Copied <scrubbed-local-path> to <scrubbed-local-path> as a real executable.
- Verified zsh and bash resolve <scrubbed-local-path>
- Verified burpvalve --version reports v0.2.1.

Scope for product fix:
- Update install.sh to install/copy the release binary into --bin-dir as an executable file instead of creating a symlink to skills_dir/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve.
- Keep --no-shims compatibility or rename the wording carefully if changing CLI text.
- Update install preview/output copy from command shim/symlink language to command executable language.
- Update skill/burpvalve/INSTALL.md and any README/release-install/docs references that say bin_dir/burpvalve is a symlink.
- Update cmd/burpvalve/install_script_test.go expectations so the bin command is a real executable and remains runnable after moving/removing the skill directory.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fresh install from archive leaves an executable file at <bin-dir>/burpvalve, not a symlink.
- <bin-dir>/burpvalve --version works even if the installed skill directory is moved after install.
- Installer still installs the skill package under skills_dir for agent skill usage.
- PATH verification checks command -v burpvalve points into bin_dir.
- Tests cover replacement of an existing stale symlink in bin_dir.
- Install docs no longer tell users the user-bin command is a symlink into the skill tree.
notes: Owner ruling 2026-07-08: the fix is not just copy-instead-of-symlink - the installer must ASK the user where their preferred skills directory is instead of assuming ~/skills. Human flow: interactive prompt with a sensible default; agent flow: --skills-dir flag and robots JSON field; persist the choice in config so upgrades reuse it without re-asking. Copy the binary into the user bin (no symlink into the skills tree) so later skills-dir reorganizations cannot break the command.
close_reason: Landed installer skills-dir/copy-not-symlink fix in ebf0c5a. Attestation: backpressure/attestations/924ed0b54232a769bd599c58c494672c15b0a43e0c404b17fa9c30e0936335d1.json. Verifiers: JadeCreek judgment cells; PurpleStream mechanical cells.

title: osl: independent re-verification of launch-window main-agent-attested commits
description: Owner ruling D-OSL-21 (2026-07-05): for the launch execution window, backpressure/manifest.yaml was relaxed to verifier_policy main_agent_allowed on all conditions so the remaining mechanical launch commits self-attest with truthful main_agent provenance. This bead is the compensating control: after the flip and anonymous verification complete, (1) revert the manifest to independent_required (or the pre-window per-condition mix), and (2) run independent codex-pane verification over every launch-window commit (from the manifest-relaxation commit through the flip), recording verdicts as supplemental verifier evidence or follow-up findings. Any finding is fix-forward on the public repo. Do not close until both the manifest revert and the retro-verification evidence exist.

No new finding beads required.

title: osl: run dependency license review
description:
Background:
Public binary distribution raises dependency-license hygiene expectations. The plan names Bubble Tea/Lip Gloss stack as MIT-family but requires checking the full graph.

Scope:
- Enumerate `go.mod` dependency licenses, for example with `go-licenses` or equivalent.
- Confirm all dependencies are compatible with the selected license and public binary distribution.
- Decide whether third-party notices are required in release artifacts.
- Feed NOTICE requirements back to the license packaging bead.

Suggested commands:
- `go list -m all`
- `go-licenses report ./cmd/burpvalve` or an equivalent dependency-license report command available in the environment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dependency-license report exists in docs or release/audit notes.
- Any notice obligations are explicitly handled.
- License packaging bead includes required third-party notices if needed.
- No incompatible dependency license is unresolved at launch.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Completed dependency license review; findings sent in Agent Mail 3116. No copyleft/incompatible licenses found; MIT-compatible, with third-party notices required for MIT/BSD/Apache dependency obligations including gopkg.in/yaml.v3 NOTICE.

title: Approved Burpvalve improvement program
description:
Background:
This is the umbrella tracking bead for the approved Burpvalve improvement recommendations from the June 28 ideation and GitHub issue review pass. The approved work includes improving setup rather than adding doctor, verifier provenance/policy, staged deletion/rename handling, bead delivery closure, explain, config foundation, guided config init, config-default consumption across commands, attestations TUI/JSON, install/completion verification, attestation JSON formatting hygiene, truthful lint no-op reporting, and an E2E harness plus approved scenarios. The broader guided lint setup/preset work is captured in GitHub issue #17 for discussion and is not part of this approved implementation program unless explicitly approved later.

Goal:
Coordinate the implementation sequence and close only when all child beads are complete, tested, documented, verified, and synced.

Preferred sequence:
- Finish foundations first: structured recovery, staged payload v2, formatter-safe JSON, git/hook ordering, config foundation, E2E harness, and verifier provenance. These are already closed unless reopened by a regression.
- Finish config-default consumption after guided config init so saved choices actually affect later commands without hiding explicit user intent.
- Verify install, PATH, and completion behavior next, using the shared config-default layer and preserving global-vs-project boundaries.
- Implement bead delivery metadata and dry-run preflight before attestation browsing, so bead IDs are stable and queryable before the TUI displays them.
- Put human/TUI surfaces on top of stable robot contracts: no prose scraping, no hidden mutation, and no state changes from read-only browsers.
- Run E2E scenarios after the relevant behavior exists, grouped by user workflow so failures are actionable.
- Do not expand into unapproved lint preset generation, registry integration, automatic bead mutation, automatic admin-only classification, or native subagent spawning unless the user approves that scope separately.

Acceptance criteria:
- All dependent beads are closed with evidence and verifier review where required by the project operating contract.
- br dep cycles reports no cycles.
- br list --json shows no unexpected open child work for this program.
- The limited GitHub issue #8 scope is satisfied by truthful no-op lint evidence; broad lint preset generation remains separate.
- GitHub issue #10 is satisfied by stable formatter-safe generated attestation JSON and documentation, without silent formatter config mutation.
- Delivery bead closure avoids attestation drift by running the final gate against the exact staged payload to be committed.
- go test ./... and make build pass after the final implementation.
- README, skill docs, command help, and relevant examples are updated for user-facing behavior.
design:
Plan audit revision 5:
- Active implementation order is now narrow: finish E2E bead delivery/attestation scenarios, then E2E explain/recovery scenarios, then close the E2E umbrella and finally this program umbrella.
- Closed foundation beads are treated as substrate, not future work: structured recovery, staged payload v2, formatter-safe JSON, git/hook ordering, config, setup readiness, install/completion verification, verifier provenance, verifier prompts, attestation query APIs, attestation TUI, and delivery metadata are already implemented unless a regression forces a reopen.
- Keep human/TUI surfaces on stable JSON contracts; tests should assert structured fields and only stable human substrings.
- Do not expand into lint preset generation, registry integration, automatic Beads mutation, automatic admin-only classification, native subagent spawning, or a separate doctor command unless separately approved.
- Setup remains the readiness surface. Explain remains the recovery translator. Attestations remain read-only evidence surfaces.
- Beads integration must remain optional at the product level: Beads-specific commands may require br, but setup, commit, lint, explain, and attestation queries must stay useful in projects without Beads.
- E2E tests should prove real user workflows, not every internal permutation. Edge matrices with fake br or synthetic fixtures belong in focused unit tests, with E2E covering the representative integration path.
- Every mutating flow must preview changes, ask for confirmation by default, and provide exact recovery commands when it stops.
- The final close gate is: child beads closed, br dep cycles --json empty, go test ./... passes, make build passes, git diff --check passes, docs/help are current, and a verifier subagent reviews the umbrella evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- All dependent beads are closed with evidence and verifier review where required by the project operating contract.
- br dep cycles --json reports no cycles.
- br list --json shows no unexpected open child work for this program.
- The limited GitHub issue #8 scope remains truthful no-op lint evidence; broad lint preset generation remains separate.
- GitHub issue #10 remains satisfied by stable formatter-safe generated attestation JSON and documentation, without silent formatter config mutation.
- Delivery bead closure avoids attestation drift by running the final gate against the exact staged payload to be committed.
- First-install and completion flows have tested preview/confirmation, PATH/source guidance, repo-local-vs-global distinction, and cancel/no-partial-write behavior.
- Core Burpvalve usage remains documented and tested for projects that do not use Beads.
- Remaining E2E child beads prove bead delivery/attestation workflows and explain/recovery workflows at user level, with exhaustive edge cases kept in lower-level tests where more deterministic.
- go test ./... and make build pass after the final implementation.
- README, skill docs, command help, and relevant examples are updated for user-facing behavior.
- Final verifier review passes before this umbrella bead is closed.
close_reason: Completed approved Burpvalve improvement program. All 17 dependencies are closed, including E2E umbrella with child beads .1-.4 closed and verified. Final implemented coverage includes setup readiness improvements, verifier provenance/policy, staged payload delete/rename handling, formatter-safe attestation JSON, config foundation/init/defaults, install/completion verification, attestation query/TUI surfaces, Beads delivery metadata/preflight, explain/recovery, hermetic no-br core E2E, truthful lint no-op reporting, stale attestation drift blocking, docs/help/skill updates, and rebuilt binary. Final gates before close: go test ./... -count=1, make build, git diff --check, br dep cycles --json all passed. Read-only final verifier Hilbert PASS.

title: lint: defer user-defined preset storage until config schema work
description:
Background:
Revision 2 resolves that first increment ships built-in presets only. User-defined preset storage in global config with per-project overrides is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields`; adding `defaults.lint_presets` requires real schema, merge, normalize, validate, and source-note work.

Deferred scope:
- Add a versioned config schema field such as `defaults.lint_presets` only in a later increment.
- Implement Merge/Normalize/Validate/source-note support for global presets and project overrides.
- Support import/export for user-defined lint presets after schema work exists.
- Applying a stored preset must still run the preview-diff-confirm flow and never auto-apply at init.

Acceptance criteria for future work:
- Unknown config fields remain rejected; no sidecar ad hoc preset file bypasses config validation.
- Global/project precedence is documented and exposed in robot output.
- User presets contain full command definitions and still require per-project confirmation.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.

title: osl: propagate author and timeline conduct rules to scaffold templates
description:
Background:
D-OSL-9 requires every Burpvalve-initialized repository to carry the no-AI-coauthorship instruction. D-LEX-8 also requires initialized repositories to carry the no-development-timeline-estimates instruction. The current repo records both rules in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, but generated scaffold templates need the same policy so future repos inherit it.

Scope:
- Add this exact authorship instruction, with owner-corrected author spelling, to scaffolded operating contracts: `When git committing, never add co-authorship for Codex or Claude (no Co-Authored-By trailers for AI tools). Author is clicksopendoors only.`
- Add this exact timeline instruction to scaffolded operating contracts: `Never estimate or reference development time or timelines. Describe scope, sequencing, and blockers instead.`
- Update `internal/scaffold/templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl`, `internal/scaffold/templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl`, top-level `templates/AGENTS.md.tmpl`, top-level `templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl`, and generated Claude orchestrator content where commit/gate choreography or agent conduct is documented.
- Coordinate template reservations with ScarletOwl/EmeraldBrook before editing; do not overwrite active lexicon/COS template slices.
- Add or update template contract tests so generated content includes both D-OSL-9 and D-LEX-8 rules.

title: lint: add built-in Go and Node Astro preset templates
description:
Background:
Revision 2 resolves preset design: presets store full command definitions as policy templates, but only built-in presets ship in the first increment. Applying a preset must still run preview/diff/confirmation; presets are never auto-applied at init.

Scope:
- Add built-in Go preset entries for `go test ./...`, `go vet ./...`, and gofmt check, using `run_directory` when scoped.
- Add built-in Node/Astro preset behavior that maps detected existing scripts to command definitions via the detected package manager.
- `--preset go` selects the built-in Go template; future `--preset node` or auto-proposed Node/Astro templates must still be derived from detected scripts/config.
- Preset entries carry full `lint_commands` definitions: id, command, required/advisory recommendation, paths, run_directory, timeout.
- Applying a preset always goes through the same preview-diff-and-confirm flow as manual selection.
- Do not store user-defined presets in config in this bead.

Tests and commands:
- Add tests for Go preset proposal, Node/Astro script-derived preset proposal, preview output, confirmation required, and no auto-apply during normal `burpvalve init`.
- Run `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*Preset|Test.*GoPreset|Test.*NodePreset' -count=1` or actual test names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- Built-in presets carry full command definitions.
- Presets require per-project confirmation.
- Command-ID-only storage is not used.
- Unconfirmed templates do not mutate the manifest.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Implemented in commit b8b39f8 (lint: add built-in preset commands); verifier passes in Agent Mail 2677 and 2682; gate attestation fbd60fb3f0c917394aeda0b7fb97b4b2d8a6bd139d6fda9aca8cc3a2681c6be1

title: lint: coordinate issue-17 guided lint setup
description:
Background:
This umbrella converts `plans/issue-17-guided-lint-setup.md` revision 2 into implementation work. Issue #17 asks Burpvalve to move from honest no-op lint reporting toward guided setup of real deterministic lint/static-analysis commands, without pretending prose recommendations are enforcement. The feature is scoped to first-increment Go and Node/Astro detection, first-class `run_directory`, durable reduced-coverage reporting, manifest writes with preview/confirmation, robot-safe flows, and bounded parallel `lint_commands` execution.

Goal:
Coordinate the full issue-17 lint setup work and close only after the first-increment chain is implemented, tested, documented, and the two explicit p4 deferrals are recorded. This umbrella intentionally does not depend on the deferred p4 items, because they are not first-increment deliverables.

Backbone:
- Unit A schema/runtime foundation blocks all implementation work.
- Unit B output vocabulary extends issue #8 truthfulness.
- Unit C manifest/recommendation writer makes safe writes possible.
- Unit D CLI/robot wiring exposes `burpvalve lint init` and flag behavior.
- Unit E detection bounds implements the first-increment detection surface.

Acceptance criteria:
- First-increment lint setup beads are complete and `br dep cycles` remains empty.
- Every acceptance criterion from the plan's two acceptance lists is covered by at least one child bead.
- `burpvalve lint init` can detect, preview, confirm, and write real manifest commands without overclaiming enforcement.
- `burpvalve lint` preserves honest no-op/advisory/enforced vocabulary and adds partial coverage visibility without changing status semantics.
- Deferred user presets and attestation scan concurrency are tracked as p4 deferred beads and do not block the first increment.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.

title: Add a TUI browser for attestations and blocked reports
description:
Background:
The user prefers a TUI for scanning many attestations because a raw CLI list can be annoying after many commits. The TUI should sit on top of the robot-safe attestation query APIs, bead metadata, and explain output rather than duplicating parsing logic.

Goal:
Add a read-only human-friendly attestation browser TUI.

Scope:
- burpvalve attestations with no subcommand opens a Bubble Tea TUI when interactive; burpvalve attestations browse is the explicit equivalent.
- Noninteractive use must print help or require list/show/browse subcommands; it must not hang.
- Show a searchable/filterable list of recent passing attestations and blocked reports using the attestation query API.
- Detail pane should show feature id, optional bead ids, payload hash, file path, pass/blocked status, condition verdicts, verifier metadata, evidence snippets, parse warnings, and next action for blocked cells.
- Include quick actions for showing the artifact path, printing the equivalent list/show JSON command, and showing the matching burpvalve explain command text when explain exists.
- Provide keyboard help, color, focus states, empty states, narrow-terminal behavior, and no-color mode that match existing Burpvalve style.
- Expected keys should include q/esc to quit, slash to search, arrows/j/k to move, tab to switch focus where relevant, enter to open details, and ? for help.
- The TUI must be read-only; do not add close/delete/repair/stage/commit actions in this bead.
design:
Plan audit revision 4:
- Build this only on top of attestation query APIs, bead metadata, and explain. The TUI should not parse artifact files itself.
- This is a browser, not an editor. It must not close beads, delete artifacts, repair files, stage files, commit files, or run commit gates.
- Optimize for scanning many artifacts: fast filtering, status/provenance visibility, bead IDs, parse warnings, payload hash, and exact equivalent CLI/JSON commands.
- The default interactive behavior is acceptable only when stdin/stdout are terminals; noninteractive runs must return help or a structured error and never hang.
- Show blocked reports and passing attestations in one list with clear status labels so users do not need to remember separate paths.
- Make the recovery path visible from the detail view: blocked cells should show why they blocked, what evidence is missing, and the equivalent burpvalve explain command.
- Prefer printable/showable paths and commands over OS clipboard integration. Clipboard support, if added later, must be optional and unneeded for tests.
- Rendering must use the same Lip Gloss/color/no-color conventions as the rest of Burpvalve and degrade cleanly at narrow widths.
- Include an explain action or copyable explain command for blocked items so scanning naturally leads to recovery.
- Rendering tests should cover color/no-color and narrow/wide widths to prevent overlap regressions.
- Keep agent ergonomics intact: every TUI view must have an equivalent JSON/list/show command documented so agents do not need to drive the TUI.
- The bead depends on delivery metadata so bead IDs are first-class in the browser rather than bolted on later.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- TUI model tests cover list rendering, selection movement, filtering, detail rendering, bead ID display, blocked report display, verifier provenance display, parse warning display, empty state, small terminal sizing, focus/keyboard help, and no-color mode.
- Rendering tests assert no obvious text overlap/truncation in common narrow and wide sizes, including long paths, long bead IDs, and missing optional metadata.
- Noninteractive invocation returns help or structured error quickly and does not start Bubble Tea.
- TUI actions are read-only in tests: no .beads mutation, no git index mutation, no artifact deletion, and no command execution beyond read-only query/explain data.
- Every TUI-visible operation has an equivalent documented CLI/JSON command for agents and scripts.
- A deterministic snapshot or VHS demo is added if existing docs demo tooling supports it without adding flaky CI requirements.
- README documents the TUI and JSON alternatives, including equivalent commands for agents and scripts.
- go test ./... and make build pass.
- A verifier subagent reviews this bead after implementation before it is closed.
close_reason: Implemented read-only attestation browser TUI. Evidence: attestations browse opens only interactively, noninteractive root prints help and browse exits with JSON/list guidance, model renders list/detail/filter/help/no-color/narrow/empty/warning states, detail shows show --json and explain commands, README documents human TUI plus agent JSON alternatives. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier Hooke reported PASS.

title: osl: audit full git history for secrets and old blobs
description:
Background:
The plan requires full git history scanning, including old blobs of committed binaries and release archives. Secrets/tokens/creds are always blockers under the proposed threshold.

Scope:
- Run a full history secret scan with gitleaks or equivalent.
- Ensure old blobs of `bin/burpvalve`, `dist/*.tar.gz`, and any deleted files are included.
- Triage every finding against owner thresholds.
- Record pass/fail and remediation decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- Full-history scan command and output summary are recorded.
- Every finding is classified: blocker, accepted history, or false positive.
- No secret/token/credential remains in publishable history.
- If a blocker exists, visibility flip is blocked until remediated or squash-relaunch is explicitly chosen.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Closed by owner ruling D-OSL-7 squash-relaunch: the public-history BLOCK is resolved by publishing a fresh public initial commit from a scrubbed current tree, while the private repo keeps full history and seal bindings. Remaining obligation is current-tree scrub before flip.

title: osl: decide launch announcement scope
description:
Background:
The launch plan lists announcement scope as an open owner question, but says it is out of tree and affects timing only. This is not one of the four P1 blocker decisions named for repo-home/license/history/old-releases, so it is tracked separately and should not block technical preparation.

Question:
Should launch be README-only quiet launch, or include a separate writeup/announcement outside the repo?

Acceptance criteria:
- Owner chooses quiet launch or writeup.
- If writeup: timing owner and external location/channel are known.
- Repo launch does not wait on marketing copy unless owner explicitly says it should.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Cross-review polish binding clarification:
This bead is an OWNER decision, not an implementer task. It controls external launch timing only and must not become hidden product or repo work.

Decision options:
- README-only quiet launch.
- Separate external writeup/announcement, with channel/location and timing owner named.
- Defer announcement until after public verification, with repo launch proceeding independently.

Recommendation:
Choose README-only quiet launch for the initial public flip. Treat any external writeup as follow-up unless the owner explicitly wants launch timing gated on it.
close_reason: Owner ruling recorded in docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md D-OSL-5 and D-OSL-6: quiet README-only launch; hard gate prohibits public launch or visibility flip until explicit owner permission; both local-only issue bundles denied for filing and remain informational. Recording only; no launch, visibility flip, deletion, or filing executed.

title: PRS-00 Bootstrap Go implementation repo and source layout
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-00. Create the durable implementation source repo in the current project root <scrubbed-local-repo-path> as a normal Go module, not a binary-only package. Scope is limited to the source skeleton and build/test substrate needed by later beads. Files/patterns: go.mod, Makefile, cmd/setup-command-project/, cmd/backpressure-commit/, internal/scaffold/, internal/attestations/, internal/gitindex/, internal/lintconfig/, internal/projectregistry/, internal/ntm/, templates/, fixtures/, docs/, plans/, skill/. Include build targets that compile setup-command-project and backpressure-commit with -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w" and enforce warning over 8 MiB plus failure over 12 MiB. Do not implement full scaffold, verifier, registry, or NTM behavior here beyond minimal command skeletons.

title: rel13: package and split-scope validate v0.1.3 locally
description:
Release v0.1.3 binding context:\n- Owner ordered this private release so the current machine can install current Burpvalve; repo visibility stays unchanged.\n- Model the flow on plans/release-v0.1.2.md and the closed rel bead chain, including annotated tag, tag-triggered release.yml, full asset verification, split-scope JSM package validation, and pinned post-release install smoke.\n- Release gates after dogfood mode lands; do not tag or release until the dogfood-mode bead is closed and included in changelog/version evidence.\n- Do not use workflow_dispatch. Push main only after accepted gated commits; push tag v0.1.3 only after the release-prep commit is on origin/main.\n- Do not commit dist archives or local binaries; GitHub release assets are canonical.\n- Keep license/visibility/launch metadata unchanged; open-source visibility flip remains out of scope.\n- Coordinate GATE-WINDOW before staging any commit; begin by proving git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Use version v0.1.3 everywhere in this chain.\n
Background:
Before a tag exists, v0.1.3 package validation must build from the current source tree into a temporary `DIST_DIR` and prove the archive contents independently. This inherits the v0.1.2 correction from ruling/A20: JSM validation applies to the skill package scope, while the embedded binary is validated separately.

Scope:
- Run `make build VERSION=v0.1.3` and confirm `./bin/burpvalve --version` reports v0.1.3.
- Run `tmp_dist=$(mktemp -d); VERSION=v0.1.3 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`.
- Run `make check-size`; if it warns above threshold, record the size in the v0.1.3 changelog or release evidence.
- Extract the platform archive from the temp dist directory.
- Validate archive completeness against checked-in `skill/burpvalve` plus `scripts/bin/burpvalve` plus `VERSION`.
- Apply the split-scope JSM validation pattern: temporarily move the embedded binary out of the extracted skill tree, run `jsm validate` on the remaining skill package, then restore/assert the binary separately.
- Assert extracted `VERSION` is exactly `v0.1.3`, embedded binary is present/executable, and embedded binary reports v0.1.3.

Acceptance criteria:
- Package archives are generated only under a temp directory, not repo `dist/`.
- Extracted `VERSION` is exactly v0.1.3.
- Split-scope `jsm validate` passes for the extracted skill package.
- Embedded binary reports v0.1.3.
- `make check-size` result is recorded.
- `git status --short` confirms no release archive/binary was staged or left as tracked source.
close_reason: Completed package validation from commit c6aa631 source tree. Evidence: make build VERSION=v0.1.3 produced ./bin/burpvalve reporting v0.1.3; VERSION=v0.1.3 DIST_DIR=<scrubbed-temp-path> ./scripts/package-skill.sh wrote burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and .sha256 under temp dist only; make check-size warned binary is 8565028 bytes above 8388608 threshold; extracted VERSION was v0.1.3; embedded scripts/bin/burpvalve was executable and reported v0.1.3; split-scope jsm validate passed after moving embedded binary aside; git status showed no release archive/binary staged.

title: oxp: add or absorb hash --staged verifier payload helper
description:
Background:
Verifiers need a first-party way to confirm the staged payload hash they are asked to verify. The current packet notes that the staged payload hash is Burpvalve-internal and not reproducible by naive `git diff --cached | sha256sum`. A helper command closes that gap.

Approved plan ownership rule:
`burpvalve hash --staged` is owned by this plan only if the verifier-orchestration work has not already landed an equivalent helper. The approved plan says the increment must absorb already-landed fixes by documenting them; do not duplicate.

Absorb rule:
Before implementing, inspect current commands and verifier-orchestration artifacts. If a compatible staged-hash helper already exists, update docs/help/tests to reference it and close this bead as absorbed. Do not create a second helper with overlapping semantics.

Scope if not already landed:
- Add a read-only `burpvalve hash --staged` command or equivalent approved command shape.
- Use the same internal staged payload hashing path that `commit`/verifier prompts use.
- Output JSON and human forms that include the payload hash, included paths, generated-path exclusions, and a warning that naive git-diff hashing is not equivalent.
- Include robot/help docs and examples suitable for verifier packets.
- Ensure generated evidence paths excluded from the payload hash are surfaced so packet path-list omissions do not recur silently.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verifiers can run one documented command to reproduce the Burpvalve staged payload hash for the current index.
- Output explains included paths and generated/excluded paths clearly.
- Command is read-only and safe for verifier agents.
- If already landed elsewhere, this bead records the landed command and does not duplicate.

Tests:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Hash|StagedPayload|Generated|Excluded'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Hash|Staged|Robots|Help|Verifier'`
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented and pushed in commit 1ead769 (oxp: add staged payload hash helper); attestation d43d61d789b4fb7fe0e53cecf381af38d370324211168a04a6d32beaf149b2d2 validates the staged helper payload.

title: ifr2-C3: add optional Beads enrichment and docs for payload accounting
description:
Background:
This bead is Unit C3 from the approved issue-followups round-2 plan. C1 defines the ownership schema and C2 adds the read-only payload accounting command. C3 adds optional Beads enrichment, docs, and help without changing the core ownership authority model.

Scope:
- Add optional Beads enrichment to the payload accounting output if C2 left it unwired and if the implementation remains low-risk.
- Document the ownership map schema, source precedence, statuses, and non-mutating command behavior.
- Add help/robot wording so agents understand that Beads metadata is display/enrichment only unless a future accepted design names an authoritative Beads field.
- Ensure the command degrades cleanly in non-Beads repos.

Binding contract:
- Beads may identify open or in_progress tasks and display bead metadata.
- Closed, deferred, tombstoned, and historical Beads are not conflict participants.
- Beads enrichment must not create ownership claims in this round. Ownership claims come from explicit stdin JSON and/or --ownership-file records only.
- --ownership-file wins over display-only Beads enrichment.
- The command remains read-only and must not mutate .beads, stage files, reserve files, or decide claims.

Likely file touches:
- cmd/burpvalve/main.go if flags/help require extension
- cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/account_test.go or equivalent
- internal ownership/accounting package from C1/C2
- docs/result-contract.md
- possibly plans/README.md only if the owner wants the approved plan indexed after conversion.

Tests required:
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run "Account|Payload|Owner|Beads|Robots|Help|Docs"
- go test ./internal/... -run "Account|Payload|Owner|Beads|Active|Closed|Deferred"
- go test ./...

Required scenarios:
- command works in a non-Beads repo and reports Beads enrichment unavailable without failing core ownership accounting;
- open/in_progress Beads can be displayed as active context when enrichment is requested;
- closed/deferred/tombstoned/historical Beads do not become conflict participants;
- Beads metadata does not create ownership claims;
- docs describe source precedence, statuses, structured expires_or_scope, and read-only guarantees;
- help/robot output warns that ownership claims must be explicit.

Acceptance criteria:
- Optional Beads enrichment is display-only and safe.
- Documentation and help make the helper usable without consulting the original plan.
- Non-Beads repos degrade cleanly.
- No durable repo-local ownership config is introduced.

Non-goals:
- No authoritative Beads ownership field.
- No automatic Agent Mail reservation integration.
- No command mutation of .beads or git state.
- No inference from bead titles, labels, close reasons, or arbitrary prose.
close_reason: Completed in commit 5094025; attestation backpressure/attestations/4115480b73c8b7080b17fcacb1f0bd550aecee8b6ebab9a342cb4a0561739627.json; verifier refs BrightOwl Agent Mail 3426 and ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3427; gate accepted staged payload sha256:4115480b73c8b7080b17fcacb1f0bd550aecee8b6ebab9a342cb4a0561739627.

title: rel13: install released v0.1.3 on this machine
description:
Release v0.1.3 binding context:\n- Owner ordered this private release so the current machine can install current Burpvalve; repo visibility stays unchanged.\n- Model the flow on plans/release-v0.1.2.md and the closed rel bead chain, including annotated tag, tag-triggered release.yml, full asset verification, split-scope JSM package validation, and pinned post-release install smoke.\n- Release gates after dogfood mode lands; do not tag or release until the dogfood-mode bead is closed and included in changelog/version evidence.\n- Do not use workflow_dispatch. Push main only after accepted gated commits; push tag v0.1.3 only after the release-prep commit is on origin/main.\n- Do not commit dist archives or local binaries; GitHub release assets are canonical.\n- Keep license/visibility/launch metadata unchanged; open-source visibility flip remains out of scope.\n- Coordinate GATE-WINDOW before staging any commit; begin by proving git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Use version v0.1.3 everywhere in this chain.\n
