title: E2E setup, config, install, and completion scenarios
description:
Background:
The setup/config/install path is the first user experience for Burpvalve. It needs E2E coverage because many failures only show up with fake HOME, PATH, shell rc files, git initialization, and real filesystem writes.

Goal:
Add E2E scenarios for setup, config precedence, install verification, completion verification, and git/hook ordering.

Scope:
- Non-git repo recovery and --git-init ordering before hook setup.
- setup output when a repo-local binary is missing but a global burpvalve is on PATH.
- Global/project config precedence in isolated fake HOME/XDG dirs.
- Guided/noninteractive config smoke path sufficient to prove config can affect later commands.
- install/completion verification in fake zsh/bash/fish homes where feasible.
- Ambiguous shell detection and exact next-step output.
- No unexpected root-level files after setup/install flows.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests assert exit codes, stable JSON fields, stable human substrings, and final git status.
- Project config overrides global config without rewriting global config.
- Completion script mode remains explicit and does not dump raw shell script from the no-arg guide path.
- PATH verification distinguishes command found on PATH, repo-local binary only, and command missing.
- Slow or shell-specific cases are either in go test ./... or documented under make test-e2e.
design:
Plan audit revision 4:
- Run after config-default consumption and install/completion verification are implemented and verified.
- Treat setup as inspection/readiness output in scenarios; use init, repair, config init, completion, and install commands for mutations.
- Fake HOME, XDG dirs, PATH, shell env, and shell rc files are required so tests do not inherit developer-machine state.
- Assert both beginner-readable human substrings and JSON facts because this is the first user-facing path.
- Cover command found globally on PATH, repo-local fallback only, both present, wrong origin/version when detectable, and command missing. The output must guide different next steps for each.
- Completion tests must prove the no-arg path is a guide and raw script output requires explicit script/print mode.
- Install tests must exercise install.sh from a packaged archive/skill-shaped fixture because that is how a user or agent can bootstrap Burpvalve without already having the CLI installed.
- Config tests must prove project config overrides global config without rewriting it and that configured defaults visibly affect later prompts/robot facts.
- Mutating flows in E2E should assert final confirmation behavior and no partial writes after cancel.
- No scenario should require a real user shell to be sourced; source/restart guidance is asserted as text/JSON.
- Keep project setup and global installation separate: project init/repair should affect repo files, while install/completion should affect user-level command and shell integration files.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests assert exit codes, stable JSON fields, stable human substrings, and final git status.
- Project config overrides global config without rewriting global config.
- Completion script mode remains explicit and does not dump raw shell script from the no-arg guide path.
- PATH verification distinguishes command found on PATH, repo-local binary only, both present, wrong origin/version when detectable, and command missing.
- Fresh archive install via install.sh covers planned writes, default cancel with no partial user-location writes, --yes success, skill destination, shim target, and post-install PATH/source guidance.
- Project init/repair scenarios prove repo-scoped writes do not silently mutate global shell/PATH state.
- Shell completion verification covers zsh/bash/fish/PowerShell where feasible with hermetic rc/completion paths and no real shell sourcing.
- Slow or shell-specific cases are either in go test ./... or documented under make test-e2e.
- A verifier subagent reviews this bead after implementation before it is closed.
close_reason: Added E2E setup, config, install, and completion first-run scenario. Evidence: covers non-git setup recovery, init --git-init before hooks, global PATH command without repo-local fallback, project-over-global config precedence, configured completion guide without raw script dump, completion install/verify --no-path, archive install plan/skill/shim/verification guidance, and final clean generated repo status. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed; read-only verifier Maxwell reported PASS.

title: osl: audit and refresh demo sources and generated assets
description:
Background:
README embeds generated demo assets, and launch requires refreshed VHS demos. Generated SVG/GIF files can contain paths, usernames, terminal prompts, or stale CLI behavior.

Scope:
- Audit demo sources: `docs/demos/*.sh`, `docs/demos/*.tape`, `docs/demos/*.txt`.
- Audit generated assets: `docs/demos/generated/*.svg`, `docs/demos/generated/*.gif`.
- Refresh VHS demos manually when needed; `readme-demos.yml` only uploads artifacts and does not commit regenerated files.
- Scan for usernames, hostnames, machine paths, stale CLI output, and misleading interactions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Demo source and generated assets are scanned and results recorded.
- Generated assets show current public CLI behavior.
- Any regenerated demos are staged intentionally and reviewed visually.
- No secret/private machine context is visible in demos unless explicitly accepted as harmless history.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Audit findings delivered in Agent Mail 3103; remediation landed in scrub commit 2bf5bbd with attestation backpressure/attestations/8866a766e83eadffb2486f637eec65ba2b8bbd4b7318a4454c20d5602fa7f5c1.json. Closed as tracker hygiene after OSL scrub.

title: land: 03 track live gate scaffold and hook
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 0 commit 3 tracks the live backpressure scaffold and pre-commit hook. After this commit, later landing commits can be genuinely gated and attested under the repository's own tracked rules.

Exact payload file list:
- `.githooks/pre-commit`
- `backpressure/manifest.yaml`
- `backpressure/README.md`
- `backpressure/anti-reward-hacking.md`
- `backpressure/autonomy-boundary.md`
- `backpressure/data-integrity.md`
- `backpressure/definition-of-done.md`
- `backpressure/destructive-operations.md`
- `backpressure/dry.md`
- `backpressure/evidence-log.md`
- `backpressure/lint-rules.md`
- `backpressure/one-function-one-test.md`
- `backpressure/performance-budget.md`
- `backpressure/scope-control.md`
- `backpressure/security-boundaries.md`
- `backpressure/visual-regression.md`
- `backpressure/attestations/README.md`
- `log/README.md`
- `log/backpressure/failed/README.md`

Implementation notes:
- `.githooks/pre-commit` must use `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` and forward the environment expected by the Gate Execution Contract.
- Do not include Phase 2 source changes or plan docs here.
- This is the last bootstrap-exception commit; every later landing commit must carry a real attestation.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is in the Phase 0 bootstrap-exception window. Commits 1-3 cannot honestly carry final Burpvalve attestations because the tracked hook/backpressure scaffold is not complete until commit 3. Still run staged-content verification: stage only this bead's payload, run `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`, execute the listed test commands against the staged slice, then `git stash pop`. The tracked hook introduced in commit 3 must invoke the gate through `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` rather than the stale `bin/burpvalve`. From commit 4 onward, use the full attestation choreography: stage exact payload, gather real verifier responses, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>` (plus `--bead-rationale` if multiple beads are deliberately combined), stage only the attestation path named by the tool, rerun the gate, then commit. Do not stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` or other generated files after attestation creation because that changes the payload hash.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hook file is tracked and invokes source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`.
- All listed backpressure condition docs and README files are tracked.
- `backpressure/manifest.yaml` references the intended condition files without missing paths.
- Commit message/landing notes identify this as allowed bootstrap exception 3 of 3 and state that later commits are attested.

Test commands:

- `rg -n "go run ./cmd/burpvalve|NO_TUI|BURPVALVE|responses" .githooks/pre-commit`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve --help`
- `go test ./...`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-03-verify`, `go test ./...`, `git stash pop`.

title: lint: Unit D CLI and robot wiring for lint init
description:
Background:
The first increment must expose a deterministic command surface for humans, agents, and CI without changing existing `burpvalve lint --json` behavior. The primary command is `burpvalve lint init`.

Scope:
- Add `burpvalve lint init` subcommand without regressing existing `burpvalve lint` or `burpvalve lint --json` behavior.
- Add flags: `--detect`, `--write`, `--preset <name>`, `--force` to `lint init`; add `--jobs N` to `burpvalve lint` itself.
- Binding flag matrix:
  - `--detect` is always read-only regardless of other flags, including `--force --write --detect`.
  - `--write` performs writes only after confirmation.
  - `--force` skips interactive confirmation only when `--write` is also present; `--force` without `--write` mutates nothing.
  - `--robots` accepts structured input with explicit `"confirm": true`; no robot path mutates without it.
- Update robot/help surfaces: `robotInputDoc`, `--robots` examples, command help, detection facts, proposed diff, confirmation, coverage fields, and `--jobs` semantics.
- Keep terminal/TUI state machine sequential outside the explicit concurrency beads.

Tests and commands:
- Add CLI tests for existing `burpvalve lint --json` compatibility, `lint init -h`, `--detect`, `--write`, `--force` matrix, robot confirm/no-confirm, and help/robots docs.
- Run `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*LintInit|Test.*LintJSONCompat|Test.*Robots|Test.*DetectNeverMutates|Test.*JobsFlag' -count=1` or actual test names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- `burpvalve lint init --json --detect` exposes detection facts and proposed manifest diffs.
- `burpvalve lint init --robots < input.json` supports deterministic noninteractive setup.
- `burpvalve lint init --force --preset go --write` is legal only as confirmed write behavior.
- `--detect` never mutates, including with `--force --write --detect`.
- Robot output exposes detection facts and proposed manifest diffs.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Implemented in ef12ab1

title: E2E explain and recovery scenarios
description:
Background:
Recovery output is valuable only if it works with real failed commands and blocked reports, not just handcrafted unit fixtures. explain should turn structured Burpvalve output into plain next steps without guessing, scraping terminal prose, or mutating the repo.

Goal:
Add E2E scenarios for burpvalve explain over real setup, lint, completion, commit, blocked-report, malformed, unknown, and artifact-path inputs.

Scope:
- setup --json output for environment/readiness failures such as non-git or hook drift where the structured contract exists.
- lint --json output for no configured lint command, proving not_enforced is explained as truthful non-enforcement rather than success.
- completion verification output where it helps first-run recovery.
- Real blocked verifier reports and generated artifacts from commit/backpressure flows, including at least one Beads-specific case after bead-delivery E2E exists.
- Malformed input and unknown-but-valid JSON input.
- Human output that says what happened, why Burpvalve stopped, what to run next, and when the user must decide. JSON output remains stable for agents.

Out of scope:
- New repair behavior or silent fixes. explain is a translator and recovery guide, not a mutating command.
- Snapshotting broad styled output; assert stable content and structured fields instead.
design:
Plan audit revision 5:
- Run this after the bead delivery/attestation E2E bead so explain can consume real blocked reports and artifacts from the current delivery model.
- Use real generated setup, lint, completion verification, and commit outputs where possible, then pipe or point explain at them.
- Tests should prove explain consumes structured fields, not terminal prose.
- Each recovery case should map to a beginner-readable shape: what happened, why Burpvalve stopped, what command to try next, and when Burpvalve cannot know the answer.
- Include one case where the correct next step is to ask the user rather than infer policy or mutate files.
- Keep human assertions short and stable; put detailed facts in JSON assertions.
- Verify malformed and unknown input return actionable errors without mutation.
- Include at least one artifact-path scenario so explain works after the user only has a saved attestation or blocked report file.
- Explain must not turn advisory output into an unconfirmed mutation. If it suggests repair/init/install, it should name confirmation expectations or a read-only verification command.
- Cover one non-Beads recovery path and one Beads-specific recovery path so br requirements are clear.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests assert exact exit codes, stable JSON fields, and beginner-readable human substrings.
- explain does not scrape human text when structured fields exist.
- explain names when Burpvalve cannot know the fix and must ask the user.
- setup, lint, completion verify, blocked report, malformed input, unknown input, and artifact-path inputs are covered where their structured contracts exist.
- Explain output never suggests mutating commands without making confirmation/review expectations clear.
- Explain covers at least one non-Beads recovery path and one Beads-specific recovery path so users understand when br is required.
- The lint no-op path is explained as not_enforced, not passed.
- Final git status is asserted after generated failure artifacts are handled.
- A verifier subagent reviews this bead after implementation before it is closed.
close_reason: Implemented explain/recovery E2E coverage for setup, lint no-op, completion verify, commit blocked, blocked report path, passing attestation path, malformed input, and unknown input. Added completion verify explanation support plus feature/bead fields in explain output. Fixed explicit --feature metadata so br-shaped feature IDs do not become bead_ids/source_bead without explicit --bead; added E2E regression coverage. Verification before close: go test ./... -count=1, make build, git diff --check, br dep cycles --json all passed. Read-only verifier Noether PASS.

title: vorch: 03 verifier prompts bound contract
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 2 upgrades `verifier prompts`. The existing generator already creates packets and profiles; this bead extends it with staleness bindings, condition content, bounded staged content, recorded-authorization behavior, explicit submit instructions, human-readable schemas, and a reproducible payload-hash contract.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts.go`: packet schema, binding hashes, payload-hash reproduction instructions, content rendering, authorization conditional, profile hygiene text, use-time condition model warnings.
- `internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts_test.go`: packet/binding/hash-reproduction/content/profile/authorization tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: `verifier prompts` command/help/robot output hunks.
- `cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go`: CLI JSON/human output tests, including hash reproducibility text in both formats.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: robot help assertions if verifier prompt help lives there.

Binding behavior:
- Each packet carries `staged_payload_hash`, `manifest_hash`, and that condition's `condition_file_hash`.
- Each packet explains how the verifier can independently reproduce or check the staged payload hash. This must be a precise Burpvalve canonicalization rule or an exact helper command such as `burpvalve hash --staged` if that helper is introduced in this bead or a tightly coupled prerequisite. Do not leave verifiers to guess with `git diff --cached --binary | sha256sum`; finding A9 from the first attested commit showed that naive command does not match Burpvalve's hash.
- The hash reproduction/check text must name generated paths excluded from the payload hash through `internal/gitindex.GeneratedPathPrefixes()` and must describe the per-entry inputs currently used by `HashStagedPayload`: sorted staged entries, path, normalized status, old path, raw git status, staged content size, and staged content for non-deleted files.
- Condition file contents are included inline, not only the path.
- Staged paths include git status; staged content excerpts are included with a documented cap. Oversized content lists paths and instructs the read-only verifier to inspect them directly.
- Packets do not include environment variables, config secrets, credentials, or unrelated repo context.
- Success criteria, verdict vocabulary, and per-verdict evidence requirements are rendered in the packet.
- Human/manual output renders the verdict JSON schema inline; it must not merely say to match `response_schema`.
- Packet includes the exact `burpvalve verifier submit` command line with prefilled condition and binding arguments.
- Native profile says to close the subagent thread when done; NTM profile says to batch cells per reviewer pane according to `docs/ntm-bridge.md` and preserve per-cell evidence.
- With recorded authorization, packets embed the recorded scope. Without it, they warn and say authorization is not recorded and must be obtained via `burpvalve config init` or the repo owner.
- D7 hard rule: the authorization string alone is never acceptable evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
- `verifier prompts` without recorded authorization warns and does not claim authority.
- `verifier prompts` with recorded authorization includes the recorded scope and still states it is not evidence.
- JSON and human outputs both include binding hashes, hash reproduction/check instructions, inline schema, submit command, and profile hygiene notes.
- A test proves the documented hash reproduction path matches the current `HashStagedPayload` output for a staged payload containing at least one text modification and one deleted file.
- `condition_models` keys not present in the active manifest produce a warning at use time, not a config-load failure.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'VerifierPrompt|Binding|PayloadHash|HashRepro|Authorization|ConditionContent|Schema|Profile'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Verifier|Prompt|PayloadHash|HashRepro|Authorization|Robots|Help'`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve verifier prompts --help`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.
Live dogfooding findings added 2026-07-02:
- A14 verdict-semantics rule is binding and must be printed in every verifier packet's success criteria: `not_applicable` means the staged payload contains no surface governed by this condition; `pass` means the staged payload contains a governed surface, the verifier inspected that surface, and the condition is satisfied, even when the factual finding is "nothing dangerous present." The distinction is whether there was a governed thing to check, not whether a problem was found. Packets must state this rule directly so verifiers do not infer pass/not_applicable semantics from taste.
- A15 packet path-accounting rule is binding: packets must list ALL staged paths from the payload under review. Paths excluded from the payload hash because they are generated Burpvalve evidence paths must still be shown separately and labeled as hash-excluded/generated. Never silently omit hash-excluded staged paths from verifier packets. The packet should make clear which paths are included in the hash and which paths are staged but excluded from that hash.

Additional acceptance criteria from A14/A15:
- Tests cover the pass vs not_applicable semantics text in both human and JSON packet output.
- Tests stage at least one generated-prefix path plus one ordinary path and prove packet output lists both, with generated/hash-excluded paths labeled separately rather than omitted.
- Verifier packets remain usable even when hash-excluded paths are present: verifiers can see the whole staged path set and can independently decide whether a condition governs any of those paths.

Live dogfooding findings added 2026-07-02 - A17/A18 packet and response contract:
- A17 multi-verifier contract is binding for verifier packets and response schemas. Each condition response schema rendered in human and JSON packet output must allow an optional `supplemental_verifiers` array and optional `adjudication` object. A supplemental verifier entry must contain the same verifier provenance expectations as the primary verdict plus verdict, message, evidence, transcript_ref when available, and next_action for fail/unknown. `adjudication` must contain the ruling authority (agent/person), ruling summary, final ruling/verdict if applicable, and an audit reference such as Agent Mail message id/thread id. The primary condition verdict remains the condition's final committed outcome; supplemental entries preserve additional verifier evidence, and adjudication explains disagreement resolution.
- Backward compatibility follows the existing legacy-file decision: new bound packets/responses/attestations understand these optional fields, but legacy unbound `--responses` files are not required to provide them and continue through the vorch-06/vorch-10 compatibility path with notices rather than strict breakage.
- A18 anti-reward-hacking scope text is binding in packet success criteria: the anti-reward-hacking cell checks the staged payload's consistency with its own claims, not merely the absence of shortcut-authorization language. Brief rationale to include in packet/template text: in land-04, the staged decision document contained a bare arrow chain in D10 while D4 of the same document declared bare arrow chains defective; the verifier correctly failed anti-reward-hacking because the payload contradicted its own standard and the gate held until the document was fixed.

Additional acceptance criteria from A17/A18:
- Human-readable and JSON packet schemas include optional `supplemental_verifiers` and `adjudication` fields with clear field meanings and examples.
- Tests prove packets for shared/double-verified cells can instruct verifiers to return supplemental evidence without overwriting the primary condition result.
- Tests assert the A18 anti-reward-hacking success-criteria wording appears in packet output for that condition.
- Packet examples remain paste-safe and do not use bare arrow-chain workflow prose while explaining the land-04 rationale.
close_reason: Completed in commit 372db52

title: bcm: 05 admin batch and multi-bead rationale paths
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 4 adds the admin batched path and multi-bead delivery rationale path. Admin closures are tracker-only; delivery multi-bead closures intentionally close all beads before one gate run so `.beads/issues.jsonl` is part of the attested payload.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: admin path, multi-bead close loop, rationale plumbing, confirmation handling, journal behavior for multiple bead IDs.
- `cmd/burpvalve/beads_test.go` or `cmd/burpvalve/beads_close_test.go`: admin-only, batched admin, multi-bead rationale, partial-failure, and confirmation tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go`: only if attestation `bead_ids` or `bead_rationale` commit integration needs additional assertions.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: robot/help assertions for multi-bead and admin close behavior.

Admin path binding rules:
- Admin path is allowed only when staged paths are exclusively `.beads/**` or the close flow itself has only tracker changes to stage.
- `--admin-only` with any non-`.beads` staged path hard-errors and names paths. It must not fall back to delivery silently.
- Admin closures run safe-order steps 2 through 5 plus a tracker-only commit with the same confirmation rules.
- Admin closures never ask for code/verifier attestations and never fabricate implementation evidence.
- Admin closures may batch multiple admin beads.

Multi-bead delivery binding rules:
- Multiple delivery beads require `--bead-rationale`; every bead must intend the same staged payload.
- Close all beads before the single gate run. This is the resolved recommendation because `.beads/issues.jsonl` must be inside the attested payload.
- The single `burpvalve commit` invocation records all bead IDs and preserves `--bead-rationale` into attestation metadata through `PreCommitOptions.BeadRationale`.
- If one close/sync step fails, the journal/result must show which bead IDs are closed, which are pending, and the exact resume command.

br command hygiene:
- Every `br close` and `br sync --flush-only` command is printed before execution and recorded.
- Never pass `--force`, `--bypass-policy`, `--bypass-reason`, or any br bypass flag.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin-only batch can close/sync/stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` and optionally commit without verifier evidence.
- Admin-only with non-Beads staged payload hard-errors naming all offending paths.
- Multi-bead delivery without rationale blocks; with rationale, all bead IDs are recorded in the final attestation.
- Close-before-commit order is preserved for multi-bead delivery.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'BeadsClose.*(Admin|AdminOnly|Batch|Multi|Rationale|Coupled)'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Commit.*Bead|Rationale'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed Beads preflight and verifier-orchestration foundations it uses. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m bcm-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses when the payload is delivery work, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. Never fabricate verification evidence, and never gather evidence before `.beads/issues.jsonl` is staged for a closure flow.
close_reason: Implement bcm-05 admin batch and multi-bead rationale paths

title: bcm: 08 defer inverse drift until bead-file linkage
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 6 also defers the inverse drift check until bead-file linkage exists. The accepted drift check only flags possible closed-bead-plus-dirty-tree drift; it does not try to infer committed work for in-progress beads from filenames or prose.

Exact payload file list for future work:

- Future work may touch `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`, `cmd/burpvalve/beads_drift_test.go`, and any future bead-file-linkage schema once that schema exists.

Deferred binding rules:
- Do not implement inverse drift in the initial `beads drift` command.
- Do not infer file linkage from bead titles, labels, close reasons, or arbitrary commit text.
- Future implementation requires a durable bead-to-file/work linkage model first.
- Output must remain advisory unless wired into lint/CI by project policy.

Acceptance criteria for future activation:
- A bead-file linkage source exists and is documented.
- In-progress-bead committed-work detection uses that linkage, not heuristics from titles or labels.
- Tests include false-positive prevention for similarly named files/beads.

Test commands for future activation:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'InverseDrift|BeadFileLinkage|FalsePositive'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: p4 backlog only. Do not implement until the linkage model exists.

title: gate run: single fail-closed command collapsing commit ceremony
description: Round-3 headline candidate from dogfooding C27. After verify-early/gate-fast, the dominant per-commit cost is ceremony executed as LLM turns (~20-30 turns per commit). Specify and build a single 'burpvalve gate run' invocation that takes a prepared hash-bound handoff and executes the mechanical sequence fail-closed: index check, stage, hash check, gate, commit, attestation staging + second commit, push, close, sync, release, wake. Explicit stop points: hash mismatch, verifier disagreement or fail/unknown cells, dirty index/peer dirt, test failure. Pairs with the D14 Spark gate-operator ritual (a distrusted low-tier operator or a command can run mechanics because the valve is fail-closed). title: osl: decide public repo home first
description:
Owner decision required FIRST.

Question:
Should the public repository stay under `clicksopendoors/burpvalve`, or move to another owner/org before launch?

Why this blocks everything:
Repo home changes raw installer URLs, `BURPVALVE_REPO` defaults, README commands, badge URLs, release links, repo About metadata, vulnerability-reporting surface, and any future Go module path. The plan explicitly says nothing else starts until this decision is made.

Decision options:
- Stay under `clicksopendoors/burpvalve`.
- Move to a product/personal org, with the exact owner/name provided.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decision is recorded in a durable decision doc or this bead's close reason.
- Exact owner/repo string is known.
- Dependent beads use that owner/repo consistently.
- Any move timing is defined before installer/docs work begins.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.

Cross-review polish binding clarification:
This bead is an OWNER decision, not an implementer task. The owner must answer with one of the decision options above.

Recommendation:
Stay under `clicksopendoors/burpvalve` for the public launch unless the owner already has a concrete target org/repo ready. This minimizes installer URL, badge URL, release-link, and future module-path churn; a repo move can be handled later as a separate migration if needed.
close_reason: Owner ruling recorded in docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md D-OSL-1: public repo home stays clicksopendoors/burpvalve. Recording only; no launch or visibility action executed.

title: lexicon: align prompt bank and skill wording
description:
Background:
Agents read Burpvalve's prompt bank and skill docs more often than they read long-form docs. The approved lexicon plan requires those surfaces to use the same terms as README and docs/vocabulary.md while preserving stable prompt names, response fields, and feature x condition terminology.

Depends on:
- Canonical D-LEX decisions and docs/vocabulary.md from the first lexicon bead.

Scope:
- Update prompt-bank content so verifier and orchestrator prompts use:
  - work unit for generic work;
  - feature when referring to stable response fields, staged payload binding, or the existing feature x condition matrix;
  - bead only when explicitly Beads-aware;
  - seal/attestation for evidence artifacts;
  - valve/burp language for gate refusal and recovery.
- Update skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md and related skill docs so incoming agents see the same definitions.
- Preserve backpressure as the anchor concept in skill descriptions.
- Keep prompt names stable public API; do not rename prompt IDs.
- Do not claim NTM or Beads is required for every work unit.
- Keep robot/machine-readable prompt response contracts formal and stable.

Out of scope:
- No CLI help/error sweep except synchronized expected strings if a shared prompt fixture requires it.
- No README glossary changes except link/reference consistency.
- No command, schema, path, flag, prompt ID, or attestation directory renames.

Acceptance criteria:
- Prompt-bank tests still pass.
- Skill wording uses the same definitions as README/docs/vocabulary.md.
- Prompt response fields and feature x condition terminology remain stable.
- Seal is presented as an attestation, not a replacement for attestation.
- Bead appears only for Beads-aware prompt/skill contexts and is described as tracker-backed work-unit vocabulary when needed.
- No prompt ID/name is renamed.
- No claim says NTM or Beads is required for every work unit.

Verification:
- git diff --check -- internal skill backpressure
- go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Prompt|Verifier|Condition' -count=1
- rg -n 'work unit|feature|bead|seal|attestation|valve|burp|backpressure|NTM|Beads' internal skill backpressure
- go test ./...
close_reason: Delivered prompt-bank and Burpvalve skill wording adoption for the D-LEX lexicon: canonical prompt bank, prompt export tests, Codex skill wording, and mirrored orchestrator skill templates now use work unit/seal/valve vocabulary while preserving Beads/br tool vocabulary and stable prompt contracts. Commit 9ca68a5 pushed. Attestation backpressure/attestations/ee1042a6fa3d7ab5b4af700d4cac70f74cdb9d3ce0a09d8b5ccdac714ee179ba.json. Corrected verifier refs: BrightOwl 3353 judgment/high; ScarletMarsh 3352 mechanical/shared. Old hash 404561e failed scope due unrelated .beads catch-up; corrected hash ee1042a excluded .beads and passed.

title: bcm: 03 delivery close safe-order state machine
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 3 implements the single-delivery-bead close path. This is the core mutating workflow: it closes a bead, syncs tracker state into the staged payload, gathers or consumes verifier evidence against that final payload, stages the attestation, and optionally commits only after explicit confirmation.

Exact payload file list:
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: `beads close` command, options, robot input docs, result types, journal/state-machine helpers, print-before-execute command wrappers, and close-flow orchestration.
- `cmd/burpvalve/beads_test.go` or new `cmd/burpvalve/beads_close_test.go`: single-delivery happy path, confirmation, journal, resume, br hygiene, and result-contract tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go`: robot help assertions for `beads close` flags and stdin schema.
- `docs/result-contract.md` only if closure result-contract extensions need durable documentation.

Command surface:
- `burpvalve beads close <bead-id> [<bead-id>...]`
- Flags: `--root`, `--admin-only`, `--reason` required, `--bead-rationale`, `--responses <file>`, `--feature <id>`, `--resume`, `--yes`, `--json`, and `--robots` structured stdin.
- Interactive confirmation defaults to No. Robots require explicit `confirm: true` plus a commit message for the `git commit` step.
- Dry-run preview prints every mutating `br` and `git` command before asking.

Binding nine-step safe order for delivery close:
1. The agent has already staged the delivery payload. The command never stages arbitrary dirty files.
2. The command runs preflight by reusing `buildBeadsPreflightReport`.
3. The command runs `br close <id> --reason "<reason>"` without force or bypass flags.
4. The command runs `br sync --flush-only` without force.
5. The command stages exactly `.beads/issues.jsonl`; no other path is staged by this step.
6. Verifier evidence is collected or consumed against the final staged payload. Evidence from before `.beads/issues.jsonl` was staged is stale and must be rejected by hash binding with a message pointing back to this numbered order.
7. The command runs `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --bead <id>` plus `--feature`, `--responses`, and `--bead-rationale` when applicable.
8. If the gate writes an attestation and asks for it to be staged, that attestation-staging bounce is a normal middle state, not a failure. The command records `attestation_written_unstaged` or equivalent with `fatal: false`, stages exactly the named attestation path when confirmation/flow allows, and reruns the gate so the final staged payload is validated.
9. The command runs `git commit` only after explicit confirmation. Without confirmation, it stops with exact structured `next_steps`.

Journal/state machine design:
- Journal path is `log/backpressure/closures/<bead-id>.json`, created lazily on first `beads close`.
- Journal contains ordered steps with `id`, `status` (`pending`, `done`, `failed`, `skipped`, plus a clear nonfatal waiting/status value if needed for normal attestation staging), executed command, captured output reference, and timestamps.
- Every run recomputes reality first: bead status via `br show --json`, staged paths, response file presence, and attestation presence. Reality wins over stale journal claims.
- Steps are idempotent: already closed bead is skipped with closed_at/actor warning, already flushed sync is skipped, existing bound responses file is reused, an existing unstaged attestation from the current payload is staged/revalidated rather than rewritten, and an already-staged attestation is revalidated rather than rewritten.
- Failure returns `partial_success: true`, updates the journal, and emits structured `next_steps` objects with `id`, `message`, exact `command`, and `fatal`, including `burpvalve beads close <id> --resume`.
- Normal nonfatal stops, such as waiting for the user to stage/confirm an attestation or commit, also emit structured `next_steps` with `fatal: false`; they must not be reported as generic gate failures.

br/git command hygiene:
- Never pass `--force`, `--bypass-policy`, `--bypass-reason`, or any bypass flag to `br close` or `br sync`.
- Print every mutating `br` and `git` command before execution and record command, stdout, stderr, exit status, and output refs in JSON/journal.

Acceptance criteria:
- A single delivery bead can close through the numbered order to a gated attestation and, with confirmation, a commit recording its `bead_id`.
- The normal attestation-written-then-stage-and-rerun bounce is classified as expected and resumable, with `fatal: false` next steps, not as a failed close.
- Without commit confirmation, the command stops after gate revalidation and gives the exact `git commit` invocation in structured `next_steps`.
- Resume reclassifies reality and continues from the correct step rather than trusting stale journal status.
- `br` absent blocks with structured JSON exit 2 and actionable guidance.
- No bypass flags appear in invoked `br` commands.

Test commands:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'BeadsClose|Delivery|Journal|Resume|Confirm|AttestationWritten|StageAttestation|BRHygiene|ResultContract'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Robots|Help|BeadsClose'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed Beads preflight and verifier-orchestration foundations it uses. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m bcm-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses when the payload is delivery work, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. Never fabricate verification evidence, and never gather evidence before `.beads/issues.jsonl` is staged for a closure flow.

title: oxp: pxpipe orchestrator context mode (two-lane boot packet)
description: Owner direction 2026-07-08 (exploratory - owner explicitly unsure it helps; validate before trusting): build a pxpipe mode for the orchestrator that packs everything a fresh Claude orchestrator needs - ORCHESTRATOR.md rationale, orchestrator toolbox, ntm-bridge, vibing-with-ntm patterns, dogfooding findings - as a pxpipe two-lane packet. Research source: ~/Projects/command/docs/18-pxpipe-research.md (pxpipe-proxy@0.8.0). Design constraints from the research: (1) image lane = gist/rationale/war stories only; (2) factsheet.txt text lane = ALL exact strings (command syntax, tool names, provenance fields, session names, traps) - no pass/fail or command recall may depend on image OCR; (3) rules the orchestrator must OBEY stay live text (upstream issue #66 instruction-authority weakness); (4) never image secrets; treat ~/.pxpipe logs as sensitive (issue #69); (5) start with pxpipe export (pure local), proxy mode later and Codex routing is a separate unproven spike; (6) probable shape: burpvalve pxpack --orchestrator writing backpressure/pxpipe-packets/<id>/ with manifest+prompt+factsheet+pages+source-map; (7) MUST include the A/B validation experiment (imaged+factsheet reviewer vs plain-text reviewer on the same orchestration questions, scored for cost/latency/missed exact strings/decision quality) as the acceptance gate before the mode is recommended in shipped templates. PLAN FIRST per D10: plans/pxpipe-orchestrator-mode.md before beads decomposition.

title: rel13: verify v0.1.3 GitHub release assets
description:
Release v0.1.3 binding context:\n- Owner ordered this private release so the current machine can install current Burpvalve; repo visibility stays unchanged.\n- Model the flow on plans/release-v0.1.2.md and the closed rel bead chain, including annotated tag, tag-triggered release.yml, full asset verification, split-scope JSM package validation, and pinned post-release install smoke.\n- Release gates after dogfood mode lands; do not tag or release until the dogfood-mode bead is closed and included in changelog/version evidence.\n- Do not use workflow_dispatch. Push main only after accepted gated commits; push tag v0.1.3 only after the release-prep commit is on origin/main.\n- Do not commit dist archives or local binaries; GitHub release assets are canonical.\n- Keep license/visibility/launch metadata unchanged; open-source visibility flip remains out of scope.\n- Coordinate GATE-WINDOW before staging any commit; begin by proving git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Use version v0.1.3 everywhere in this chain.\n
Background:
The installer needs both platform archives and checksums. v0.1.3 release verification must prove the tag-built GitHub release exposes the full expected asset set before installation from the release can be trusted.

Scope:
- Wait for `.github/workflows/release.yml` to complete for tag `v0.1.3`.
- Run `gh release view v0.1.3 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,isDraft,isPrerelease,assets`.
- Verify exactly these nine assets exist and are nonzero/uploaded: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Confirm v0.1.3 is latest in `gh release list` or via `isLatest`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `gh release view v0.1.3` succeeds.
- Release is not draft/prerelease unless owner explicitly changes policy.
- v0.1.3 is latest.
- Exactly the expected nine assets are present; missing or extra archive/checksum assets are blockers unless owner approves.
- Evidence includes the JSON or a saved path to it.

Evidence saved at <scrubbed-temp-path> and <scrubbed-temp-path> gh release view v0.1.3 succeeded; release is not draft and not prerelease; gh release list shows v0.1.3 as Latest; exactly nine expected nonzero uploaded assets were present: four platform archives, four .sha256 files, and checksums.txt. title: oxp: implement prompt export content-hash and divergence protection
description:
Background:
The prompt bank solves session drift only if local exports cannot silently become a second source of truth. Revision 3 requires metadata/content-hash protection, local divergence warnings, and a hard boundary that repair never mutates exported prompts.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Prompt-bank command contract (exact): prompt names are a stable public API (renames are breaking changes). `prompts show <name> [--var key=value ...]`: missing required variables is a usage error listing them; `--json` emits `{name, version, variables: [{name, required, description}], body}`. `--write` exports to `docs/prompts/<name>.md` with a metadata header (burpvalve version, prompt name, content hash); it refuses to overwrite an export whose content hash shows local modification unless `--force`; `repair` never touches `docs/prompts/`. `prompts show` warns when a differing local export exists. Rendering is tested with hostile variable values."

Scope:
- Implement `prompts show <name> --write` export to `docs/prompts/<name>.md`.
- Metadata header must include Burpvalve version, prompt name, and content hash.
- If an existing export's content hash indicates local modification, refuse overwrite unless `--force`.
- If a local export exists and differs from embedded prompt content, `prompts show` warns without mutating.
- `repair` must never touch `docs/prompts/`.
- Exported prompt files are local copies, not authoritative prompt definitions.

Acceptance criteria:
- First `--write` creates `docs/prompts/<name>.md` with metadata and rendered body.
- Re-running `--write` is idempotent when the export is unmodified.
- Modified local export refuses overwrite without `--force` and explains why.
- `--force` overwrites deliberately and updates metadata.
- `prompts show` warns about divergent local export but renders embedded canonical content.
- `burpvalve repair` leaves `docs/prompts/` untouched in tests.

Tests:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'PromptsWrite|PromptExport|ContentHash|Divergence|Force|Repair'`
- `go test ./internal/... -run 'Prompt|Hash|Export'` if helper package is introduced.
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented and pushed in commit c8d0a2d (oxp: add prompt export content hash and divergence protection); attestation backpressure/attestations/b59a90e2f8f2c89443b2fd4a6f6ae01a8fb059df5ba39c7e55a1c436f0f77cfa.json validates the prompt export payload.

title: vorch: 06 commit auto-discovery and legacy compatibility
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 3 finishes by making `commit` consume bound verifier responses automatically and preserving compatibility for legacy unbound `--responses` files. This lets the loop be `verifier begin`, one submit per cell, then `burpvalve commit` without manually passing the hash-keyed file.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts.go`: bound response loading/validation, legacy notice path, auto-discovery helper, stale-file reporting.
- `internal/backpressure/artifacts_test.go`: strict bound validation, legacy unchanged validation, auto-discovery stale-file tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/main.go`: commit/pre-commit command output and robot/help text for auto-discovery.
- `cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go`: commit auto-discovery, explicit override, stale hash, incomplete unknown cells, legacy notice tests.
- `cmd/burpvalve/e2e_test.go`: full begin/submit/commit loop if e2e coverage belongs there.

Binding behavior:
- When no `--responses` flag is given and no TTY questionnaire is in play, `runPreCommit` looks for `log/backpressure/responses/<current-staged-hash>.json`.
- Explicit `--responses` always overrides auto-discovery.
- Hash-keyed stale files do not match; the error should say responses exist for a different staged payload and recommend rerunning `verifier begin`.
- Incomplete files with any `unknown` cell report through the existing gate behavior.
- Bound response files validate strictly: binding hashes plus evidence-on-pass are mandatory.
- Legacy unbound `--responses` files keep today's validation unchanged and print a notice that bound flows exist.
- Existing `ExpectedBinding(plan)` continues to protect generated attestation integrity for legacy files.

Acceptance criteria:
- Full loop works on a fresh repo: `verifier begin`, N `verifier submit` calls, `burpvalve commit` without `--responses`, then a passing attestation whose conditions carry provenance and workspace-resolvable `transcript_ref` values.
- Auto-discovery stale-file test covers a staged payload change after begin and before commit.
- Explicit `--responses` path overrides auto-discovery.
- Legacy response fixtures still pass/fail exactly as before except for the added notice.
- Result-contract JSON includes actionable `next_steps` for stale, incomplete, and missing auto-discovered files.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Bound|Legacy|AutoDiscover|Stale|EvidenceOnPass'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Commit|AutoDiscover|Legacy|Stale|Verifier'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'E2E.*Verifier|Begin|Submit|Commit'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this plan is intentionally not wired to cross-plan beads here, but implementation must happen after the landed verifier-policy/prompt foundations exist. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. The commit gate must run from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve` / `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`. Gather real verifier responses, stage only the attestation file Burpvalve names, rerun the gate, and commit. D7 is binding: standing authorization is never per-cell verification evidence, and agents must not fabricate or replay verifier evidence.
close_reason: Implemented in ffb325c; audit passed for VORCH-06 commit auto-discovery and legacy compatibility.

title: rel: verify v0.1.2 GitHub release asset set
description:
Background:
The installer's GitHub path requires both the platform tarball and `checksums.txt`. The release workflow builds four platform archives, four per-archive sha256 files, and an aggregate checksums file. This bead verifies the tag-built GitHub release before any post-release install smoke can be trusted.

Scope:
- Wait for `.github/workflows/release.yml` to finish for tag `v0.1.2`.
- Use `gh release view` to verify v0.1.2 exists, is latest, and exposes exactly the expected nine assets and no extra archive/checksum assets unless a maintainer explicitly approves them.
- Confirm `checksums.txt` exists; without it, `install.sh` direct GitHub install cannot verify downloads.
- Do not upload assets manually unless the workflow failed and a maintainer explicitly approves a recovery path. The normal path is tag-push workflow output.

Exact verification commands:
```bash
gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets | jq -r '.tagName, .isLatest, (.assets[].name)'
```
Expected nine release assets:
```text
burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz
burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256
burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz
burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256
burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz
burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256
burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz
burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256
checksums.txt
```
Optional workflow inspection:
```bash
gh run list --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --workflow release.yml --limit 5
gh run view --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve <run-id> --log-failed
```

Acceptance criteria:
- `gh release view v0.1.2` succeeds.
- `tagName` is `v0.1.2` and `isLatest` is true or the release list clearly shows v0.1.2 latest.
- Exactly the expected nine asset names are present: all four platform archives, all four `.sha256` files, and `checksums.txt`; any extra archive or checksum asset is a blocker unless explicitly approved.
- No tracked repo `dist/` files are used as a substitute for release assets.
Release command anchors every implementer must preserve:
- Local versioned build: `make build VERSION=v0.1.2`
- Temporary package build that must not dirty repo `dist/`: `tmp_dist="$(mktemp -d)"; VERSION=v0.1.2 DIST_DIR="$tmp_dist" ./scripts/package-skill.sh`
- Size gate: `make check-size`; if the binary/archive warning is >8 MiB, record the size in `docs/CHANGELOG.md` v0.1.2 because `release.yml` uses generated release notes.
- Extracted package validation: `tmp_extract="$(mktemp -d)"; tar -xzf "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" -C "$tmp_extract"; test "$(cat "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/VERSION")" = "v0.1.2"; jsm validate "$tmp_extract/burpvalve"; "$tmp_extract/burpvalve/scripts/bin/burpvalve" --version`
- Pre-release archive smoke, never `latest`: `tmp_home="$(mktemp -d)"; tmp_bin="$(mktemp -d)"; bash ./install.sh --from-archive "$tmp_dist/burpvalve_$(go env GOOS)_$(go env GOARCH).tar.gz" --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version; "$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json`
- Release commit/tag sequence: `git status --short`; `git add <release-prep files>`; `git commit -m "Release v0.1.2"`; `git push origin main`; `git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"`; `git push origin v0.1.2`
- Release verification command: `gh release view v0.1.2 --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --json tagName,isLatest,assets`
- Expected nine release assets exactly: `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_amd64.tar.gz.sha256`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`, `burpvalve_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sha256`, `checksums.txt`.
- Post-release pinned private install smoke: fetch installer with authenticated `gh api 'repos/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/contents/install.sh?ref=main' --jq .content | base64 -d > "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh"`, then run `bash "$tmp/install-burpvalve.sh" --repo clicksopendoors/burpvalve --version v0.1.2 --skills-dir "$tmp_home/skills" --bin-dir "$tmp_bin" --yes`; verify `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" --version` is v0.1.2 and `"$tmp_bin/burpvalve" setup --json` works.

Global release prohibitions:
- Do not use `workflow_dispatch`; `release.yml` must be triggered only by pushing annotated tag `v0.1.2`.
- Do not close GitHub issues #13, #14, or #17 in this release. #13 and #14 may receive boundary comments pointing to their plans; #17 stays open for guided lint setup.
- Do not change license or visibility; keep `skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md` frontmatter `license: proprietary` and `distribution: forbidden` until the separate public launch plan.
- Do not add new product features between the landed working set and the v0.1.2 tag.
- Do not regenerate README demos unless a demo is actively wrong; `readme-demos.yml` uploads artifacts but does not commit regenerated assets.
- Do not commit release archives or binaries to source; GitHub release assets are canonical.

Release URL https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/releases/tag/v0.1.2; latest tag API returned v0.1.2; release is not draft/prerelease; exactly nine expected assets present with nonzero uploaded state including checksums.txt. Evidence JSON: <scrubbed-temp-path>

title: ifr2-B: harden staged-path packet accounting and generated exclusions
description:
Background:
This bead implements Unit B from plans/issue-followups-round-2.md, approved on 2026-07-03 after FuchsiaBear review and owner ruling. It covers issue draft 7 and dogfooding finding A15. The goal is evidence-surface integrity: verifiers must see every staged path, while payload hash identity excludes only recognized generated evidence JSON.

Binding contract:
- Every verifier packet lists every staged path from the index.
- Packet output distinguishes hash-included staged paths, hash-excluded generated evidence paths, generated evidence rules/prefixes, and bounded staged content details where safe.
- Hash exclusion must use gitindex.IsGeneratedEvidencePath, not broad prefix matching. Scaffold/docs files under backpressure/attestations/ and log/backpressure/failed/ are hash-included unless recognized generated evidence artifacts.
- burpvalve hash --staged --json, burpvalve verifier prompts --json, and human verifier prompt output must classify paths consistently.
- Owner ruling: do NOT add staged_payload_all. The contract is the union invariant: every staged path appears in exactly one of staged_payload or hash_excluded_staged_payload, with no omissions and no duplicates. A duplicate all-path field invites drift.
- This is an absorption/hardening bead. Existing VORCH work may already satisfy much of the behavior; inspect first and limit changes to missing tests/docs when the contract already holds.

Likely file touches:
- internal/gitindex/gitindex.go
- internal/gitindex/gitindex_test.go
- internal/backpressure/core.go
- internal/backpressure/core_test.go
- internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts.go
- internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/hash_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go
- cmd/burpvalve/help_test.go
- docs/attestation-schema.md or docs/result-contract.md for path-classification wording.

Tests required:
- go test ./internal/gitindex -run "Generated|Evidence|Path"
- go test ./internal/backpressure -run "Verifier|Prompt|Hash|Generated|StagedPayload"
- go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run "Verifier|Hash|Robots|Help|Generated|Staged"
- go test ./...

Required scenarios:
- staged backpressure/attestations/README.md and log/backpressure/failed/README.md are listed and hash-included;
- staged generated attestation JSON is listed and hash-excluded;
- packet JSON has all staged paths even when some are hash-excluded;
- verifier prompt JSON and hash JSON satisfy the union invariant over staged_payload plus hash_excluded_staged_payload;
- human prompt output names hash-excluded generated paths separately;
- hash --staged --json and verifier packet JSON agree on classification.

Acceptance criteria:
- Complete staged path accounting is locked by tests and docs.
- Generated evidence JSON is excluded from the staged hash but still visible to verifiers.
- Scaffold/docs files under generated evidence directories remain hash-included.
- No staged_payload_all field is added.
- If existing behavior already satisfies the contract, the final diff is limited to regression tests/docs.

Non-goals:
- No ownership accounting helper.
- No attestation schema changes for supplemental verifiers.
- No broad generated-directory exclusion.
- No automatic staging/unstaging or file reservation behavior.
close_reason: Completed in commit 322934a; attestation backpressure/attestations/8740ebec1efcb2ff451348ec8a48e471d6770bcf6fdc0525bae4d09e6d438476.json; gate passed with BrightOwl 3135 and ScarletMarsh 3131.

title: Add burpvalve explain for blocked reports and setup failures
description:
Background:
Approved idea #7: users and agents need a way to turn blocked reports and setup failures into plain recovery instructions. This should build on the structured recovery contract and verifier provenance model, not invent parallel heuristics or scrape human text.

Goal:
Add burpvalve explain to summarize setup/init/repair/lint/commit blockers and blocked report files in plain language plus exact recovery commands when Burpvalve can know them.

Scope:
- Support explaining a blocked report path, an attestation path, or stdin JSON from setup/init/repair/lint/commit.
- Output should include what failed, why it matters, whether it is fatal, what evidence is missing, the relevant verifier policy when applicable, and exact next steps.
- Provide --json for robots with stable fields.
- Reuse structured next_steps and verifier policy failure data; do not scrape human text or duplicate setup/commit decision logic.
- Explain lint no-op as missing configured evidence, not success.
- Explain setup/install/config blockers using config source information when present.
- Avoid overclaiming: when Burpvalve cannot know the fix, say what must be decided by the user.
- Human output should be short enough to act on from a terminal without becoming another wall of text.
- This command must be read-only and should never repair, stage, or write files.

Acceptance criteria:
- burpvalve explain log/backpressure/failed/<file>.json explains missing verifier cells, verifier policy, and next action.
- burpvalve setup --json | burpvalve explain - produces recovery instructions for non-git, missing br, or hook drift.
- burpvalve lint --json | burpvalve explain - explains no configured lint command without calling it a pass.
- Tests cover blocked report, attestation path, setup JSON, lint no-op JSON, malformed input, unknown input, config-source details when present, and verifier policy failures.
- Human output is concise and beginner-readable; JSON output is stable for agents.
- go test ./... passes.
design:
Plan audit revision:
- Explain is a read-only translator from structured Burpvalve facts to next action. It must not repair, stage, write files, or summarize free-form terminal prose.
- Use normalized attestation/query data for attestation and blocked-report paths; use command JSON schemas for stdin input.
- Human output should answer four questions: what stopped, why it matters, what to run next, and what Burpvalve cannot know.
- JSON output should keep enough original facts for agents to choose retry, ask user, or stop without scraping human text.
- Treat lint no-op as missing configured evidence. Never present it as a clean pass.
- For setup/config/install failures, include config source and command-path facts when present so users can see whether the problem is global, project-local, or PATH-related.
- Unknown or malformed input should return a short graded error with examples of valid input, not a panic or a long help dump.
close_reason: Implemented burpvalve explain for setup/init/repair/lint/commit JSON and attestation/blocked-report paths. Added stable JSON and concise human output, config-source preservation, lint no-op missing-evidence explanation, verifier policy blockers, read-only behavior, init/repair scaffold mutation explanations, strict artifact shape validation for stdin/path evidence, docs/help/skill coverage, and lint --json quiet mode for clean explain pipes. Verification: focused explain/attestation/validate tests passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build and git diff --check passed; direct lint pipe and malformed artifact repros passed; read-only verifier Anscombe rechecked PASS.

title: PRS-13 Define attestation artifact schema and validation
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-13. This bead intentionally precedes PRS-09 even though the GOAL.md dependency table also says PRS-13 depends on PRS-09; that table would create a cycle, and br dep cycles must stay empty. Define the source-backed schema for passing attestation artifacts and blocked-attempt reports before the CLI writes them. Files/patterns: internal/attestations/, docs/attestation-schema.md, optional JSON schema under templates or docs. Schema fields must cover tool name/version, manifest hash, condition order, generator identity, git HEAD before commit, feature identity, source bead or diff cluster, staged payload hash, condition file hash, subagent model alias, subagent_confirmed, verdict/status, message, evidence, next_action, and timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: Go types and validation functions reject missing cells, fail/unknown in passing artifacts, subagent_confirmed=false, not_applicable without message, stale hashes, missing condition file hash, malformed timestamps, and missing feature identity; docs describe reviewer discovery rules; focused tests cover pass, not_applicable, fail, unknown, stale, and malformed cases; go test ./... passes; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.
close_reason: Defined attestation schema, validation rules, tests, and reviewer docs; verifier subagent Bacon approved with no gaps.

title: PRS-09B Implement pre-commit artifact writing, blocked reports, and staging gate
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-09 artifact responsibilities and Tracked Attestation Artifact. Extend backpressure-commit pre-commit behavior so complete pass/not-applicable matrices write tracked artifacts and incomplete/failing matrices write local blocked reports. Files/patterns: cmd/backpressure-commit/, internal/attestations/, log/backpressure/failed/ generation, backpressure/attestations/ generation. Enforce that the passing attestation must be staged and valid before exit 0; do not silently stage files by default; fail once after generating the artifact with an exact git add command.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: pre-commit mode writes backpressure/attestations/<staged-payload-hash>.json for complete accepted matrices; blocked attempts write log/backpressure/failed/<timestamp>-blocked.json; payload hash excludes generated artifacts; un-staged passing artifact exits non-zero with exact staging instruction; staged valid artifact exits zero; no auto-staging by default; tests cover all exit paths; go test ./... passes; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.
close_reason: Implemented pre-commit artifact engine: passing matrix writes backpressure/attestations/<payload>.json then blocks with exact git add instruction, staged valid artifact passes, missing/failing responses write blocked reports, generated artifacts stay excluded, and no auto-staging occurs. Verified by Rawls: APPROVED no gaps. go test ./... and make build passed.

title: land: 18 AGENTS template refresh
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 2 unit 18 refreshes the generated `AGENTS.md` template so future repositories inherit the clarified operating contract from this landing round.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/scaffold/agents_template.go`
- `internal/scaffold/template_contract_test.go`: only AGENTS-related functions/assertions.

Implementation notes:
- Preserve the canonical Beads Workflow Integration and backpressure language.
- Do not include hook template changes from unit 13 or broad docs refresh from unit 21.

title: land: verify issue #6 init readiness matrix
description:
Background and purpose: This is the mandatory verification cell for issue #6 after unit 12. It proves the setup/init readiness claim in real command contexts before later Phase 2 work continues.

Exact payload file list:

- `tmp=$(mktemp -d); git -C "$tmp" init; (cd "$tmp" && go run <scrubbed-local-repo-path>/cmd/burpvalve init --force --json --no-beads --no-ntm)` expecting success or documented recoverable checks.
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Init|Setup|Repair'`
- `go test ./...`
close_reason:
Issue #6 verification cell completed after land-12 commit 3c2671f.

Source hygiene: commands were run from a clean `git archive HEAD` checkout so the dirty future-unit working tree could not affect the result. The initial attempt to run `go run <scrubbed-temp-path> from inside target dirs failed before Burpvalve ran because Go module resolution used the target cwd; final evidence below uses the clean HEAD source with explicit `--target` temp dirs.

Transcript summary:
- Clean source: <scrubbed-temp-path>
- Non-git target: <scrubbed-temp-path>
- Git target: <scrubbed-temp-path>

Non-git reproduction command:
`(cd <scrubbed-temp-path> && go run ./cmd/burpvalve init --target <scrubbed-temp-path> --force --json --no-beads --no-ntm)`
Result: rc=1. JSON summary: schema_version=1, command=init, status=partial_success, fatal=true, partial_success=true, mutating=true, target_root=<scrubbed-temp-path> `next_steps` contained `.git` with message `git repository is required before installing commit hooks; rerun with --git-init or skip hooks with --no-hooks` and command `burpvalve setup --json`. `conflicts` contained the same `.git` blocker. Stderr: `init <scrubbed-temp-path> init encountered 1 conflict(s)` followed by `exit status 1`. Check: `NON_GIT_HAS_DOT_GIT=no`. This satisfies the non-git requirement: missing git context is explicit/actionable, not a misleading success.

Git-directory reproduction command:
`git -C <scrubbed-temp-path> init -q`, then `(cd <scrubbed-temp-path> && go run ./cmd/burpvalve init --target <scrubbed-temp-path> --force --json --no-beads --no-ntm)`
Result: rc=0. JSON summary: schema_version=1, command=init, status=applied, fatal=false, partial_success=false, mutating=true, target_root=<scrubbed-temp-path>, next_steps=null, conflicts=null. Created list included scaffold files plus `.githooks/pre-commit` and `git config core.hooksPath`. Check: `git -C <scrubbed-temp-path> config --get core.hooksPath` returned `.githooks`; `.githooks/pre-commit` was executable.

Required tests from the same clean HEAD source:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Init|Setup|Repair'` passed (`ok burpvalve/cmd/burpvalve 3.501s`).
- `go test ./...` passed across all packages, including `cmd/burpvalve`, `internal/backpressure`, `internal/config`, and `internal/scaffold`.

No source payload was staged or committed for this verification bead. Later units remain unstarted until this cell is closed.

title: vorch: 09 defer direct-subagent submit mode
description:
Background and purpose: Increment 5 explicitly defers direct-subagent submit mode. The accepted default is that the committing/main agent runs `verifier begin` and `verifier submit`; read-only subagents return verdict JSON and do not mutate the repo.

Acceptance criteria for future activation:

title: lint: Unit C safe manifest writer and recommendations file
description:
Background:
The wizard cannot safely write `backpressure/manifest.yaml` until Burpvalve has a read-modify-write helper that preserves existing project knowledge. No safe YAML patch helper exists today. This bead creates the write path used by both human TUI and robot flows.

Scope:
- Implement manifest read-modify-write for `lint_commands` and `lint_coverage` that preserves existing conditions, existing lint commands, key order, and comments where the YAML library allows.
- Writes must be atomic: temp file plus rename, with no partial manifest on failure.
- Detect duplicate command IDs during proposal. Since `executableLintCommands` fails on duplicates, ask human users to update/skip/rename; robot flow requires explicit per-command `on_conflict` and defaults to skip.
- Make rerunning the wizard with the same selections produce zero diff.
- Write `backpressure/lint-rules.md` recommendations with the same preview/diff/confirm discipline.
- Put recommendations under a marked section `<!-- burpvalve:lint-init recommendations -->` so reruns replace that section instead of duplicating it.
- Treat structural rules as prose recommendations only in the first increment unless backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.

Tests and commands:
- Add unit tests for preserving existing manifest content, atomic write behavior, duplicate IDs, update/skip/rename conflict paths, robot `on_conflict`, idempotent rerun zero diff, and recommendation section replacement.
- Run `go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/backpressure -run 'Test.*ManifestWriter|Test.*LintRules|Test.*Idempotent|Test.*Conflict' -count=1` or actual test names.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- Generated manifest entries are explicit, readable, and easy to remove.
- Preview includes exact manifest diff before writing.
- Wizard rerun idempotency produces zero diff.
- Burpvalve never silently edits project scripts/configs.
- Structural rules are generated recommendations only in first increment and are not claimed as enforcement.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Completed in commit 3c3ff89 (lint: add manifest writer); audit passed after verifier wake recovery
