title: osl: flip public and run anonymous verification suite
description:
Background:
The visibility flip occurs only after private preparation, private launch release, and audit sign-off. Immediately after flipping, anonymous verification must run and any failures must be fixed forward.

Scope:
- Flip repository visibility to public after final pre-flip gate.
- Set repo About metadata: description, topics, homepage.
- Run anonymous verification with no GitHub auth on a clean machine/container.
- Verify anonymous clone.
- Verify both documented install paths on linux amd64; arm64/darwin as available.
- Verify `burpvalve --version`.
- Verify `burpvalve init` end-to-end in a fresh repo.
- Verify badge rendering anonymously.
- Verify release asset downloads anonymously.
- Verify installer with `gh` absent and `gh` installed but unauthenticated.

Acceptance criteria:
- Anonymous user can install and run `burpvalve init` using only public instructions via both paths without `gh`.
- Anonymous clone works.
- Badges render publicly.
- Release assets download publicly.
- `gh` absent and unauthenticated fallback paths work.
- Any failure is recorded and fixed forward before launch is considered complete.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.

Cross-review polish binding clarification - Distinct GitHub CLI verification cases:
The anonymous verification suite must treat these as separate required cases, because they exercise different installer branches and failure messaging:

1. `gh` absent: run the documented installer path in a clean environment where no `gh` binary is discoverable on `PATH` (or an equivalent clean container/image without GitHub CLI). Evidence must show the installer does not require `gh`, falls back to direct public release URLs, downloads the expected linux amd64 archive, and leaves a working `burpvalve --version` plus `burpvalve init` smoke test.

2. `gh` installed but unauthenticated: run the documented installer path with `gh` present but no usable auth state (for example, clean `HOME`/`GH_CONFIG_DIR`, unset `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN`, and `gh auth status` nonzero before the installer). Evidence must show the installer handles unauthenticated `gh release download` failure, falls back to direct public release URLs, and produces the same working `burpvalve --version` plus `burpvalve init` smoke test.

Acceptance clarification:
Passing the no-`gh` case does not prove the unauthenticated-`gh` case, and passing the unauthenticated-`gh` case does not prove the no-`gh` case. Both cases need separate command evidence in the bead close notes or linked verification artifact.
D-OSL-7/D-OSL-8 fresh-history verification:
- Anonymous verification targets the fresh-history public repository named `clicksopendoors/burpvalve`, initialized from the scrubbed current tree as its public initial commit.
- Do not verify by cloning or inspecting this private repository's historical commit graph; the private history remains private.
- Confirm the public repository name is `clicksopendoors/burpvalve` after the private repo has been renamed at flip-prep time and the public repo has been created under the canonical name.
close_reason: Flip executed and verified: private repo renamed private-source-repo, fresh-history public clicksopendoors/burpvalve (single initial commit 37e9b79), About metadata applied per D-OSL-11, PVR enabled and verified (D-OSL-17 deferral closed), v0.2.0 re-cut publicly then v0.2.1 fix-forward after the anonymous suite caught the init-without-br fatal (bead zmh2). Anonymous evidence: clone pass, 9/9 assets + checksums, badges, both installer paths as separate evidence sets (nogh + ghnoauth containers), init smoke PASS on v0.2.1 re-run. Reports: flip-report.json, anonverify/final-report.json incl rerun_v021.

title: rel: write v0.1.2 changelog and ToolVersion comment
description:
Background:
Decision D6 says v0.1.2 is the next release after the land-working-set commits. `docs/CHANGELOG.md` currently ends at v0.1.1, while the landed work introduces multiple user-visible capabilities and fixes. Revision 2 also resolves that `internal/attestations/attestations.go` has `ToolVersion = "0.1.0"` as a schema/tooling constant, not the binary release version; add a code comment so future release agents do not bump it.

title: Improve setup for Git initialization and hook recovery
description:
Background:
The approved direction is to improve setup rather than introduce a separate doctor command. setup already checks repo scaffold state; the gap is that it does not always provide deterministic recovery steps, and git/hook ordering needs to be explicit.

Goal:
Make setup/init/repair explain and enforce the correct order around git initialization and hook wiring.

Scope:
- setup remains inspection-oriented: it may report drift and exact commands, but it should not silently mutate repository state.
- If hooks or hooks-path are requested but .git is missing, Burpvalve must not pretend hook setup succeeded.
- Interactive init should ask whether to initialize Git before wiring hooks when hooks are selected and the target is not yet a Git repo.
- Non-interactive init/repair should require an explicit --git-init flag or return partial/fatal recovery instructions from the structured recovery contract.
- --no-hooks and --no-hooks-path should avoid requiring Git.
- setup --json should include exact commands such as git init and burpvalve repair --force --json hooks-path when applicable.
- The flow must keep the human-facing distinction clear: init creates scaffold, repair fixes existing scaffold/drift, setup reports readiness and recovery steps.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add --git-init to init/repair only if needed; document exact semantics in help and robots help.
- Tests cover non-git repo with hooks selected, non-git repo with hooks skipped, interactive prompt model defaults, --git-init success, and setup next_steps output.
- Existing behavior for already-git repos remains unchanged.
- Generated hook path is only configured after Git exists.
- setup output and setup --json clearly report when hooks were skipped because Git is absent.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Implemented explicit Git initialization and hook-ordering behavior. Evidence: setup remains non-mutating and reports git init / hooks-path recovery next_steps; init/repair expose --git-init and robot git_init input; noninteractive hook-path wiring in non-Git repos blocks unless --git-init is used or hooksPath is skipped; --git-init creates .git before configuring core.hooksPath; --no-hooks and --no-hooks-path avoid Git requirements as appropriate; interactive flows have a Git-init prompt predicate; help/robot help document the option. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; read-only verifier Hume reported PASS after --no-hooks-path recheck.

title: vorch: fix generated-path exclusion over-matching in payload hash and packets
description:
Background and purpose: Live dogfooding finding A15 exposed two related issues in verifier packet and payload-hash handling. `verifier prompts` omitted staged paths under generated evidence prefixes (`backpressure/attestations/` and `log/backpressure/failed/`) from the packet path list, and the same prefix-based exclusion can also exclude legitimately tracked scaffold files such as `backpressure/attestations/README.md` and `log/backpressure/failed/README.md` from the canonical payload hash. `vorch-03` fixes packet visibility by requiring packets to list all staged paths and label hash-excluded paths; this bead fixes the underlying over-matching so generated evidence is excluded without hiding legitimate tracked scaffold/documentation files from payload identity.

Exact payload file list:
- `internal/gitindex/gitindex.go`: replace broad prefix-only generated-path exclusion with a more precise generated-evidence classifier. It must distinguish generated attestation/report artifacts from intentionally tracked scaffold/readme files under the same directories.
- `internal/gitindex/gitindex_test.go`: classifier tests for generated attestation JSON, failed-report files, nested generated artifacts, and legitimate tracked scaffold files such as `backpressure/attestations/README.md` and `log/backpressure/failed/README.md`.
- `internal/backpressure/core.go`: update `HashStagedPayload` to use the precise classifier while preserving deterministic sorted-entry hashing and the existing per-entry hash inputs.
- `internal/backpressure/core_test.go`: payload-hash tests proving legitimate scaffold README files under generated directories affect the hash while actual generated attestation/failed-report artifacts remain excluded.
- `internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts.go` and `internal/backpressure/verifier_prompts_test.go`: if packet code consumes generated-path classification directly, update it to show all staged paths and label only truly hash-excluded generated artifacts.
- `cmd/burpvalve/verifier_test.go` and/or `cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go`: CLI-level regression proving a staged scaffold README under a generated directory is not silently excluded from the payload hash or verifier-visible path set.

Binding behavior:
- Generated evidence files created by Burpvalve during the commit gate remain excluded from payload hashes so the attestation-staging bounce stays stable.
- Legitimately tracked, user-authored or scaffold files inside generated evidence directories must not be excluded merely because their path has a generated prefix.
- The classifier must be conservative. If the code cannot prove a path is generated evidence, include it in the payload hash and show it as an ordinary staged path.
- Packet path lists and payload-hash exclusion must use one shared source of truth, not duplicate string-prefix rules that can drift.
- Do not change the canonical hash format except for the intended inclusion/exclusion decision. Existing sorted order, status normalization, old path handling, raw git status, size, and staged content inputs remain as specified in `HashStagedPayload`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `backpressure/attestations/README.md` and `log/backpressure/failed/README.md` are included in `HashStagedPayload` when staged as intentional scaffold/docs changes.
- Actual generated attestation artifacts and failed reports remain excluded from `HashStagedPayload`.
- Verifier packets list all staged paths; generated/hash-excluded paths are labeled separately; legitimate scaffold README files under generated directories are not labeled hash-excluded.
- Tests cover both tracked README paths named in A15 and at least one generated attestation JSON file.
- No existing attestation-bounce behavior regresses: staging the attestation file named by Burpvalve after response validation still does not change the payload hash that the final gate checks.

Test commands:
- `go test ./internal/gitindex -run 'Generated|Exclusion|Payload|README'`
- `go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'HashStagedPayload|Generated|VerifierPrompt|PayloadPath|Attestation'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Verifier|Commit|Generated|PayloadHash|Attestation'`
- `go test ./...`

Landing/gate reminder: this bead follows `vorch-03` because the prompt contract first defines the user-visible packet semantics. It must land before `verifier begin`/submit work relies on bound payload hashes. Stage only this bead's payload. Verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m vorch-generated-exclusion-verify`, run the listed tests, then `git stash pop`. Run the commit gate from source with `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, gather real verifier responses, stage only the named attestation file, rerun the gate, and commit. Do not fabricate verifier evidence and do not broaden generated exclusions to make tests pass.
close_reason: Completed in commit ba92ad3

description:

Push main only after accepted gated commits; push tag v0.1.3 only after the release-prep commit is on origin/main.\n- Do not commit dist archives or local binaries; GitHub release assets are canonical.\n- Keep license/visibility/launch metadata unchanged; open-source visibility flip remains out of scope.\n- Coordinate GATE-WINDOW before staging any commit; begin by proving git diff --cached --quiet.\n- Use version v0.1.3 everywhere in this chain.\n

description:

title: oxp: add orchestrator config enum schema
description:
Background:
Option B uses a separate `ORCHESTRATOR.md` file, but the install rule depends on new config semantics. Current `internal/config` scaffold defaults are bool-only fields under `defaults.init` and `defaults.repair`; Revision 3 requires a string enum for init plus a bool for repair.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Config schema (exact): new string-enum field `defaults.init.orchestrator` with values `"off" | "orchestrator-md" | "claude-md"` (default `"off"`; `"claude-md"` is rejected with a not-yet-supported error until the deferred option-A bead ships), plus bool `defaults.repair.orchestrator` (repair touches the file only when true or when explicitly targeted). Extends `internal/config` Merge/Normalize/Validate and `noteDefaultsSources` for a non-bool field — schema work, named as such."

Scope:
- Add config model fields for `defaults.init.orchestrator` and `defaults.repair.orchestrator`.
- Preserve `DisallowUnknownFields`; do not accept ad hoc sidecar schema.
- Validate init enum values exactly: `off`, `orchestrator-md`, `claude-md`.
- Reject `claude-md` with a clear not-yet-supported error until the deferred option-A bead ships.
- Default init orchestrator mode to `off`.
- Add merge, normalize, source reporting, config command value reporting, robot docs, and tests for the non-bool field.
- Add repair bool merge/source/reporting support.

Implementation notes:
- This is schema work, not a skip flag.
- Keep bool scaffold defaults behavior unchanged for existing keys.
- Use exact persisted paths `defaults.init.orchestrator` and `defaults.repair.orchestrator`; do not introduce top-level `init.orchestrator`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Config load/merge/source output reports global and project sources for both new keys.
- Invalid enum values fail validation with actionable messages.
- `claude-md` fails with a not-yet-supported error that points to deferred option A rather than silently falling back.
- Existing config fixtures continue to pass.
- Robot/help/config output documents the exact values.

Tests:
- `go test ./internal/config -run 'Orchestrator|ScaffoldDefaults|Validate|Merge|Sources'`
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Config|Setup|Init|Repair|Robot|Help|Orchestrator'`
- `go test ./...`

title: Apply config defaults across Burpvalve commands
description:
Background:
Burpvalve now has a durable global/project config model and a guided config init flow. The next user-facing gap is making later commands actually respect those saved defaults without hiding what came from config or overriding explicit flags.

Goal:
Make Burpvalve command prompts and defaults consistently consume global/project configuration, with project config overriding global config and explicit CLI flags overriding both.

Scope:
- Apply config defaults to human prompts and noninteractive defaults for setup, init, repair, completion, and install-related flows where those commands already ask about the same setting.
- Show the source of remembered defaults in human output when it changes what the user sees, such as "from global config" or "from .burpvalve.json".
- Keep explicit flags highest priority. A flag must never be ignored because config exists.
- Keep project config scoped to project behavior; global install/PATH/completion choices remain personal defaults unless explicitly written to a project config.
- Do not make config init run setup/init/repair/completion automatically. Config only remembers choices.
- Do not persist machine-detected guesses from later commands unless the user confirms writing config.
- Return config source facts in JSON/robots output so agents can explain why a default was selected.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover global defaults, project-overrides-global defaults, explicit flag override, missing config fallback, and invalid config recovery for setup/init/repair/completion/install where applicable.
- Human output identifies configured defaults without adding noisy prose to every line.
- JSON/robots output exposes effective value and source for settings that affect behavior.
- No command writes config as a side effect unless it is a config command or an explicit confirmed write-config action.
- README and skill docs explain that config changes future prompts/defaults but does not mutate repos by itself.
- go test ./... and make build pass.
design:
Plan audit revision 2:
- This bead exists to keep config init from becoming a hidden mega-feature. Config init writes settings; this bead makes other commands read those settings.
- Implement precedence once through the config loader: explicit CLI flag or explicit command target > project config > global config > built-in default. Positional targets such as init docs must count as explicit intent and must not be expanded by config defaults.
- Add compact source labels only where they help the user understand why a default appeared. Prefer a short "Config defaults" summary over provenance text in every prompt.
- For mutating flows, a configured default may preselect an answer but must not remove the final confirmation. Confirmation still defaults to No unless --force or robots confirm=true is used.
- For robots, expose effective values and source fields so agents can make deterministic choices without scraping human text.
- Treat repo-local binary fallback, global PATH binary, skills dir, shell, completion paths, scaffold toggles, repair toggles, color, and confirmation defaults as the main settings surface.
- Invalid config should fail with a structured recovery message that names the file and field, rather than falling back silently. Missing config should be quiet and use built-in defaults.
- Commands must not write config as a side effect. Any future write-config action must be explicit, previewed, and confirmed.
close_reason: Implemented config-default consumption across setup/init/repair/completion flows. Added effective config settings with source/value in JSON and compact human summaries; project overrides global config, explicit targets/input override config, invalid config fails before mutation, and setup/init/repair/completion do not write config as side effects. Completion robot mode now emits structured JSON with shell/path source facts, and human completion guide labels configured shells instead of mislabeling them as detected. README and skill docs state config changes future defaults/robot facts and does not mutate repos by itself. Evidence: focused config/default tests pass; go test ./... passes; make build and git diff --check pass; read-only verifier Arendt PASS.

title: oxp: final acceptance gate for orchestrator experience
description:
Background:
This final gate confirms the orchestrator-experience plan shipped as a coherent product surface: option-B contract file, prompt bank, evidence surfaces, NTM guidance, and skill references. It intentionally excludes deferred option A.

Coverage required against Revision 3 Resolutions:
1. Option A deferred wholesale to p4, with the nine-case truth table as first future deliverable.
2. Option B install rule exact: not in standard targets; explicit target, config non-off, or visible unchecked TUI checkbox when Beads/NTM selected; never silent.
3. Config schema exact: `defaults.init.orchestrator` enum `off|orchestrator-md|claude-md`, `claude-md` rejected until option A ships, plus `defaults.repair.orchestrator` bool.
4. Prompt-bank command contract exact: stable names, `--var`, JSON shape, `--write`, metadata content hash, local modification refusal, divergence warning, repair boundary, hostile variable tests.
5. `verifier-bootstrap` prompt exists and covers fresh verifier arrival.
6. `ci --commit` contract exact: CLI/robots flag, artifact/provenance JSON, artifact-bound feature discovery, `--feature` assertion-only.
7. `BURPVALVE_BEAD` parse rules exact: comma-separated, trim, reject empty, dedupe warning, no commas inside IDs, shell hook tests.
8. Drift guard structural: `ORCHESTRATOR.md` references AGENTS gate/DoD and does not duplicate forbidden headings/rules/verdict vocabulary.

Additional acceptance:
- `hash --staged` helper is either implemented or explicitly absorbed as already landed, with no duplicate helper.
- Hook-context-aware next steps are either implemented or explicitly absorbed as already landed.
- NTM peer-pane guidance and skill prompt-bank references are updated.
- No workflow-engine behavior is introduced into Burpvalve.
- Prompt and docs text remains paste-safe under D4.
- `br dep cycles` is empty and `.beads/issues.jsonl` has been flushed.

Verification commands:
- `go test ./...`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve prompts list` after prompts command exists.
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve ci --help`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci -h`
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve hash --staged --help` if the helper is implemented under that command shape.
- `rg -n "defaults.init.orchestrator|defaults.repair.orchestrator|orchestrator-md|claude-md" internal cmd docs`
- `rg -n "verifier-bootstrap|commit-choreography|orchestrator-tick|bead-conversion" .`
- `rg -n -- ' -> ' docs skill internal/scaffold/templates || true` and manually confirm no unsafe workflow chains.
- `br dep cycles`
- `br sync --flush-only`

Close criteria:
- Every non-deferred oxp bead is closed or explicitly marked absorbed with rationale.
- This final gate records the coverage check result and any remaining risks.
- Deferred option A remains p4/deferred and is not required for closure.
close_reason: OXP final acceptance passed after fixing verifier-caught blocker #12. Non-deferred OXP implementation beads are closed; only project-readiness-kit-oxp-defer-option-a-truth-table-me5 remains deferred p4 as intended. Original acceptance checks passed: go test ./..., prompts list, ci help, robot ci help, hash --staged help, config/prompt/paste-safety greps, and br dep cycles. Blocker fix landed in commit 92a8e17 (bug: fix ci commit feature assertion), pushed to origin/main, with attestation backpressure/attestations/e2332d370b939efb9f151dba4ec8b9b83277ac904c587208482cf09e95878135.json. Post-fix commit-audit checks pass for f3c4a46 + project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu, dc8c2ee + project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8, and 92a8e17 + project-readiness-kit-ci-commit-multicluster-feature-9hf. br dep cycles clean.

title: oxp: forward BURPVALVE_BEAD with exact parse rules through hooks
description:
Background:
Orchestrators need the git hook to carry delivery bead metadata into `burpvalve commit` without retyping repeated flags. Existing `commit` accepts repeated `--bead` flags and requires rationale for multiple beads. Revision 3 defines exact env parsing rules for hook forwarding.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"`BURPVALVE_BEAD` parse rules: comma-separated; tokens trimmed; empty tokens rejected with a named error; duplicates deduplicated with a warning; commas are not permitted inside bead IDs (validated). Mapping to repeated `--bead` flags is covered by shell-level hook tests in both template copies."

Absorb rule:
Before implementing, check whether bead env forwarding or parse rules landed in another current-round bead. Absorb already-landed fixes by documenting/reusing them; do not duplicate parser helpers or hook tests.

Scope:
- Update both hook template copies and this repo's live hook if applicable: `templates/githooks/pre-commit`, `internal/scaffold/templates/githooks/pre-commit`, and `.githooks/pre-commit` when this repo needs the behavior.
- Parse `BURPVALVE_BEAD` as comma-separated IDs.
- Trim tokens.
- Reject empty tokens with a named/actionable error.
- Deduplicate duplicate IDs and emit a warning.
- Validate that bead IDs do not contain commas.
- Map parsed IDs to repeated `--bead` flags.
- Forward `BURPVALVE_BEAD_RATIONALE` to `--bead-rationale`.
- Multiple IDs without rationale must fail exactly as `commit` fails today without `--bead-rationale`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Single bead env maps to one `--bead` flag.
- Multiple bead env maps to repeated `--bead` flags in order after trimming/deduplication.
- Empty token errors are named and tested.
- Duplicate token warning is visible without duplicating metadata.
- Comma inside bead ID is rejected by validation.
- Hook template tests cover both template copies and live hook behavior when applicable.

Tests:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Bead|BeadRationale|Hook|Env|Duplicate|Comma|Empty'`
- Shell-level hook tests for both template copies.
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Implemented and pushed in commit 8520bfb (oxp: forward BURPVALVE_BEAD through hooks); attestation backpressure/attestations/07693e14c8e4d1dd4152ce14240fb9150debf991dbe52b8fd1b9b44ac55edf67.json validates the hook-forwarding payload.

title: oxp: orchestrator experience post-current-round start gate
description:
Background:
This is the root/start-gate bead for the approved Revision 3 orchestrator-experience plan. Burpvalve currently serves the committing coder well, but the orchestrator role also needs first-class product surfaces: an installable role contract, canonical prompt bank, commit-scoped evidence audit commands, and documented NTM/Agent Mail verifier patterns. Owner direction D12 says the orchestrator is a Burpvalve user; D11 says independently checked, well documented work can proceed without pre-approval except for irreversible/outward-facing actions.

Execution order:
This plan executes only after the current implementation round terminals are complete. This root bead is intentionally wired to depend on exactly these four terminal beads:
- project-readiness-kit-rel-final-gate-8cu
- project-readiness-kit-lint-final-acceptance-hif
- project-readiness-kit-vorch-07-verifier-doctor-report-only-a6i
- project-readiness-kit-bcm-06-attestation-keyed-drift-check-5th

Scope summary:
- Ship option B only: `ORCHESTRATOR.md` alongside `CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md`.
- Add exact orchestrator config schema and install rules from Revision 3.
- Add `burpvalve prompts` with stable prompt-name API, `--var`, JSON, safe export, and `verifier-bootstrap`.
- Add evidence surfaces: `ci --commit`, `hash --staged` if not already landed, `BURPVALVE_BEAD` forwarding, and hook-context-aware next steps.
- Update `docs/ntm-bridge.md` with calibrated peer-pane verifier guidance.
- Refactor the skill to cite the prompt bank while retaining enough bootstrap text for first-install users.
- Defer option A (`CLAUDE.md` as orchestrator contract) wholesale to a p4 backlog bead.

Non-goals:
- Burpvalve must not become a workflow engine: it does not spawn panes, send mail, or monitor sessions.
- No new enforcement claims beyond the commit gate; prompts/contracts are guidance surfaces.
- No public launch or license/visibility work.

Acceptance criteria:
- All oxp implementation beads are linked under this plan and eventually covered by the final oxp acceptance bead.
- This root remains blocked until the four current-round terminal beads are complete.
- `br dep cycles` is empty after dependency wiring.
- The plan's Revision 3 Resolutions are represented in individual beads so implementers do not need to reopen the markdown plan.

Verification commands:
- `br show <this-id> --json`
- `br dep tree <this-id>`
- `br dep cycles`
- `br list --json --limit 300`
close_reason: OXP chain opened early by owner direction after rel/lint/vorch terminals closed; BCM terminal dependency explicitly removed to use idle capacity. br dep cycles clean before closure.

title: lexicon: add README pitch and glossary section
description:
Background:
README is the product entry point. The approved lexicon plan requires Burpvalve's pitch to keep backpressure as the anchor concept while adding a compact glossary that makes valve, burp, seal/attestation, work unit, feature, and bead unsurprising to readers.

Depends on:
- Canonical D-LEX decisions and docs/vocabulary.md from the first lexicon bead.

Scope:
- Update the README opening pitch so it still says Burpvalve installs repo-local backpressure or otherwise names backpressure as the reason the product exists.
- Add a compact README lexicon section that links to docs/vocabulary.md.
- Include short inline definitions:
  - valve: the fail-closed commit gate.
  - burp: a gate refusal.
  - seal: a Burpvalve attestation.
  - work unit: the atomic thing being checked.
  - feature: the stable CLI/schema binding term for an existing work-unit payload.
  - bead: a Beads/br tracker issue, one possible tracker-backed work unit.
- Replace conceptual product-doc uses of bead with work unit where the text is not specifically about Beads/br.
- Preserve Beads/br references where discussing tracker behavior, bead IDs, bead rationale, close/preflight behavior, or .beads paths.
- Keep OSL launch scrub wording out of this bead except where a lexicon sentence is already being touched.

Out of scope:
- No command/help/error implementation.
- No public visibility, license, launch, or contribution-policy scrub.
- No rename of flags, commands, schema fields, file paths, or generated evidence names.

Acceptance criteria:
- README pitch names backpressure.
- README has a short lexicon section and links to docs/vocabulary.md.
- README does not define seal without attestation.
- README uses work unit for the generic product concept and bead only for Beads-specific text.
- README preserves feature/--feature as stable CLI/schema language.
- README Beads examples explain bead as a tracker-backed work unit rather than erasing the tool term.

Verification:
- git diff --check -- README.md
- rg -n 'backpressure|valve|burp|seal|attestation|work unit|feature|bead|Beads|br' README.md
- If README examples or generated help snippets are changed, run the relevant README/help tests; otherwise record docs-only verification.
close_reason: Delivered README glossary + pitch line. Commit 6c70f04 pushed to origin/main. Attestation: backpressure/attestations/684f5cf2423e6f84a05e863f327eb645f23562103f64c08e8720ef9dd851dd72.json. Staged payload hash sha256:684f5cf2423e6f84a05e863f327eb645f23562103f64c08e8720ef9dd851dd72. Verifier packet/replies: BrightOwl Agent Mail 3280/3284, ScarletMarsh Agent Mail 3281/3283. Gate summary: README.md-only payload, all 13 conditions pass or N/A; no code/schema/robot JSON/OSL scrub changes.

title: land: 05 import historical beads state
description:
Background and purpose: Phase 1 commit 5 imports the existing Beads tracker state as tracker history, not as proof of landed code. The plan explicitly forbids rewriting or renaming old beads to make history look cleaner.

Exact payload file list:
- `.beads/issues.jsonl`

Implementation notes:
- Stage the Beads JSONL exactly as tracker state at this point in the landing sequence.
- Historical closed beads may predate the commit history and may reference older project names; preserve them as records.
- Fresh `land:` beads created for this landing sequence are the only delivery beads that later commits may cite. No later landing commit may cite an old historical bead as its delivery bead.
- If this bead conversion has already added fresh `land:` beads to `.beads/issues.jsonl`, preserve that tracker reality and do not edit JSONL by hand.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- Only `.beads/issues.jsonl` is staged for this commit.
- The file is produced by `br sync --flush-only`, not manual JSON editing.
- The commit is treated as an administrative tracker import and not as implementation evidence.
- Later implementation beads are current `land:` beads, not historical records.

Test commands:
- `git status --short`
- `br dep cycles`
- `br sync --flush-only`
- `git diff --cached -- .beads/issues.jsonl`
- staged-slice verification with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-05-verify`, `go test ./...`, `git stash pop`.
close_reason: Completed in commit 7bc2172a3c92583a0b6afc78695c124c4db4abcc with tracked-rule Burpvalve attestation backpressure/attestations/32a56dd1eebc599b3b4579adb3c46cda3916518bc47b18892b350c134a56d463.json.

title: osl: audit and clean tracked docs plans and D4 prose
description:
Background:
The public repo exposes all tracked docs and plans. Some files currently contain local paths, private wording, or historical project names. Owner thresholds decide whether history remains, but current public docs must not be misleading.

Scope:
- Audit `plans/GOAL.md`, `docs/CHANGELOG_RESEARCH.md`, `docs/release-install.md`, skill references, `SELF-TEST.md`, README, docs index, and all tracked docs/plans.
- Rewrite current operational docs for public audience where needed, especially private install wording and machine-specific examples.
- Remove remaining bare `->` chains from public prose per D4.
- Preserve accepted harmless history only where clearly historical.

Acceptance criteria:
- Current public-facing docs contain no stale private install instructions.
- `docs/release-install.md` is public-safe or moved/scoped as private historical documentation.
- README has no bare `->` workflow chains.
- Historical files that retain local paths/legacy names are either accepted by threshold or clearly marked historical.
Open-source launch ordering constraint:
This plan executes LAST. Do not start this bead until all other current-round plans are complete, tested, and released as applicable: land-working-set, release v0.1.2, issue-17 lint wizard, verifier orchestration, and bead-closure mode. The owner must explicitly declare the feature set complete before launch work begins. RusticDog owns cross-plan dependency wiring; this bead only carries intra-plan dependencies.

Global launch constraints:
- Prepare all docs, metadata, license, audit, installer, community, and release work while the repo is still private.
- Exact visibility order: prepare everything, cut the launch release privately, flip visibility, immediately run anonymous verification, fix-forward anything found.
- Do not flip first. Proprietary/private metadata must never become visible publicly.
- No feature work belongs in this plan.
- Do not document `go install`; current `go.mod` is `module burpvalve`, and module-path migration is out of scope.
- Do not claim Windows support. Release assets are linux/darwin x amd64/arm64 only.
- Keep the no-outside-contributions policy, but remove/update private/proprietary wording and ensure the tone reads as maintainer policy.
close_reason: Audit findings delivered in Agent Mail 3084; remediation landed in scrub commit 2bf5bbd with attestation backpressure/attestations/8866a766e83eadffb2486f637eec65ba2b8bbd4b7318a4454c20d5602fa7f5c1.json. Closed as tracker hygiene after OSL scrub.

title: oxp: codify model/effort tiering in shipped orchestrator templates
description: Add the C13 effort-tiering policy to ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl and the burpvalve-orchestrator skill (likely verifier-fanout-and-attestations or a spawn/dispatch section): reasoning effort follows cell/task type, not role title, and is set deliberately at spawn, never left to runtime defaults. Tiers: critical-path implementer xhigh; judgment-cell verifier (dry, anti-reward-hacking, scope-control, security-boundaries, definition-of-done) high; mechanical-cell verifier and inventory/lookup work medium. Rationale: verifier effort burns the shared account window the implementer needs; verifier value-add concentrates in judgment cells (source: docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md C13). Include: record each pane's model+effort in dispatch notes; adjust running panes only between rounds; respawn at the right tier for long mechanical phases. Owner ruling 2026-07-05, mirrored in this repo's ORCHESTRATOR.md.
close_reason: Landed in commit ca82a9c with attestation backpressure/attestations/a5c1ce23df01cbb0de447e20aff33d048f0ba316c835efddb07aae8d85980e53.json. Verifier evidence: log/backpressure/responses/a5c1ce23df01cbb0de447e20aff33d048f0ba316c835efddb07aae8d85980e53.json with 11 pass and 2 not_applicable cells from GrayCat/CloudyEagle. go test ./... passed before gate; hook revalidated staged attestation.

title: Polish setup readiness summary and recovery guidance
description:
Background:
The approved limited #8 direction is to improve burpvalve setup instead of adding a separate doctor command. Closed foundation work already added structured recovery fields, Git/hook ordering, and separate PATH vs repo-local tool readiness facts. What is still missing is a single setup-owned pass that makes those facts feel like a coherent readiness report instead of scattered diagnostics.

Goal:
Make burpvalve setup the obvious, non-mutating command a human or agent runs to understand whether a repo is ready, what is missing, what is optional, and exactly what to run next.

Scope:
- Keep setup read-only. It may inspect Git/config/PATH/files/hooks/scaffold state and print commands, but it must not initialize Git, repair files, install completions, or change config.
- Present human output in clear sections with a compact two-column shape where useful: repository, Burpvalve command resolution, scaffold, Git hooks, backpressure artifacts, optional integrations, config sources, and next steps.
- Show global PATH command and repo-local bin/burpvalve as separate facts, including which one hooks will actually be able to run.
- Distinguish required, optional, ready, drifted, skipped, unavailable, and unknown states without pretending optional integrations are fatal.
- Prefer exact next commands over prose: burpvalve init, burpvalve repair, burpvalve completion verify, git init, br install guidance when br is missing, and command/source guidance when PATH has not refreshed.
- Use configured defaults and source attribution from the config foundation where relevant, without rewriting config.
- Preserve stable setup --json fields from the structured recovery contract; add fields only when needed for readiness facts instead of forcing agents to scrape human text.
- Keep robot help aligned with the JSON contract and explicitly say setup is inspection-only.
- Do not reintroduce project registry references and do not add a doctor command.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover clean ready repo, non-Git repo, missing global burpvalve command, PATH-only burpvalve, repo-local fallback only, ignored repo-local fallback, missing hooks path, optional beads/ntm unavailable, project config overriding global config, and skipped scaffold pieces.
- Human output has readable section headers, no wall-of-text dump, and names M/D or any status shorthand it uses.
- setup --json exposes separate command_path, repo_bin_path, hook_command_source or equivalent stable facts, config source details, readiness severity, and next_steps.
- setup output recommends repair/init/completion/git commands only when they apply and never claims to have run them.
- go test ./... passes.
close_reason: Polished setup readiness reporting while keeping setup read-only. Added explicit readiness_severity, command_path, repo_bin_path, hook_command_source, config source details, stable next_steps JSON, human config sources, and human next-step command sections. Fixed recovery command target mapping so internal check IDs emit valid repair targets such as attestations. Evidence: focused setup/scaffold/help tests passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; make build passed; git diff --check passed. Read-only verifier Herschel found two issues, both fixed, then rechecked PASS.

title: PRS-00A Create canonical JSM skill package wrapper
description: Derived from GOAL.md PRS-00A. Create the canonical skill source at <scrubbed-local-path> while keeping Go source of truth in <scrubbed-local-repo-path>. Files/patterns: <scrubbed-local-path>, scripts/setup-project.sh, any minimal metadata required by local JSM conventions. The skill should be an agent-facing wrapper that invokes the Go setup tool, documents check/init/repair behavior, points to the source repo, and avoids binary-only distribution as the source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: skill directory exists outside this repo at the canonical path; SKILL.md is complete enough for an agent to run check/init/repair; script invokes project-readiness-kit source or built binary transparently; no opaque compiled binary is the only source of truth; cd <scrubbed-local-path> && jsm validate skills/setup-command-project passes or a precise environmental blocker is recorded; verifier subagent reviews this bead and reports no gaps before close.

title: ifr2-C1: define payload ownership input schema and result contract
description:
Background:
This bead is Unit C1 from the approved issue-followups round-2 plan. It is the first split bead for issue draft 8 / dogfooding finding A16. A16 came from orchestration load: staged hash identity proves what payload is staged, but it does not prove every staged or untracked path belongs to the right bead, nor whether split-file work was declared.

Scope:
- Define the ownership input schema, parser, validation rules, and result/status contract for a later read-only payload accounting command.
- This bead should focus on data structures and tests; command integration belongs to C2, Beads enrichment/docs/help belongs to C3.
- The first implementation uses explicit metadata only. It accepts data that later C2 can read from stdin JSON and/or --ownership-file, but this bead need not wire the CLI.

Binding ownership input fields:
- unit_id: required.
- bead_id: optional.
- path: required repo-relative path.
- ownership_kind: required enum whole_path, function, test, hunk, generated, admin, or exception.
- symbol: optional, for function/type/method ownership.
- test_pattern: optional.
- hunk_label: optional.
- source: required; examples include plan path, bead id, stdin, or explicit review packet.
- rationale: required for shared/conflict-prone paths and exceptions.
- expires_or_scope: optional enum single_bead, plan_round, or until_commit. Owner ruling requires this to be structured from round 1.
- expires_note: optional free-form context for expires_or_scope.

Binding source precedence for later command behavior:
- Explicit stdin JSON wins over --ownership-file when both provide the same unit/path/kind claim.
- --ownership-file wins over display-only Beads enrichment.
- Beads may identify active/open/in-progress tasks and show bead metadata, but must not create ownership claims unless a future accepted plan names a specific authoritative Beads field.

Binding output statuses:
- owned
- shared_declared
- conflict
- unowned
- generated_exception
- ignored_untracked
- covered_exception

Likely file touches:
- new package under internal/backpressure/ or internal/accounting/ for ownership schema and validation
- package tests and testdata if needed
- no cmd/burpvalve/main.go wiring in this bead unless implementation review proves a tiny fixture-only hook is unavoidable.

Tests required:
- go test ./internal/... -run "Account|Payload|Owner|Schema|Contract|Scope|Expiration"
- go test ./...

Required scenarios:
- rejects missing unit_id;
- rejects missing path;
- rejects invalid ownership_kind;
- rejects invalid expires_or_scope;
- accepts expires_or_scope values single_bead, plan_round, until_commit;
- accepts optional expires_note;
- rejects exception/shared conflict-prone records without rationale;
- normalizes repo-relative paths consistently and rejects absolute paths or traversal if the package owns path normalization;
- preserves source fields for audit output.

Acceptance criteria:
- A small, tested schema/parser/result package exists for ownership records and statuses.
- Round-1 schema is explicit and structured, especially expires_or_scope.
- No implicit durable repo-local ownership config is introduced.
- No Beads-derived ownership claims are inferred.

Non-goals:
- No CLI command.
- No staged/untracked git classification.
- No Beads enrichment.
- No mutation of Beads, reservations, staged files, or working tree state.
close_reason: Completed in 2297b3e (pushed). Attestation backpressure/attestations/8cf10f63c90dc66bd42cb84a5306fe147673b99f1e5ab92f4195d6c94cf925a3.json. Verifier replies: BrightOwl 3302 judgment/high pass; ScarletMarsh 3297/3299 mechanical/shared pass. Gate passed with BURPVALVE_FEATURE=project-readiness-kit-ifr2-c1-ownership-schema-contract-vr1.

title: oxp: implement prompts command API and JSON contract
description:
Background:
Orchestrators and coders improvised repeated prompts during the landing round: marching orders, verifier briefings, review packets, packet status messages, and bead-conversion assignments. The `burpvalve prompts` command makes these canonical, versioned, and robot-readable instead of session folklore.

Binding Revision 3 Resolution:
"Prompt-bank command contract (exact): prompt names are a stable public API (renames are breaking changes). `prompts show <name> [--var key=value ...]`: missing required variables is a usage error listing them; `--json` emits `{name, version, variables: [{name, required, description}], body}`. `--write` exports to `docs/prompts/<name>.md` with a metadata header (burpvalve version, prompt name, content hash); it refuses to overwrite an export whose content hash shows local modification unless `--force`; `repair` never touches `docs/prompts/`. `prompts show` warns when a differing local export exists. Rendering is tested with hostile variable values."

Scope:
- Add top-level `burpvalve prompts` command with `list` and `show <name>` subcommands.
- Store prompts embedded in the binary, versioned with the release.
- Treat prompt names as stable public API; rename/removal requires explicit breaking-change handling.
- Implement `--var key=value` rendering for required/optional variables.
- Missing required variables must fail as usage errors and list all missing names.
- `--json` for `show` emits exactly `{name, version, variables: [{name, required, description}], body}`.
- Robot help documents command shape, variables, JSON output, and the distinction from `burpvalve verifier prompts`.
- Help text explicitly distinguishes canonical templates (`burpvalve prompts`) from verifier packets generated from the current staged payload (`burpvalve verifier prompts`).

Non-goals:
- Do not make Burpvalve spawn agents, panes, or send mail.
- Do not create repo-local prompt files by default; export behavior is separate.

Acceptance criteria:
- `burpvalve prompts list` prints stable names and descriptions in human and robot-friendly output.
- `burpvalve prompts show <name>` renders body deterministically.
- `--json` exactly follows the promised shape.
- Required variable errors are complete and actionable.
- Unknown prompt names produce suggestions or a clear list of valid names.
- Command docs make the namespace distinction impossible to miss.

Tests:
- `go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Prompts|PromptBank|Robot|Help|JSON|Vars|Usage'`
- `go test ./internal/... -run 'Prompt|Render|Variable'` if a helper package is introduced.
- `go run ./cmd/burpvalve prompts list --json` or robot equivalent if implemented.
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Completed in commit 711063d (oxp: add prompts command API), pushed to origin/main; gate accepted attestation sha256:765cda42017ef7921e4a58ad15152c745ddda2f0e18736c2fc549158006262aa.

title: lint: Unit A schema and runtime foundation
description:
Background:
Current reality from revision 2: `internal/lintconfig/lintconfig.go` defines only `id`, `command`, `required`, `paths`, and `timeout_seconds`; `internal/backpressure/core.go` silently ignores unknown YAML keys; and `internal/backpressure/lint.go` always runs commands with `cmd.Dir = root`. Every later issue-17 promise is unsafe until schema and runtime foundation exists.

Scope:
- Extend `lintconfig.Command` with optional `run_directory` and optional `serial`.
- Extend manifest loading with top-level `lint_coverage` containing `declined_roots` and `declined_at`.
- Reject unknown per-command keys and unknown fields inside known sections. A typo such as `run_dir:` must be a named error, not a silent no-op. Top-level unknown sections may warn rather than fail for forward compatibility.
- Validate `run_directory` lexically after cleaning: repo-relative, no absolute path, no `..`, and contained inside target root. Do not attempt symlink resolution.
- At lint run time, missing `run_directory` fails that command with `status: failed` and a clear error, not a crash. At manifest write time, warn if the directory does not exist.
- Set `cmd.Dir` to validated `run_directory`, defaulting to repo root (`.`).
- Keep `paths` and `BACKPRESSURE_LINT_PATHS` repo-root-relative even when `run_directory` is set. This prevents `apps/web/apps/web` ambiguity.
- Preserve legacy `cd <dir> && <cmd>` command support forever. Do not migrate existing `cd` commands automatically.
- Add `coverage` and `unchecked_roots` fields to `LintResult` to support declined scoped setup.

Tests and commands:
- Add focused tests around manifest parse/validation and lint runtime execution.
- Run `go test ./internal/lintconfig ./internal/backpressure -run 'Test.*RunDirectory|Test.*LintCoverage|Test.*Unknown' -count=1` or the actual package/test names chosen.
- Run `go test ./...` before close.

Acceptance criteria covered here:
- `run_directory` is validated as a relative path inside the target repo.
- Unknown per-command manifest keys are rejected with a named error.
- `run_directory` positive/negative cases: valid subdir, `.`, absolute, `..`, nonexistent-at-run-time.
- Legacy `cd`-style command continues to work unchanged alongside `run_directory` commands in one manifest.
- `paths` and `BACKPRESSURE_LINT_PATHS` remain repo-root-relative with `run_directory` set.
- `lint_coverage.declined_roots` validates repo-relative paths and no traversal.
Common constraints for every issue-17 lint bead:
- Primary command shape is `burpvalve lint init`; do not rename it to `configure` or hide it under `config init`.
- `burpvalve init` may later delegate to this model, but this plan does not make lint setup a required part of init.
- Do not install new developer dependencies without explicit user confirmation.
- Do not silently edit `package.json`, formatter configs, CI files, hooks, or project scripts.
- Do not claim a lint rule is enforced unless a command or analyzer actually runs.
- Do not implement Python, Rust, CI workflow parsing, generic file-size/artifact checks, or built-in structural analyzers in the first increment.
- Structural rules are recommendations only in the first increment and live as prose in `backpressure/lint-rules.md` until backed by a real command/analyzer explicitly enabled by the user.
- Built-in presets may ship in the binary for Go and Node/Astro. User-defined preset storage in global/project config is deferred because `internal/config` uses `DisallowUnknownFields` and needs real schema work.
- Declined scoped setup warns and records reduced coverage; it must not block. Burpvalve refuses unverified claims, not explicitly accepted reduced scope.
- Use `br list --json --limit 200` when auditing this plan's beads so the default list limit does not hide work.
close_reason: Completed in commit 811d5aa62468320544b307da080bede761f00c2f

title: GH-3 expose commit responses JSON schema and template
description: Fix GitHub #3 by making non-interactive commit response schema discoverable. Add burpvalve commit --responses-template --feature <id> for the current staged condition matrix, include compact schema guidance in --robots -h, point human commit -h at the template command, and validate missing top-level conditions with a direct error.

title: init: missing br executable is fatal instead of skipping beads setup
description: Found by the post-flip anonymous verification suite (both containers, deterministic): in a clean environment with no beads_rust installed, 'burpvalve init --force --json' in a fresh git repo fails fatally at .beads setup instead of skipping the optional Beads integration. Contract (AGENTS.md scaffold pieces): '.beads/ when br is available and not skipped' - absence of br must mean graceful skip with a clear JSON-reported notice, not a fatal error. Evidence: scratch anonverify/final-report.json + run logs. Fix-forward on the PUBLIC repo per launch plan; mirror to private. Acceptance: failing test reproducing the no-br environment (PATH-isolated), fix, both anonymous containers re-run PASS on the init smoke.
close_reason: Fixed and released in v0.2.1. Private fix 08279d967a9f594330c0aa4754c5a1f6392b7c1d with attestation backpressure/attestations/49ef1a2d420e787b06c42916414b6d09f47bb0f8c19818d878901b2b7b90a169.json; public fix e2792e39f037fd7963a84013923e1ca6d327eb21; private bump 2935f2fd8a806ea82f599b961f4e9c89de38b92e with attestation backpressure/attestations/a2915090794d18400dad5b178ade951169f9474843838ea798bee6e01d4c796a.json; public bump/tag a27e962eac677bb957648060218af2372003e1d1; release https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/releases/tag/v0.2.1.

title: land: verify issue #16 arrow workflow sanitization
description:
Background and purpose: This is the mandatory verification cell for issue #16 after unit 19. It proves the original arrow-workflow defect and confirms the sanitized tree leaves only allowed arrow cases.

Exact payload file list:
- No source payload required by default. If a durable record is needed, use the relevant attestation artifact or a narrowly scoped issue comment/log; do not add broad docs changes here.

Required verification:
- Reproduce the scratch arrow workflow line from issue #16 before/using the historical form and confirm it creates `verify`, `close`, and `commit` artifacts or otherwise demonstrates the hazard.
- After unit 19, run a repo-wide grep for `-> ` in prose/output contexts and classify all remaining hits as allowed symlink notation or allowed non-copy-paste code block content.
- Comment on issue #16 with the before/after evidence.

Gate execution contract reminder:
This bead is after the tracked gate exists. Before committing, stage only the exact payload listed here and verify the staged slice with `git stash push --keep-index --include-untracked -m land-verify`; run the listed commands; then `git stash pop`. The active hook must run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve`/`go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit`, never the stale repo-local binary. For attestation, gather real verifier responses for the active feature x condition cells, run `go run ./cmd/burpvalve commit --responses <file> --bead <id>`, stage only the attestation file it names, rerun the same gate so the attestation covers the final staged payload, and then commit. Never fabricate verifier evidence, never reuse an attestation after changing staged content, and never stage `.beads/issues.jsonl` between attestation creation and the final rerun.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verification uses real shell/repo commands, not hypothetical reasoning.
- Remaining `-> ` hits are enumerated and justified or removed.
- Issue #16 has an evidence comment or equivalent durable record.
- Unit 20 does not start until this verification bead is complete.

Test commands:
- `tmp=$(mktemp -d); cd "$tmp"; <reproduce the issue #16 arrow workflow line>` and inspect created paths.
- `rg -n -- '-> ' . -g '!docs/demos/generated/*'`
- `go test ./...`
close_reason: Verified issue #16 after land-19. Scratch shell reproduction of 'finish work -> verify -> close bead -> sync beads -> stage payload + bead state -> Burpvalve -> commit' returned rc=127 and created zero-byte files Burpvalve, close, commit, stage, sync, verify. Post-sanitization rg sweep found only allowed symlink notation, fenced text diagrams, or historical decision/plan/log references; go test ./... passed. Evidence posted to GitHub issue #16: https://github.com/clicksopendoors/burpvalve/issues/16#issuecomment-4864625374
